action_description: Identifizierung der unterschiedlichen Dokumententypen und Abgleich der gesetzlichen Vorgaben mit denen der Konzernweisung 1026. Erstellung einer lokalen Archivierungsrichtlinie, welche im Einklang mit den Vorgaben der Konzernweisung 1026 steht. Kommunikation der erstellten lokalen Archivierungsrichtlinie an alle relevanten Mitarbeitende.

Identifizierung aller Mitarbeitenden für die seit der einmaligen Implementierungsaktion im Jahr 2013 keine Annahmedokumentation des Verhaltenskodex für Mitarbeitende existiert. Festlegung und Umsetzung der weiteren Vorgehensweise welche sicherstellt, dass die identifizierten Mitarbeitenden sowie für Neueinstellungen eine entsprechende Annahmedokumentation vorhanden ist.

Vollständige Bearbeitung des Compliance-Fragenkatalogs (Anhang 1 der Konzernweisung 1061) inklusive der Dokumentation von relevanten Rechercheergebnissen. Einholung der unterzeichneten Compliance Vereinbarung.

Abstimmung einer Implementierungsstrategie (z. B. bis zu welchem Jahreshandelsvolumen der GF Kodex für Geschäftspartner einzuholen ist) in Zusammenarbeit mit dem zentralen Einkauf der Division. Erarbeitung des Status-quo / Erstellen einer Übersicht für welche Geschäftspartner der Kodex bereits eingeholt wurde bzw. in unterzeichneter Form vorliegt. Einholung unterzeichneter Geschäftspartner Kodizes. Erarbeiten von Lösungsansätzen in Zusammenarbeit mit der Nachhaltigkeitsabteilung des Konzerns im Falle von Kooperationshindernissen seitens der Geschäftspartner.

Anpassung der Berechnungen der Sozialkennzahlen gemäss den Vorgaben der Nachhaltigkeitsberichterstattung. Sicherstellen, dass dieselben Kennzahlen (wie z. B. FTE) in allen Berichtswesen identisch berichtet werden.

Sicherstellung, dass die Fakturierung erst zum Zeitpunkt des tatsächlichen Gefahrenübergangs erfolgt. Systematische Überprüfung wesentlicher Umsatzvorgänge vor den Bilanzstichtagen (30. Juni und 31. Dezember) hinsichtlich der zeitlich korrekten buchhalterischen Erfassung.

Prüfung, ob weitere Finanzierungsleasingsachverhalte vorliegen. Entscheidung, ob identifizierte Finanzierungsleasingsachverhalte retrospektiv angepasst werden sollen. Prüfung unter Einhaltung des Vier-Augen-Prinzips (z. B. in Abstimmung mit dem Divisionscontrolling) aller zukünftiger Leasinggeschäfte ob die einschlägigen buchhalterischen Kriterien von operativem Leasing kumulativ erfüllt sind (z. B. anhand des vom Konzerncontrolling verfügbaren Templates).

Einführung geeigneter Massnahmen zur zeitnahen Identifizierung von betriebsbereiten Anlagegütern (z. B. monatliche Produktionsrundgänge zusammen mit Verantwortlichen der Anlagenbuchhaltung). Dokumentation der Entscheidungsfindung der gewählten Nutzungsdauern (z. B. durch Referenzierung zur entsprechenden Kategorie im Nutzungsdauerkatalog der Divisionsweisung 41).

Ausweitung der präventiven, systemgestützten Vier-Augen-Prinzip Kontrolle im HR SAP-Modul auf Einmalbezüge der Lohn- und Gehaltsdaten, sodass die Mitarbeitenden der Personalabteilung technisch nicht mehr in der Lage sind Einmalbezüge der eigenen Lohn- und Gehaltsdaten zu verändern.

Korrektur (inklusive Rückabwicklung) der inkorrekt berechneten Lohn-/ Gehaltsbestandteile. Einführung effektiver Vier-Augen-Prinzip Kontrollmechanismen zur präventiven Identifizierung von Unstimmigkeiten bei Änderungen von Lohn-/ Gehaltsbestandteilen.
Sicherstellung, dass die im Rahmen der jährlichen Stichtagsinventur ermittelten bewerteten Bestandsdifferenzen gemäss den Vorgaben der DoA genehmigt werden.
Überarbeiten des Gutschriftenfreigabeprozesses unter Berücksichtigung der Vorgaben der Kompetenzregelung. Kommunikation des überarbeiteten Gutschriftenprozesses an alle betroffenen Mitarbeitenden.
Perform and document the assessment of the agent "Exbrayat" according to Annex 1 of Corporate Instruction 1061.

Prepare an overview which business partners need to sign the Code for Business Partners according to Corporate Instruction 1056 and which ones have already signed the code. For those business partners that have not signed the Code for Business Partner, ensure that signed versions are collected. For case where business partners refuse to sign the GF Code for Business Partners consult Corporate Sustainability department for next steps.

In cooperation with the GFMS SAP Team investigate the reasons for the differences in the SAP transaction used for the Cognos reporting (S_ALR_87012284) and resolve the uncovered issues accordingly. Once solved, consequently use the SAP standard transaction for Cognos reporting (S_ALR_87012284) for reporting purposes.
Update the commercial register by removing the former Managing Director.

Develop a template for the assessment and valuation of second hand machines traded in. Consult the responsible BU Controller to identify whether other sales companies from the Business Unit have created such a template already.
Update the customer list on "Creditsafe" online portal and ensure completeness. Define and implement a regular process ensuring that the data in the "Creditsafe" portal is updated.
Review the list of suppliers. For the suppliers no longer used set a posting block in SAP FI and a purchasing block in SAP MM. Deactivate the "Divers" accounts.
Implement the blind count methodology for the annual stock taking at Rhenus. Supervise the stock taking with GFMS personnel.

Develop a comprehensive cost calculation and controlling template for automation projects. The template should include a comparison of actual vs. budget values. Roll-out and apply the template for all relevant automation projects.

Re-define the current process. Ensure that Managing Director approves and releases (second signatory) the salary payments and also receives all the necessary information to reconcile the posting in SAP and the bank payment issued.
Add the Managing Director to the ADP portal as second user. If this is not possible, define a secure back-up procedure together with the external payroll service provider ADP.
Develop a comprehensive skills matrix. Identify gaps for existing personnel and derive corresponding training necessities. Complete trainings accordingly.
Adapt variant '194-CYCLE-2003' of the SAP closing transactions KSU5 and KEU5 to the current business structure.

In cooperation with the GFMS SAP-Team clean up the open service orders and tickets. Follow up on pending quotations and close respective service orders timely in the system in case the customer is not interested in the service.

Installation einer Workstation zur Erfassung von Materialbezügen und zum Druck der Etiketten zur Teilebeschriftung im Ersatzteilelager für Montageteile. Beschriftung der Lagerorte und der Ersatzteile in allen Lagern. Die Ersatzteile mit einem regelmässigen Verbrauch (mindestens eine Warenbewegung pro Jahr) sollten ein Mal jährlich gezählt werden. Alle anderen Artikel sollten mindestens alle drei Jahre einer vollständigen Bestandsaufnahme unterzogen werden.

Anpassung der Rechte der HR-Leitung in SAP HR, so dass technisch eine Anpassung von z. B. Salärdaten nicht mehr möglich ist. Prüfung zur Einführung und Implementierung einer präventiven, systemgestützten Vier-Augen Kontrolle (Workflow-Management) bei allen Anpassungen in den Personalstammdaten.

Monatliche Rundgänge in der Produktion durch die Controlling Abteilung in Verbindung mit einem Jour fixe mit den Kostenstellenverantwortlichen bzw. Projektleitern (unterjährig und stichtagsbezogen für Halb- / und Jahresabschluss). Rückwirkende Aktivierung des Wirtschaftsgutes nach Eingang der Schlussrechnung.

Überprüfung des Distributors "Armaturjonsson A.S." gemäss Anlage 1 der Konzernweisung 1061. 
Einholung der Compliance Vereinbarung für die Distributoren "Duncan" und "IMI". Falls diese nicht bereit sind, die Vereinbarung zu unterschreiben, jedoch über einen eigenen Verhaltenskodex verfügen, ist zu prüfen, ob dieser mit dem GF Verhaltenskodex im Wesentlichen übereinstimmt. Falls die Distributoren nicht bereit sind, die Vereinbarung zu unterschreiben und auch über keinen mit dem von GF vergleichbaren Verhaltenskodex verfügen, ist der Head BU Building Technology entsprechend zu informieren und gegebenenfalls Corporate Compliance zu involvieren.

Schulung zur Durchführung von Inventuren des in Neuburg ansässigen Lagerpersonals. 
Beschränkung der Buchungsrechte in SAP für das Personal in Neuburg. Alle Inventurdifferenzen müssen durch das lokale Management Team in SAP in Sissach überprüft und - unter Einhaltung der Vorgaben der lokalen Kompetenzregelung - freigegeben und gebucht werden. Im Falle einer Zähldifferenz muss eine Zweitzählung lt. Inventuranweisung angestossen werden.
Definition einer lokalen Richtlinie zur Aufbewahrung von geschäftsrelevanten Unterlagen unter Einhaltung der Vorgaben aus der Konzernweisung 1026.
Implement a regular review of sales recorded on the OTC account to ensure transparency and prevent sales transactions not in line with the company's working instruction.
Implement a formal / documented approval process ensuring authorization and completeness of the manual journal entries before they are posted in SAP.

Ensure compliance with the requirements from the local DoA. Consider to analyze the volume of FoC deliveries on a regular basis (i.e. quarterly). If deemed appropriate, implement a lower threshold to partially delegate the approval responsibilities to the External Sales (preparer) and Internal Sales (approver) departments.
Create an overview of all existing contracts that includes the contracting party, purpose, amount, date of inception and expiration / renewal and major contractual components that require awareness.

Develop and communicate a dedicated working instruction for the stock taking procedures including the definition of roles and responsibilities in line with local DoA requirements (four-eyes principle), the blind stock taking methodology, timing and results handling. Deliverables to the Financial Controller should include the properly reviewed and signed-off inventory results, counting sheets with evidence that the inventory discrepancies are double-checked with a sec-ond count as required by the Division Instruction 3437 - Physical Inventory / Inventory Taking. Ensure compliance with local DoA requirements for cases of identified inventory discrepancies prior to their recording in SAP.

Avoid or at least reduce settlement of commissions in cash and ensure that payments are made in the country were the service is rendered. Consider to perform an assessment / evaluation in collaboration with Corporate Compliance of the regulation for cash payments in countries where the intermediaries operate, to anticipate and monitor potential legal and compliance risks.

Develop and implement an annual stock taking process before year-end to ensure the goods recorded in the corresponding SAP warehouse locations are counted entirely. For cases of identified discrepancies, ensure compliance with the requirements from the DoA.

Consider to implement a provision calculation in line with Division Instruction 3440 (Inventory on stock value adjustment) to ensure that the total inventory balance of GFPS International is evaluated consistently.

Consider to re-assess the situation of different types of intermediaries that have business relationships with GF and whether they all need to be subject to Corporate Instruction 1061 in the current form. Assess and evaluate if the different kinds of intermediaries could be subject to different requirements to ensure that they are actually met. Reflect if the tasks of a 2nd line of defense function such as Corporate Compliance can be fully met with the current setup and requirements.

Corporate Compliance shall take action to gain an overview of the overall implementation status of CI 1061. Implement and mandate a streamlined monitoring and tracking mechanism including a standard tracking template that all GF entities are required to use. This template should be feasible to track distributors and agents alike and have the feature to allow comprehensive tracking of any upcoming re-assessments due.

Review the official but outdated versions of the GF Compliance Agreement in the exisiting languages. Amend these language versions to ensure they are in accordance with the latest and official GF Compliance Agreement. Ensure that all language versions are published with a proper and easy access.

Adapt sections 2.5 and 2.7 of the assessment to make fulfilments practical for GF subsidiaries. Investigate available software solutions that can support the intermediary assessment in areas as mentioned in the finding.

An identifier in the ERP or CRM system should be set up to distinguish an intermediary account from other types of account. For example, a distributor account should have an identifier that distinguishes it from end customer accounts. An agent account should have an identifier that distinguishes it from other supplier accounts. This identifier should be used when compiling the complete overview of intermediaries.
The account creation process should consider whether the account is for an intermediary. If yes, the duly completed and signed Annex 1 assessment should be made as a mandatory documentation before an account can be created.

Ensure Annex 1 assessment is completed and approved for all intermediaries using the latest version of the assessment questionnaire. 
Any confusion or exception regarding CI 1061 should be addressed to the Corporate Compliance Officer before implementing any alternative approach.
Archive all relevant documentation used in conducting the checks required under the Annex 1 assessment.

Ensure all Annex 1 assessments are completed electronically using the given Excel template. Information entered must be update to date and specific to each intermediary being assessed. No section should be left unanswered.
Ensure approvals are sought for and obtained from the required management members in a timely manner before commencing or continuing business with concerned intermediaries.
Request relevant intermediaries to sign the Compliance Agreement or provide a copy of their own CoC that is equivalent to the GF CoC.

Ensure all intermediary relationships are governed by the written contracts fitting the requirements of the Corporate Legal Department. In case written contracts are not considered feasible, an approval from Corporate Legal should be obtained.

Reach a consensus with the Strategic Procurement team on the two criteria defined or whether a modification (e.g. increasing the threshold of T CHF 2) is needed. In case of any inevitable cases exceeding the threshold without vendor creation, proper approval should be obtained and documented.
Implement a quarterly review control on the transactions of the one-time vendor account to ensure internal usage compliance.

Clarify and reconcile the existing backlog. Check with Finance on any potential need for additional specific provision for materials that can no longer be traced (in case such materials are not yet provided for under the standard Range of Coverage calculation).
Ensure all materials in the quarantine area have the corresponding red tags specifying the required details. Consider to establish FIFO and/or visual management in the quarantine storage area going forward.
Include supplier consignment stocks in the annual stock counting process.

The employees responsible for verifying and approving logistics service invoices should be provided with the latest applicable price lists from each service provider. Implement a systematic verification process of logistics service invoices where invoiced charges are compared with agreed price lists.

Define a time schedule and assign responsible employees for the implementation of 5S in the Additive Manufacturing and Electronics Production Departments. Implementation progress should be tracked and reported in the monthly management meetings.
Ensure OMS contains up-to-date activity durations for all assembly operations in all departments.

Analyze machine capacity and load factor in a documented and systematic way in order to better synchronize and optimize the collective machine capacity of the whole production line.
Define production KPI (e.g. OEE, OLE, etc.) based on the equipment and labor data and track the KPI quarterly to identify potential improvement areas or solve production bottlenecks.

Enhance spare parts management in Electronics Production by defining critical spare parts based on cost, lead-time and significance on production. Timely record each receipt and consumption to ensure control on safety stock level and clarify responsibilities of proper physical custody.
Introduce the maintenance tool MIG to all departments for maintenance and spare parts management to centralize and standardize the activities.

Extract and analyze critical internal non-conformities identified during the quality tests on a regular basis. Based on the analysis, define action plans and responsibilities on the recurring and controllable cases to ensure timely implementation and follow-up.

Ensure non-conformity on received materials is analyzed timely. The root cause should be systematically communicated to Finance and Logistics Departments in order for appropriate actions to be taken. For example, block payment of the corresponding invoice and/or prevent further POs for the same material to be issued in case of non-conformities affecting the entire batch of material.

Conduct a clean-up exercise on aged cases to identify and follow up the QNs still requiring further actions and close the ones that are actually resolved.
Define a clear rule on prioritizing QNs and set up a targeted time to close the notifications.
Conduct a monthly review on open QNs aged above six months to ensure timely follow up actions.

We recommend that GFMS Divisional HR issue a guideline to emphasize on the requirement to ensure the two-level approval principle for salary increases of all employees including direct reports of the MD. Local companies should adhere to this principle.

Consider assigning the tasks for completing both accident related forms in SIS to the local Environmental Managers instead of splitting the tasks with the HR SSC. Ensure that the statistical reporting reconciles with the detailed reporting of accidents. The Data Validation Dashboard in SIS can be used for this reconciliation.

Ausarbeitung einer neuen lokalen Kompetenzregelung basierend auf den Vorgaben der divisionsweiten Kompetenzregelung. Kommunikation und Implementierung der neuen lokalen Kompetenzregelung an alle relevanten Mitarbeitende.

Erstellung einer Übersicht aller aktuell gültigen Verträge welche die wesentlichen Vertragsverpflichtungen beinhaltet. Definition eines Verantwortlichen ("Contract Owner") für das Vertragsmanagement gemäss Divisionsweisung 30.

Identifizierung der unterschiedlichen Dokumententypen und Abgleich der gesetzlichen Vorgaben mit denen der Konzernweisung 1026. Erstellung einer lokalen Archivierungsrichtlinie, welche im Einklang mit den Vorgaben der Konzernweisung 1026 steht. Kommunikation der erstellten lokalen Archivierungsrichtlinie an alle relevanten Mitarbeitende.

Abstimmung einer Implementierungsstrategie (z.B. bis zu welchem Jahreshandelsvolumen der GF Kodex für Geschäftspartner einzuholen ist) in Zusammenarbeit mit dem zentralen Einkauf der Division. Erarbeitung des Status-quo / Erstellen einer Übersicht für welche Geschäftspartner der Kodex bereits eingeholt wurde bzw. in unterzeichneter Form vorliegt. Einholung unterzeichneter Geschäftspartner Kodizes. Erarbeiten von Lösungsansätzen in Zusammenarbeit mit der Nachhaltigkeitsabteilung des Konzerns im Falle von Kooperationshindernissen seitens der Geschäftspartner.
Wiederkehrende Überprüfung aller vorhandener Erste Hilfe Materialien auf deren Ablaufdatum. Austausch oder Ersatz abgelaufener Erster Hilfe Materialien.
Erarbeitung eines Austauschplans unter Berücksichtigung von Kosten, Nutzen und Risiken. Austausch der veralteten Hardware gemäss des Austauschplans.

Korrektur der Berechnung der Kundenkreditlimits im Berechnungstemplate. Zeitnahe Weiterleitung der aktuellen und freigegebenen Kreditlimitberechnungen an die Verantwortlichen in der Debitorebuchhaltung im SSC Herzogenburg zur dortigen Einpflege in SAP.

Entscheidungsfindung in Zusammenarbeit mit dem Business Unit- und Divisionscontrolling hinsichtlich der retrospektiven Korrektur des identifizierten Sachverhalts. Anwendung der aktuellen Version des Nutzungsdauerkatalogs der Divisionsweisung 41 (Beilage 2).

Einmalige Abstimmung aller neu verhandelter bzw. geänderter Einkaufspreisänderungen mit den Artikelstammdaten (Infoblatt) in SAP. Sicherstellung der zeitnahen, parallelen Aktualisierung von Einkaufspreisverhandlungsergebnissen sowohl in SAP als auch in der Exceldatei der realisierten Einsparungen.

Risiko- und Wirtschaftlichkeitsanalyse in Abstimmung mit der Konzernrechtsabteilung sowie dem zentralen Einkauf der Division inwiefern eine Notwendigkeit zur konsequenten Einholung von Auftragsbestätigungen für Bestellungen unter EUR 3'500 bei Lieferanten ohne Rahmenvertrag gegeben ist.
Rechnungserstellung unter Berücksichtigung der Wiegescheine der GFCS Altenmarkt. Abweichungsanalysen und / oder Verplausibilisierungen der von der Aigner GmbH zur Verfügung gestellten Informationen.
Neuordnung und klare Zuweisung fester Lagerplätze aller Ersatzteile unter Einhaltung gängiger Standards (5S).
Zeitnahe Durchführung der Rekalibierungen / Zertifizierungen von derzeit überfälligen prüfungsrelevanten Messinstrumenten.

Make sure, the local policy "9. Ablage von Dokumenten und Daten" is in line with Corporate Instruction No. 1026: "Records Retention". Amend the local policy with a table indicating the retention periods by type of record.
Revoke the direct eBanking authorities at Credit Suisse, or make at least sure that the access is limited to read only authorities.
Segregate the conflicting activities to different staff such as assigning an independent person to perform the regular cycle count, etc.
Consult SAP team for a solution in extracting slow-moving provisions on the entity level and book corresponding monthly provisions in a holistic manner.
Book the refund and the disbursements of the IIT withholding fee in HBII income statements.
Considering cost and efficiency factors, monitor and record the actual time spent on each takt based on a defined measurement unit.
Set a threshold for time gaps between sequential takts as a trigger for further analysis and pos-sible restructure. Make takt references to the OMS files and split up cross-takt OMS.
Consult the SAP team to correct the set-up of the specific Sloc or material master data.

Define clear roles and responsibilities of the contract overview among departments. Build up an effective communication process such as using a common shared folder to ensure timely and complete update of the contract tracking list.
Correct the classification of the High-Speed Machines on the dashboard and include the machines into the monthly 6MIS reporting and analysis.

Implement a formal and documented approach in postponing or advancing delivery dates and record the OTD discrepancies in a complete and traceable manner. 
Discuss internally to define a more precise KPI measuring the realibility and performance of the manual IMPC planning and delivery activities.

Consult Corporate Legal to reach a formal subcontracting agreement between GFMS CAC and the subcontractor (and if necessary, also the material supplier) to better protect GF interests under this special business model.

Monitor both the payable overtime and the compensatory leaves as a whole to reflect a com-plete overview of employee overtime at the entity level. 
Analyze the root causes of excessive overtime and implement actions to balance the working load among employees.

An analysis (i.e. based on the operating vs. finance lease template from Corporate Controlling) as to whether the contracts would qualify for finance lease according to Swiss GAAP FER should be performed thoroughly. 
Convert the booking of the operating leases to finance leases.

Implement a process to ensure that whenever a medical certificate indicating professional accident was received by HR, the site Sustainability Representative is informed to investigate and report the case in SIS.

Ensure the various operational teams eliminate the safety hazards identified without delay. All issues identified in the future should be treated with high attention and immediate corrective actions. Non-action should be subject to disciplinary consequences.
Implement a regular monitoring and follow up process on open GR/IR items aged over 3 months. Ensure the results of the follow up action is documented.

Ensure accuracy of information recorded in the Excel file used to monitor and control customer credit limits. Further deliveries should not be made to customers with exceeded credit limits unless approved by COFACE or a member of management with the correct level of delegated authority. Consider again to implement the credit control process in SAP.
Ensure to capitalize and depreciate fixed assets and assets und construction according to the Finance Manual in a timely manner.
In conjunction with the Managing Director, communicate and re-emphasize the purchase order process requirements as well as the consequences of non-compliance.

Specify a due date and the responsible contact person for the implementation of the required measures in the supplier audit report. Actively follow up on the open issues not yet resolved and document the follow up results. Communicate the consequences of not implementing the defined measures to the suppliers.
Ensure specific written price agreements are negotiated and signed for all cost types. Such written agreements should be provided to the employee conducting the invoice verification.

Separate conforming materials and quarantined materials visually and physically. Ensure the SAP location of material matches its actual physical location and only quarantined materials are stored in the quarantine room. For oversized quarantined materials, clearly mark them visually when stored outside the quarantine room.
Implement visual management in the measurement lab in order to ensure materials with tem-perature stabilization requirements are only measured after such requirements are met.
Implement a workflow approval process for stock adjustments in accordance with the requirements defined in the DoA.

Remove the SAP authorization to post goods receipt from the employees in Operational Pur-chasing who do not perform goods receipt for service related POs. Establish a regular monitor-ing process to ensure the remaining employee with the authorization only posts goods receipt for service related POs.
Remove the SAP authorization to make changes to the valid supplier from the employees in Operational Purchasing.

Investigate the cases identified. Resolve the cases in an appropriate matter, e.g. by invoicing the customer for the consignment stock used. Improve the current tracking process to ensure timely and complete follow up of all outstanding consignment stock. Formal consignment contract should be used to regulate such business setup.

Implement cross-department 5S audits. Address observations in the weekly shopfloor meeting and define responsible employees for corrective actions. Non-action by the responsible employee should be addressed specifically and immediately to ensure the employee understands that 5S is mandatory and not optional.
Draft and sign extended workbench agreements with the 4 service providers in consultation with Strategic Procurement and Corporate Legal.
Dedicate resources from the industrialization and technical teams to create and maintain an accurate and up-to-date set of OMS. Ensure assembly operators understand and follow the OMS consistently.
Comply with the preventive maintenance plan for all production machinery. Ensure proper staff planning in the maintenance department to avoid shortages.

Conduct a clean-up exercise on aged quality notifications and close cases that are no longer active. Ensure active follow up on pending open cases and document status updates in the corresponding notification in SAP.

Insist on supplier driven quality assurance procedures where incoming materials must be accompanied by certificated test and measurement results in order to reduce the number of materials requiring an incoming quality inspection by GFMS. Adapt the existing process to ensure a timely incoming inspection on the remaining materials prior to storing them in the warehouse.

Define standardized quality control plans including exact measurements or tests to be taken manually as well as the required sample size for each incoming material requiring inspection. Such definition should be done in conjunction with the competent employees in the industrialization and/or R&D teams. Ensure details and results of the quality inspections are sufficiently documented and traceable.

Ensure complete and correct registration of all production tools in Primas in cooperation with the Quality Department. Recalibrate all applicable production tools timely. 
Require operators on the production floor to check and only use calibrated tools.

Update the employment contract templates in consultation with Corporate Legal. Seek advice from Corporate Legal on how to handle existing contracts signed containing this outdated wording. It is recommendable to have an explicit written confirmation from the employee that he or she accepts the GF Code of Conduct.
Update the ICS description to reflect the new practice.
Ensure analyses of individual cases are documented with clear action plans. Follow up should be made based on these action plans.
Finalize and implement the exit checklists to ensure a streamlined and documented exit process.

Implement a consistent operational procedure in bonus calculation across GFMS HR Switzerland. Document the approach in detail in order to ensure knowledge transfer in case of any potential employee absence or turnover. Ensure written approvals are kept on file for all bonus payments.
Ensure system enforced 4-eyes-principle by implementing a mandatory approval workflow for all changes to sensitive payroll relevant information in SAP.

Payroll payment file upload for all GFMS companies in Switzerland should be made by the HR SSC following a consistent approach via Bellin. Direct upload of payroll file to E-Banking platforms should be avoided. Remove the direct access to UBS E-Banking by the HR BP since it would be no longer needed. Ensure confidentiality on individual salary information.
Review the entire local records retention guideline and align it with the Corporate Instruction 1026 while ensuring compliance with the statutory law and fulfilling business needs.

Consult the Corporate Compliance Officer for the appropriateness of giving the identified gifts and entertainments to the concerned recipients, and obtain proper approval from the Head of Division. The table stated in the CI 1061 is highly generic and does not outline all situations with which the management and employees may be confronted, in case of doubts going for-ward, the Corporate Legal Department should be contacted to give instructions that are more pragmatic on specific cases.

Consult Corporate Legal for advice or risk mitigation measures in case of new contract templates, orders or contracts provided by the business partners and without standard GF terms. Internal Audit understands that currently there is only one local FTE in the Corporate Legal department shared by three divisions in China, and in order to meet customers' requirements timely, the local in-house legal resources might not be sufficient for the region of responsibility. Thus for critical contracts in relatively higher value or frequency (e.g. the annual service agreement with HRHY, orders with same terms and conditions), or agreements with special liability clauses (e.g. the confidentiality contracts), corrective measures after legal review could also be taken to mitigate risks if preventive control was not possible before business commencement.
Consult SAP Specialists for configuring the automatic approval workflow in SAP (PO Release Strategy) to ensure a systematic and efficient authorization process.

Global Authorization Administration together with the local LAA (Local Authorization Administrator), understand the business needs for SAP accesses and check for violations of segregation of duties across the key processes for the existing SAP users. Regarding the existing SAP users, discuss how to establish proper segregation of duties by amending the access privileges of users (e.g. splitting the conflicting roles across the different entities), especially for the critical and high role combinations. In terms of new SAP user creation or position changes, request training or suggestions from the GFPS Global SAP team for proper access authorization.

Consult SAP support team to help launch the automatic vendor assessment method regarding the pricing, delivery date performance, and delivery quantity performance. Follow the manual guideline regarding quality and service criteria to ensure alignment with the GFPS directive.
Deliver training to the concerned Maintenance Operators regarding practical instructions and maintenance schedules. Update the template and checklist to align with the Maintenance planning.
Either configure the safety stock levels of critical spare parts in the system (ECOUNT) or manually monitor the stock levels of critical spare parts via the offline-tracking list.

Define clear roles and responsibilities in creating, following up, and closing quality inspections and notifications in SAP. Implement a regular (e.g. monthly or quarterly) review control to correct master data configuration errors in SAP.

Correct the classification methodology of banker's drafts received as prepayments at each month-end in Cognos for HBII reporting. Besides, For the identified commission case and similar scenario going forward, where the distributor also acts as a vendor, book the corresponding service expenses under the correct account or adjust them manually in the COGNOS at month-end closing.
Discuss with SAP consultant to generate a standard report of price changes on sales order lev-el as a detective measure to mitigate the potential risks due to lacking systematic/preventive controls.
Review the existing distributorship contracts and correct such mismatch by modifying or adding suitable contractual clauses.

Configure the useful life of respective asset categories in SAP master data based on GF Finance Manual. If not possible to realize this one-by-one match in SAP, then based on the CAPEX application form (determining the asset category) and the GF Manual (determining the asset useful life), input the correct useful life upon asset creation in SAP.

Define roles and responsibilities for contract management at GFPS France. Develop a contract repository / overview in-cluding all key criteria from all contractual agreements currently in place with GFPS France.
Create a local Records Retention policy ensuring compliance with requirements from Corporate Instruction 1026.
Implement a regularly (i.e. annually) performed CRM master data analysis for key CRM data ele-ments (i.e. accounts, contacts, projects etc.).

Review and distribution of comprehensive payroll file to service provider by Managing Director. Assess the possibility to segregate duties for payroll preparation as well as payroll reconciliation by training of the Accountant. Implement a cell lock in Excel for salary changes for users of the comprehensive payroll file.

Submit signed external service contracts to Corporate Legal for a subsequent review. Re-negotiate amendments to the contract(s) in case of any violation of European General Data Pro-tection Regulations (GDPR) and / or other amendment requirements with the external provider.

Reconcile the local Delegation of Authorities (DoA) with the DoAs of the Business Unit and the Division. Ensure that the local DoA includes all relevant business transactions and that authorization levels are in line with superordinate DoAs.
Review the definition and setup of the user access rights in the SAGE ERP system and adapt them where necessary in order to avoid cumulated access rights hampering an effective segregation of duties.

Implement a more robust solution for the processing of the XML upload file for the salary payments, e.g. by authorizing the external payroll provider (Laboreda) to upload the XML file directly to the ebanking of GFMS Spain at Commerzbank.

Re-assess the currently identified risks for remaining existence. Define at least one action item with a due date and a person responsible for all risks identified and report those details accordingly in the respective risk reporting sections.

Define roles and responsibilities for contract management at GFPS Germany (e.g. by establishing a "Contract Owner") taking requirements from GF Corporate Instruction 1042 into account. Update the existing contract overview file with all missing contracts and their critical details.

Ensure acceptance and sign-off of GF's Code for Business Partners for all key suppliers / business partners. For cases of push back from suppliers / business partners, consult with Business Unit, Division and / or Corporate Sustainability to coordinate next steps. Put in place a contract with "Voigt-Armaturenfabrik."

Clarify with Business Unit / Division Controlling the classification and reporting of such "goodwill" warranty cases in Cognos. Consider to develop a discounting methodology / factor for cases with no year over year changes and apply this methodology / factor accordingly to the provision calculation.
Implement an approval workflow in SAP for purchase order release according to the latest DoA.
Remind employees of current DoA approval thresholds and levels concerning FOC deliveries. Ensure that requirements from DoA concerning FOC deliveries are complied with.
Remind employees of current DoA approval thresholds and levels concerning credit notes. Ensure that requirements from DoA concerning credit notes are complied with.

Ensure completeness of personnel files by creating job descriptions defining the tasks and responsibilities of all employees (including Managing Director). Define a signed copy of the job description as a requirement in the personnel files.

Inform the Division President about the business practice of golf events for customers and request the necessary written approval. Omit the invitation of customers to any similar customer entertainment events as long as the approval is not granted. Discuss with Corporate Compliance whether such an approval can be granted in general, as long as these events do not exceed local customs and agree upon these local customs.

Distribute GF's Code for Business Partners to identified key suppliers. Inform relevant key sup-pliers about the requirement of acceptance by signature of GF's Code for Business Partners. For cases of push back consult with GFMS Strategic Procurement and Corporate Sustainability for next steps.

Implement regular safety patrols conducted by departmental heads and the Health and Safety Manager to check the occupational safety on the company premises and compliance with GF's Health and Safety Standards.
Transfer the saw machine to a more secure workspace. While operating the machine, ensure that safety goggles and protective gloves are used. 
Recurring review of all existing first aid materials for expiration date. Replacement or substitution of expired first aid materials.
Ensure the correct transfer of actual cost updates to the standard costs on a quarterly basis. Investigate if other machine types are also affected by the exposed risk.

Perform a one-time reconciliation of all newly negotiated or changed purchase prices with the
material master data in SAP. Ensure that material prices in SAP are changed as soon as they be-come valid to actually benefit from purchase price negotiations.
Consult with Corporate Legal to determine to which extent the consistent collection of order confirmations outside of a contractual framework is reasonable.
Continuously monitor the delivery status of all open purchase orders in SAP with transaction MEL2L. Verify whether the delivery times maintained in the material master data are accurate and correct.

Implementation of a systematic selection process in the annual audit planning for supplier audits. Incorporate additional factors into the supplier assessment that describe the timeliness of a sup-plier's delivery (e.g. OTIF).
Ensure the timely conduction of planned supplier audits. If a postponement was necessary and / or results of the re-assessment in the following year did not change, carry forward not conducted audits to the next year's audit plan.
Separate incoming and outgoing goods and materials visually and physically. Ensure the implementation of a dedicated area for shipping and receiving goods and materials in the warehouse.

Define a dedicated annual inventory count procedure with proper segregation of duties and doc-ument approvals of the difference list properly. Define a threshold above which reasons for iden-tified differences require follow-up action items.
Remove the SAP access rights from the Material and Procurement Manager to validate inventory stock adjustments. Ensure the proper approval / validation by signature from the Head of SCM and local Financial Controller.

Implement a monthly report distribution of the actual working hours and flextime balances of the employees to the line managers in charge. Define mitigating actions with the respective employ-ees to reduce / increase hours to a reasonable level on their flextime account.

Use the defined comprehensive checklist provided by the HR SSC to ensure a systematic exit procedure and proper documentation a soon as available. Define the exit checklist as a mandato-ry element of the personnel file.

Define an overall responsible (i.e. "contract owner" according to Division Directive 30). Develop an overview of all existing contracts and agreements that includes all key contractual terms (i.e. the contracting party, purpose, volume, date of inception and expiration / renewal clauses) that require awareness.

Prepare an overview which business partners are registered in "Astras", have signed GF's Code for Business Partners, and which are not registered and need to sign GF's Code for Business Partners according to Corporate Instruction 1056. For those business partners that have not signed GF's Code for Business Partners, ensure that signed versions are collected or docu-mented in "Astras". For cases where business partners refuse to sign GF's Code for Business Partners consult the Corporate Sustainability department for next steps.
Establish processes to reconcile and review the values entered in SIS. Ensure the audit trail from the reported KPIs to supporting evidences is in place and reconciles.

Implement a follow-up reporting and tracking of identified corrective measures resulting from cross-site safety audit observations. Share the results of the follow-up reporting / tracking with appropriate levels of Management (i.e. DEM, corrective measure owner(s), local Management team(s)) regularly.

Improve the understanding of key applicable incoterms (i.e. DAP) by conducting a refresher training with all people involved in the revenue recognition analysis procedures during cut-off dates. Ensure that revenue is recognized in the correct accounting period and based on the agreed upon shipping terms.
Implement a regular monitoring and follow up process on open GR/IR items aged over 3 months. Ensure the results of the follow-up action are documented and releases are recorded according-ly.

Ensure the various operational teams eliminate the safety hazards identified. All issues identified in the future should be treated with high attention and immediate corrective actions. Non-action should be subject to disciplinary consequences. Develop a storage concept for spare parts that will be relocated from GFCS Werdohl.
Complete pending annual target agreement and confirmation processes. Develop minimum doc-umentation requirement for expected results in me@GF and communicate and apply those ac-cordingly in.

Extend the current raw material flow analysis for scrapped material and finished goods. Conduct this analysis regularly (i.e. monthly). Derive actions and corrective measures for identified / unexplained differences and track their implementation accordingly.
Remind employees of current DoA approval thresholds and levels concerning credit notes. Ensure that requirements from DoA concerning credit notes are complied with.
Perform a gap analysis and update the local DoA to ensure alignment with the Division DoA. Communicate the updated version of the local DoA to all involved employees.
Use the Anticorruption Questionnaire (Annex 1) to conduct due diligence on all existing and future agents / sales representatives.

For those distributors identified in the risk assessment as high risk, start the implementation of signed distributor agreements. Any pushback should be referred to Corporate Compliance / Legal for further instructions.
Implement GFPS' compliance screening tool "Compliance Xpress" or implement compensating manual compliance screening procedures satisfying the requirements from CI 1046.

Ensure acceptance and sign-off of GF's Code for Business Partners for all non-material key sup-pliers / business partners. For cases of push back from suppliers / business partners, consult with Business Unit, Division and / or Corporate Sustainability to coordinate next steps.
Implement mutually signed rental agreements for each machine rental. Ensure to document potentially existing payment or rental fee schemes in these agreements.

Clean up identified data incompleteness and insufficiency (either deactivate, delete or update). Implement a regularly (i.e. quarterly) performed CRM master data analysis for accounts, contacts and projects information based on Excel extracted information.

Limit employees' access rights in iScala to their actual function. For employees fulfilling duties across functions identify segregation of duties conflicts in their access rights. Verify if functions can be allocated differently to facilitate proper segregation of duties or consider the implementation of compensating controls.
Amend the concept of the local records retention with a list indicating the filing location and the retention period by type of document.

In cooperation with GFMS Strategic Procurement identify the business partners required to sign the Code for Business Partners. Collect signed versions of the Code for Business Partners for identified business partners. In case of any push back consult with the GFMS Strategic Procurement team.

Have all contracts with intermediaries reviewed by Corporate Legal. In cooperation with Corporate Legal decide on the further proceeding. Where necessary, ask the respective intermediaries to sign a new contract complying with the standards of Corporate Legal.

Instruct the Sales Engineers to use the Quote Manager from the beginning of the sales process, i.e. for the initial configuration and calculation of machines to be offered to the customers. Collect required approval before any quotation is issued to the customer. Keep the history of the approved cost sheets.

Conclude a contract with UIC for the payroll services to be provided by UIC. The contract should include the definition of a single point of contact at GFMS Singapore and the regulation that the Financial Controller of GFMS Singapore has to be copied into all emails sent to GFMS Singapore by UIC.
Conclude service agreements on the basis of the templates provided by Corporate Legal with the service providers of GFMS Singapore.
Investigate the reasons for the long response time of the Customer Services hotline and take measures to decrease the call-back time to a competitive level.
Improve the skills matrix of the Field Service Engineers showing the skill level by employee and type of machine. Identify the skills gaps. Develop a training plan to fill the gaps.

In cooperation with IT clean up the service order database in iScala. Develop and implement a process to ensure that the data in the service database is updated timely and always reflects the real situation.
Instruct the Sales Engineers to use the Quote Manager from the beginning of the sales process, i.e. for the initial configuration and calculation of machines to be offered to the customers.
Managing Directors should cascade any updates to Corporate Instructions down to all local employees. Adaptations to local processes should also be made in order to comply with any updated CIs.
Ensure Annex 1 assessment is completed and approved for all intermediaries using the latest version of the assessment questionnaire.

Reassess all intermediaries using the latest Excel version of the Annex 1. In case of any red flags, seek approval from the BU Head as required by CI 1061 or consult the Corporate Compliance Officer if the reason for the red flags are unclear.
Archive all relevant documentation used in conducting the screening required under the Annex 1 assessment.

Ensure all intermediary relationships are governed by written contracts fitting the requirements of the Corporate Legal Department. In case written contracts are not considered feasible, an approval from Corporate Legal should be obtained.

GFMS Italy should consult Corporate Legal on the appropriateness of the contract used with its agents.
Request the remaining intermediaries to sign the Compliance Agreement or provide a copy of their own CoC that is equivalent to the GF CoC.
Cooperate with Market Region management in updating the local DoA in order to ensure alignment with the divisional DoA as well as any specific Market Region requirements.
Consult Corporate Legal and Market Region management in implementing a collective signatory power in commercial registers of the legal entities in Poland and Hungary.

Obtain retrospective approvals from Corporate Legal for the 2 ongoing legal cases. Ensure all future involvements of outside legal counsel are approved according to the requirements of CI 1070. Engagement letters should be signed using the required template (Annex 2 to CI 1070).
Consult BU Controlling for best practice and implement a closing checklist including tasks for both the Finance Department in GFMS Poland and the Accountant in the Hungarian branch office.
Consult Division Controlling and implement a process to ensure Privilege Club points are treated in accordance with the GFMS Revenue Recognition Policy.

Contact the local ERP provider to find a solution in order to calculate stock provision according to the GFMS Stock Directive. In case this is not feasible, discuss with Division Controlling to obtain an approval for the alternative calculation method.

Service Departments in both GFMS Poland and the HU branch office should work together to provide the Financial Controller with required figures for warranty provision based on calculation from actual data.
Calculate bonus accrual using the updated calculation tool and report the adjusted figure in the next reporting period.

Instruct the local Accountant employed at the HU branch office to prepare a detailed aging schedule and bad debt allowance calculation in accordance with the GF Finance Manual. Results should be communicated to the Financial Controller on a monthly basis.
Consolidate and harmonize the aging schedules of both entities to ensure completeness and accuracy of overdue balances and bad debt allowances.

Document the current detective review control of vendor bank information by downloading (e.g. creating a PDF file) the payment list entered in the E-Banking system before executing it and comparing with the respective IBAN numbers stored in the beneficiary list. Evidence of the performed review should be retained either electronically or physically.

Review the current SoD in the purchase-to-pay process to ensure the four-eyes principle is sufficiently observed. Amend the user access rights in EXACT to make sure that the posting of goods receipt in the ERP system is segregated from the purchase orders (POs) entry and confirmation.

At the minimum, implement a documented verification process for vendor invoices from third parties. This should ascertain that incoming invoices are checked against the PO value and goods receipt record booked in EXACT. Eventual differences should be investigated by the CS Department resulting in either corrections made to the PO value or goods receipt record in EXACT or an amended vendor invoice. Finance should only book vendor invoices that match the PO value and the goods receipt record in EXACT.

Harmonize the fixed assets register structure in both entities in order to facilitate a more efficient consolidation for reporting purposes. Establish a regular (e.g. monthly) CAPEX status monitoring with the local responsible Sales Manager to ensure an accurate overview of completed construction works, time of capitalization and comparison against the budgeted amount. Ensure all capitalized assets as well as assets under construction are recorded in the dedicated register.

Reclassify in COGNOS the amount of fixed assets not yet completed to asset under construction to ensure that asset additions are reflected in the appropriate accounting period. Recalculate the depreciation amount based on actual capitalization date to identify eventual differences that should have been booked in fiscal year 2020 in order to validate the accuracy of the fixed asset opening balance at January 1st, 2021 as well as the P&L result reported in COGNOS at year-end 2020.

At GFMS Poland, implement margin control on billable service interventions using available data from the 3S and ERP systems. PMC margin should be calculated based on the hourly cost rate including all applicable costs.

At the HU branch office, implement the same margin controls once sufficient data collection procedure is set up in the local systems.
Cooperate with the 3S service software provider to set up automatic recurring service requests for active PMCs.
Follow up on all spare parts missing credit notes aged more than 3 months. Ensure reasons and follow up activities are documented in the CS tracking sheet.

Review the ICS of GF Munot Re. Make sure that all relevant control risks of the core processes are identified and included in the ICS documentation. Remove the controls of processes outsourced to Georg Fischer AG or not applicable to GF Munot Re from the ICS documentation.

Update the current monitoring list of contracts in place by including all other relevant contracts and make sure that key contractual information such as contract value, period of validity, purpose and renewal clauses are complete and available at a glance.
Ensure intermediaries are assessed by using the latest version of the Anticorruption Questionnaire for Assessment of Intermediaries available in Annex 1 of CI 1061.

For every key supplier, document a dual sourcing list presenting at least one alternative supplier in accordance with the requirements of Divisional Instruction 3385. Consider also to include in the dual sourcing list any local suppliers that provide key material for the extrusion production process, to make sure that the risk of potential material shortage will be further reduced.

For every assessed supplier, put in place a monitoring list that includes score, rating, date of assessment and date of follow-up of any eventual corrective action taken, to ensure that historical ratings and evolution of supplier performance can be monitored at a glance.
Review the accuracy of the current maintenance plan of the extrusion lines and ensure that any deviations and / or exceptions from the planned interventions are explained and documented.
Define a strategy (e.g. dividend payout strategy) with the support of GF Corporate Risk Management & Tax to ensure that the equity ratio stays in the range defined by the GF Finance Manual.

Perform an annual review of the production lot sizes to ensure that the calculation of standard production cost is in line with Divisional requirements. Analyze the cost for regrinded raw material scrapped from the molding process and calculate the correspondent cost allowance to determine the adjusted standard cost. Compare the identified cost with the market value (e.g. based on sales invoices of sold scrapped material) to ensure that the regrinded material is valued as prescribed by DI 3431.

For every key supplier, document a dual sourcing list presenting at least one alternative supplier in accordance with the requirements of Divisional Instruction 3385. Consider also to include in the dual sourcing list any local suppliers that provide key material for the molding production process, to make sure that the risk of potential material shortage will be further reduced.

Based on the last performed annual supplier audits, review the correspondent audit reports and put in place a monitoring list detailing the audit results, remediation actions, due dates and actual status (e.g. resolved / open). Ensure that a regular follow-up of any eventual observation and remediation plan agreed with the suppliers is available at a glance.
Resolve all aged quality cases. Ensure that items recorded in quarantine stock will be blocked automatically so they are not available for production.

Develop a procedure to evaluate the quality of logistic providers by criteria commonly defined by the Sales Support and Supply Chain Management team. Conduct evaluations regularly (i.e. annu-ally) and document results accordingly.

Clean all existing oil stains. Implement recurring review procedures of production equipment cleanliness (i.e. through regular safety patrols). Consider to replace old machines with new machines in the mid- to long-term to lower the average age of the machine park.

Review the accuracy of the current maintenance plan for the production equipment and ensure that any deviations or exceptions from the planned maintenance activities are explained and documented. Define a reasonable threshold (i.e. 30 business days) for overdue maintenance activities and ensure that overdue maintenance activities above the threshold are executed accordingly.

Define critical spare parts, determine the respective safety stock levels in formal documentation, and make it aware to all concerned parties to ensure safety stock levels are constantly met. 
Perform periodic reviews on obsolete and damaged parts and follow up to fix or replace them.
Increase the frequency of spare parts stock take if applicable and form documented process to make sure discrepancies are timely addressed and adjusted.
定义关键备件库存备品备件，并在正式文档中确定其安全库存；告知所有相关同事，以确保其库存能够持续满足安全库存水平。对过时和损坏的零件进行定期检查，并进行后续修理或更换。
在可行的前提下，建议增加备品备件仓盘点的频率，形成书面盘点和跟进流程以确保及时盘点差异得到及时的跟进和调整。

Review the stock take procedure and make it clear that inventory differences should be calcu-lated and analyzed in absolute quantity and value. Set a tolerance rate for variance analysis and define action plans to solve the issues causing the discrepancies. Significant variance at the material level should also be reviewed and followed up according to the pre-determined tolerance rate.
重新审查库存盘点程序，在其中明确阐明应使用绝对数量或绝对价值差异来计算和分析盘点差异。 设置差异容忍度，对于超过容忍度的差异及时进行原因分析，并制定行动计划以解决引起差异的问题。物料层面的显著差异也应基于预先设定的容忍度进行检查和跟进。

Formalize the comprehensive planning methodology as defined in a documented formula, including critical elements like sales orders, safety stock levels, batch size, sales forecast, and other relevant information that help optimize the accuracy of material resource planning. Certain estimated data such as safety stock level should be defined in a traceable list based on quantified data e.g. realized turnover in the past several years, or sales forecast in the coming months, etc. For existing data source such as sales orders, retain the linkage between production and sales orders.
按照全面的排产制订方法形成一个计算公式，其中应包括关键因素如销售订单，安全库存，批次量，销售预测以及其他有助于优化物料需求准确性的信息。针对某些估计数据（如安全库存量）应将可量化的数据（例如过去几年的已实现营业额或之后月份的销售预测等）以一种可追溯的方式来进行定义。对于现有的数据源如销售订单，要留存生产订单和销售订单之间的关联信息。

Document action plans, responsible individuals, and deadlines for operational KPIs, which could be optimized in the monthly meeting memo. Follow up the action plans in the subsequent monthly meeting and track the implementation status.
针对那些可优化的运营关键指标，在月度会议纪要中记录相应的改进计划，负责人员和执行时间。并在下个月的月度会议上跟进行更进，并追踪实施进度。

Set up a proper internal sourcing and external bidding process for subcontracting services and consult Chinaust Legal in contract review in case of new regulations or business model to avoid potential administrative or regulatory issues.
对委外加工服务建立适当的内部采购和外部询价的流程，并在新法规或新业务模式的情况下，在拟定合同前咨询亚大集团法务，以避免潜在的问题。

Enhance training on Hei Hu to all related staff at SHA Plastics, define and formally document procedures based on the use of Hei Hu to make sure maintenance tasks are correctly and timely recorded in Hei Hu. In the meantime, production management should conduct a periodic re-view on Hei Hu report to identify abnormal equipment failure cases or frequently occurred failure types and take action accordingly.
加强对上海亚大塑料所有相关人员的关于黑湖应用的培训，基于黑湖的使用定义和设计程序，以确保设备维修及保养任务被正确，及时地记录在黑湖中。 同时，生产部门应定期导出黑湖的内置报告，以发现异常设备故障事项或频繁发生的故障类型，采取相应的措施。

Procurement should include service providers into the scope of supplier periodic review and maintain review documentation for continuous supplier performance improvement.
采购部门应将服务类供应商包括在供应商定期评估的工作范围内，并保留相关审查文件，以持续改善供应商的绩效。

Define a correct credit exposure calculation methodology, in which the current AR balance, the new order value, and other credit commitments not reflected in the financial statement such as "Shipped not Invoiced" should all be covered. Follow the new credit-control methodology upon sales order receipts.
定义正确的信用敞口计算方法，应收账款余额，新订单的金额和财务报表中未反映的其他信用承诺，例如“发货未开票”都应涵盖在内。依据这个新的信用控制方法来接收订单。

Set up controls in the collection process e.g. reviewing payment terms in sales contracts and in case of unclear definition in the contracts, push sales to encourage favorable payment method "Wire Transfer" instead of drafts if possible via setting up additional criteria in AR collection KPIs, etc. In case of receiving drafts in certain unfavorable conditions (e.g. maturity longer than six months), upfront review from Finance Manager could be set up as a standard control to find possible endorsement to 3rd-party vendors before receipt.
在回款过程中设置相应控制，例如核查销售合同中的付款方式，对合同中付款方式定义不明确的情况，尽可能通过将汇款方式纳入销售人员个人指标等方式来促使销售人员鼓励客户采用有利的付款方式如电汇而不是汇票进行付款。对某些不利的汇票条件（例如，汇票到期时间超过六个月），可以将财务经理的前期审核设置为标准控制，以便在收取汇票前找到可背书的第三方供应商。

Analyze and record the reasons for negative or low margins per customer and per product. 
Based on the analysis, bring up the issues during the monthly operation meetings, define corrective actions for those controllable issues and timely follow them up in a documented approach.
分析并记录每个客户和每个产品出现负或低毛利的原因。基于分析，在每月的运营会议上提出这些问题，定义可控事项的纠正措施并采取行动。

Arrange monthly meetings between the local HR Manager and the Managing Director to go through the critical payroll changes such as bonus, salary increase, new hires, or leavers based on the payroll file and reconcile them with the original documents. If not feasible, then spot checks or sampling test could also be applied to certain wage elements, which are not self-detectable by the employees, such as monthly bonus calculated based on the overall performance of the entity or the department, i.e. sales or production volume. 
Meanwhile, for base salary (since neither mentioned in the employment contract nor maintained as master data in any database), in particular, additional controls should be put in place via comparing salary differences between last month and the current month, verification of leavers and newcomers, etc.
安排当地人力资源经理和总经理每月召开会议，对奖金、加薪、入离职人员等关键的薪资变动的情况针对最后形成的薪资报告进行检查，并与原始文件进行核对。如果不可行，则可通过抽查的方式，针对一些员工自身无法自查的薪酬项目，如: 基于销售量或生产量等公司部门整体业绩来核算的月度奖金等。
同时，特别是对于基本工资（因为其既没有在员工劳动合同中提及，也没有以主数据的形式在任何系统中进行维护），应通过比较上个月与当月的工资差异，核实当月入离职人员等形式，实行额外的控制措施。

Set up a quantified bonus calculation methodology with reference to the personal appraisal to evaluate the annual bonus of each management team member. 
参照个人考核，建立量化的奖金计算方法体系，以此为基准计算每位管理层员工的年度奖金。

Review the current work allocation and decide if a dedicated headcount is needed for IT management in a site running with 87 employees. 
Collect policies and guidelines from Chinaust group to formalize a local standard procedure to guide the operations of IT management. 
Implement control and monitoring mechanisms for hardware, software, and system access.
考虑到上海亚大塑料是拥有87名正式员工的公司，建议重新审视当前的工作分配，并确定是否需要安排专门的人员来负责IT管理。
向亚大集团了解和获取相关的政策和程序指南，参考从而制定本地的标准程序，来指导IT管理的日常运营。为硬件，软件和系统访问权限实施监控机制。

In terms of new hires, internal transfers, and labor contract renewals, incorporate remuneration clauses in the labor contract template or issue employees additional offer letters with salary information mentioned. Archive those offer letters as evidence by the HR Managers so that confidentiality on salary information is secured. Conduct a review on the existing labor contract template to ensure full compliance with the local labor laws on the remaining topics.
针对新入职员工或者内部调岗员工，将薪酬条款纳入劳动合同模板中或或向他们发出额外的录用意向书，并提及薪酬信息。重新审阅劳动合同模板并确保其他事项符合中国当地劳动法相关规定。

Engage professional tax specialist/tax consultancy agents to provide detailed interpretations of the concerned tax regulations and assess the current implementation of requested standards. 
Conclude and take action immediately on potential adjustment of current operations.
聘请专业的税务专家或顾问，提供有关转移定价的详细解读，并评估其要求标准的当前实施情况。
得出结论后立即采取措施，对当前业务进行所需的调整。

Refine the reimbursement procedure for business entertainment expenses, specifying the required documents and information for both application and reimbursement steps as per the new Chinaust Group Policy. 
Original bills/receipts are a prerequisite for proper monitoring and controls, if not possible to be provided by the restaurant or hotels, then document the reasons on the application form for explanation.
Set a threshold for internal controls, any external entertainment exceeding the limit, should request additional approval at Business Segment level (Chinaust Utility) for cost-saving purposes.
完善业务招待活动管理流程，明确亚大集团要求的申请和报销环节所需的材料和信息。
招待活动的消费水单应该是适当监控的前提，如果餐厅或酒店无法提供，则应当在申请表上记录说明原因。
设置内部控制的额度标准，任何超过该限额的业务招待费用，应向市政业务部申请额外的批准，以达到成本控制的目的。

Update the CDS quotation calculation template with the latest hourly cost rates. Research the prevailing market rates for the various types of engineering and customization work and consider these when quoting the selling rates.
Update the process description in MS2 with the specific documents that need to be changed and the parties to be informed.
Ensure sufficient resources are allocated to reduce backlog of pending change requests.

Ensure the pre-approval form is only used in cases where long delays are expected for the full approval on the project and credit applications. In general, as much as possible, R&D work should not commence until the corresponding credit application is fully approved at the highest level required (e.g. GF Executive Committee for projects > CHF 4 mio). Ensure this pre-approval process is reflected in the management system MS2.
Strictly follow the DoA requirements regarding R&D credit and supplementary applications.
Go through existing completed projects to ensure full documentation required by the project closing process. All future project closures should diligently adhere to the same requirements.

We recommend R&D Controlling develop a template to periodically reconcile time recorded in both the CATS and HR modules. HR module data should be provided by the HR Department to R&D Controlling on a monthly basis to be reconciled with the CATS data. Define a tolerance level for the deviations and ask R&D team leaders to comment on those with greater deviations.

Foster a culture of starting small and adding more when real demand is present rather than starting big and subsequently having to compromise or reduce machine features in order to bring MFC back to a saleable level. This should start with R&D critically challenging the URS proposed by Product Management in terms of technical viability and MFC achievability. 

During the development phase, R&D should cooperate with key component suppliers early and more closely in order to ensure components are purchased at lower prices whilst satisfying the functional requirements. Conduct MFC reviews more frequently during the prototype development phase to identify potential MFC overruns early on. This approach shall aim at reducing the likelihood of R&D resources being spent on the same tasks multiple times, e.g. going through additional iterations to re-develop machine features or re-validate components in order to reduce MFC.

Ensure the system enforced 4 eyes principle on technical drawings is clearly specified as part of the new SAP PLM project. Ensure texts on drawings are fully bilingual and include the language aspect in the peer review.

R&D, Advanced Purchasing Engineer, Production, Quality & Sustainability, Operational Excellence and Finance should work together to conduct a comprehensive cost and benefit analysis following the total cost of ownership approach regarding standardized key components on existing frequently produced machine models. This analysis should consider the cost difference between standardized and specific components, quantities of respective machine models sold historically and the impact of logistical and assembly process costs. Such analysis shall lead to a management decision model on a monetary basis. Ultimately, the P&L owner (TU Milling) shall make the decision to eliminate standardized components where a net loss is generated and continue or introduce standardization on components with a net gain.

Discuss within TU Milling and with the GFMS Customer Service Center, which will be responsible for documentation topic for TU Milling from May 2021, in order to find a solution going forward concerning documentation for CDS machines.

Evaluate the skill gaps within the R&D teams based on demands of existing as well as planned tasks and projects. Define specific training plans and seek approval from TU management to intensify further training and functional development or recruit new employees with the needed skills.

Develop a list of all contracts with business partners including all key criteria from all contractual agreements or ensure completeness of contractual relationships in the available SharePoint solution.

Distribute GF's Code for Business Partners to identified key suppliers. Inform relevant key suppliers about the requirement of acceptance by signature of GF's Code for Business Partners. For cases of push back consult with GFMS Strategic Procurement and Corporate Sustainability for next steps.

Re-instatement of regular safety patrols conducted by departmental heads and the local Health and Safety Manager to check the occupational safety on the company premises and compliance with GF's Health and Safety Standards (ROSS Meetings). Restrict access to the hazardous sub-stance storage unit to only trained and authorized employees. Ensure the installation of a first aid kit close by the hazardous substance storage unit and replace or substitute expired first aid mate-rial. Recurring review of all existing first aid materials for expiration dates.

Apply Strategic Procurement's "benefit book" locally. Get in touch with Strategic Procurement to determine to which extent savings have to be reported in the file. Ensure the correct disclosure of savings in the reporting file.

Ensure a complete review of all open purchase orders placed before 2021 to check whether there is still a purchase requirement for any of these parts. Cancel purchase orders for materials that are not required anymore. Include a review session of all open purchase orders, once a month in the internal Procurement department meeting.
Define a designated area for non-conforming parts and material with restricted access to the Quality department.

Consider to transfer the red zone area to the shelve units in the inbound area. Process the mate-rial in the red zone area in a timely manner. Move confirmed parts and materials in the red zone to a dedicated storage unit for production material.
Ensure a proper approval / validation of the inventory differences according to the Delegation of Authorities.

Define a timely follow up on internal quality audit findings as an integrated and substantial part in the quality audit process. Follow up on identified observations in a timely manner. Ensure the correct implementation of defined actions.

Ensure a proper inbound inspection on critical parts and material. In cases of deviations of mate-rial specifications, obtain exceptional approval or a material rejection order from the respective department (R&D and / or Quality department). Provide additional training on the quality process to the testing employee in the inbound inspection. Verify with R&D, to which extent the current measurement tolerances in the inspection checklists are still applicable.

Define mitigating actions with the respective employees to reduce / increase excessive hours to a reasonable level on their flextime account. Implement a monthly report for actual working hours and flextime balances of the employees and distribute it to the line managers in charge.
Revise the local Delegation of Authorities (DoA) and make sure, content and structure are in accordance with the Division DoA.
Make sure, all employees of symmedia sign for the receipt of the GF Code of Conduct.
Recognize monthly accruals for business transactions.
Post the bad debt provision in the allowance for receivables sub-account in A/R and report it accordingly in Cognos table KA31.
Populate the "Cyber Incident Response Binder" (CIRB) with all information required. Ensure that the populated CIRB is accessible for the local Management team and made available to GF's CISO.

Calculate the production duration of each work order in a more traceable and quantified manner by referring to pipeline lengths, quantities, etc. 
Use the available features of the scheduling (Gantt) chart to optimize the accuracy and visualization of production planning. In particular, choose a more appropriate measurement unit for scheduling and visualizing the schedule in chart form instead of pure text.
参考管道长度、数量等，以更可追溯和量化的方式计算每个工单的生产时间。
使用排产计划表的现有功能来优化生产计划的准确性和可视化。譬如，选择一个更合适的计量单位来安排生产工期，在图表中使用可视化功能进行排产计划，而不是纯粹地用文字来描述排产计划。

Fill in the required information in the repair and maintenance monthly report.
Perform analysis and follow-up actions on the machines with high Repair & Maintenance costs.
在设备维修月报中填写所有所需信息。
对高维修或保养成本的机器进行分析和制定后续改进行动计划

Develop guidelines for spare parts management including a definition of critical spare parts. 
Define safety stock levels for critical parts considering the unit price, lead-time, and criticality.
Analyze the necessity of adding an approval process upon deliveries of critical parts, or at least set a reasonable threshold/critical list for spare parts approvals.
Enhance the reconciliation process among the actual stock quantity, tracking sheet, and the request sheets on a weekly or bi-monthly basis to timely identify discrepancies.
Improve the stocktaking process, conduct analysis and adjustments on count differences by one independent employee based on the count results.
制定备件管理准则，包括定义关键备件。
综合考虑备品备件单价、交货时间和关键性，定义安全库存。
分析是否有必要在交付关键备件时增加一个审批程序，或者至少为备件的审批设定一个合理的额度/关键性清单。
加强每周或每个月，对实际库存、清单表和申请表之间的数量核对，以便及时发现差异。
优化盘点过程，由独立人员执行盘点分析和根据盘点结果的差异调整。

Sales and Logistics should jointly sign a consignment contract with Changchun Gas or at least add relative clauses in the sales contract to clarify the duties and responsibilities of both parties. In the meantime, agree on a reasonable method and frequency of stock reconciliation and physical stock take. 
For the external warehouse in Beijing, Logistics should deliver training covering areas of inventory management, goods receipt, and goods issue processes. 
On-site inspections should also be performed periodically to make sure the external warehouse is following the guidelines and maintain the same high standards as the internal warehouses.
销售部和运营部应协调与长春燃气公司签订寄售合同或至少在销售合同中增加条款，明确双方的责任，同时就库存核对和实物盘点的合理方式和频率达成一致。
对于北京的外租仓库，运营部应对其进行培训，包括库存管理、收货和发货流程等。现场检查也应不定时进行，以确保外部仓库遵循相应内部管理要求，并保持与一样的高管理标准。

Review the current formula designed for KPI reporting and correct the identified issues as well as other potential errors.
审查目前为运营管理报告设计的公式，纠正已发现的问题以及其他可能存在的错误。

Re-design the contract tracking list template to make a perpetual contract tracking system and make sure effective date, period and expiry date are included in this template and completely filled in when doing contract registration.
重新设计合同台账模板，使之成为一个永续的合同跟踪体系，并确保生效日期、期限和到期日都包含在这个模板中，并在登记台账时完整填写。

Review the current DoA items with reference to the corresponding policies and risk ratings, and consider if modifications are necessary, e.g. adding approval levels, (e.g. Department Managers) based on certain thresholds to reduce the number of approval transactions by the high-hierarchy levels to increase effectiveness.
参照相应的政策和风险评级，重新审阅目前的审批项目，并考虑是否有必要进行修改，例如，根据一定的数额门槛增加审批层级（如部门经理），以减少管理层的审批数量，提高审批的有效性。

Finance should simplify the customer margin report by highlighting abnormal margins and clearly communicate to Sales regarding what kind of information requires their attention and feedback. Sales should perform analysis on the sales margin report; make action plans and feedback to Finance in written form for action follow-up. It is also recommended to include the margin topic in the monthly sales meeting to enhance monitoring.
财务部应简化客户利润率报告，突出异常的利润率，并明确告知销售部哪些信息需要他们注意和反馈。销售部应该对销售利润率报告进行分析，制定行动计划并以书面形式反馈给财务部，以便采取后续行动。同时，建议在每月的销售会议中加入销售利润率话题，以加强监督。

In terms of credit control, implement the organizational set-up and responsibilities of the Finance Department under the Utility Shared Service Center. In specific, transfer the role of credit control from Sales to Finance to realize the actual enforcement of the credit management, and give a more transparent overview and understanding of the current credit exposure to the Fi-nance department.
In the annual credit review, involve Finance Department as a necessary participant. Document and let all participants sign on the review sheet to agree on the reasons for credit adjustments.
Apply the correct credit exposure calculation methodology during the daily credit check.
在信用控制方面，落实市政共享中心下财务部的组织结构和责任。具体来说，就是将信用控制职能移交给财务部，以实现信用管理的实际执行，并使财务对当前的信用风险有更透明的认识和了解。
年度信用审查时，将财务部门作为必要参与方。记录信用调整的原因，并让所有参与者签字认可。
在信用额度的日常检查中，应用正确的信用风险计算方法。

Arrange joint discussion among the management team, Finance, Sales, and Operation Departments to ensure a more accurate invoicing process and revenue accrual method based on the correct accounting principles instead of customer requirements.
In the NCC (updated version of ERP NC) design discussion with the service provider, request to add the function of automatic invoice allocation based on the correct accounting method. 
组织管理团队、财务、销售和运营部门进行讨论，以确保以正确的会计原则而不是客户的要求进行更准确的开票/暂估收入方法。
在与NCC（ERP NC的更新版本）服务提供商设计讨论中，要求增加基于正确会计方法的自动发票分配功能。

Re-arrange warehouse storage areas to ensure safe operations. 
Define clear working instructions on warehouse management and deliver training to warehouse staff to make sure they are understood.
