Consider paying all vendors using bank transfers to reduce administrative efforts required in signing and mailing out physical cheques. Require dual signatures on all cheques regardless of amount.
Eliminate the safety hazards identified. All issues identified in the future must be treated with high attention and immediate corrective actions.

Implement a periodic (i.e. semi-annual) verification of the material master data (i.e. lead times and reorder flags) to avoid excess purchases of materials and availability of actually critical materials.

Implement a process to systematically compare actual margins with expected margins on every machine sold. Cases with margin deviations greater than 3% should lead to a detailed review and documented feedback to Controlling. In general, the cost of sales on the PAW should not be manually changed unless it is a demo or used machine.
Approvals for deals with expected low margins should be obtained in a documented way, e.g. email communication.

Conduct a clean-up exercise on aged quotations that should no longer be kept as active. Instruct Regional Sales Managers to follow up with their respective teams on the active quotations more frequently to keep all quotations current.

Streamline the local process for sending Set-up Protocols timely to the plants. Train and empower installation engineers to directly send Set-up Protocols to the plants instead of the current process, which is prone to delay and omission.

Conduct a clean-up exercise on aged open service calls that are no longer relevant. Going forward, conduct a management review on all service calls aged above 3 months on a monthly basis. Increase efforts on reducing the backlog of open billable service calls.
Conduct a clean-up exercise on aged POs that are no longer relevant and cancel those POs. Ensure open purchase orders are reviewed regularly to reduce aged items.

Evaluate all remaining dealers on their actual value adding activities for GFMS US in consumables sales. For those that perform no value adding activities, stop paying them a commission for consumables. For those that do carry local stock and perform active selling, draft and sign formal agreements to govern consumables commissions. Such commission agreement should have a minimum turnover requirement and ideally be limited to 5%.

Consider using Sales Pad as the single application to issue quotations for service interventions, spare parts and consumables. Standardize the quotation template to include specific conditions (e.g. service intervention quotation is only covering estimated labor and travel costs but excluding parts), validity period as well as reference to the GFMS terms and conditions of sale.

Introduce a common hotline solution across all CS teams in order to standardize the phone support process. This solution shall enable the monitoring of hotline reachability as well as callback timeliness in case the customer cannot directly reach a diagnostic engineer.

Adapt the calculation method to consider local office hours and public holidays. Service requests not yet closed past the 30-minute limit should also be considered in the calculation. 
Consider adapting the 2022 KPI target with regards to the ratio of customers with access guards in each country, namely, countries with higher ratio of customers with access guards should have a lower target since this factor significantly affects the possibility of reaching the 30-minute connection time.
Define a unified approach at the BU CS level for all CS teams to calculate the utilization rate of FSE. The results shall be used to inform management on where capacity issues exist.

Instruct CS teams using SAP to consistently use the "machine down" indicator in the system. Evaluate if it is possible to introduce such an indicator in other software used by local CS teams. Adapt the MTTR calculation method to bring more transparency on the time it takes to repair cases where a machine is down.

Define concrete actions based on NPS feedback on both division and local levels. Such actions should be documented and monitored for their progress and completion.
Re-start the process of following up on bounced email invitations. BU CS should emphasize with the local CS teams to provide feedback on such follow-up made by the central NPS responsible.
Investigate the possibility to give the Quality Champions the ability to directly resend NPS surveys.

Re-launch the non-conformity reporting process from Division Quality and re-emphasize with the CS teams the importance of providing comprehensive information as well as answers to the follow-up questions raised by the TU Quality teams.

Implement a mandatory step to clarify the responsibility for stocking customized spare parts in the sales process of every customized machine. This should involve both the CS team and the TU where applicable, before confirming the machine order to the customer.
Ensure that a separate SO is created for any issue that occur after the machine basic installation. Monitor any "INS" SOs with multiple service reports to identify deviations to this rule.
Use service orders to record Applications activities for customers to ensure NPS surveys are also sent for such activities and that they are traceable in the ERP.

Close communication gaps amongst Sales, Order Processing, CS and Applications to enable the proper planning of Applications activities.
Ensure every machine sale with acceptance test conditions is verified and confirmed by Applications before order confirmation is issued to the customer.
Applications should track all test cut requests received and monitor their status including request date and fulfilment date.

Reduce backlog of open NC tickets and timely update all tickets with their latest status. 
Create Q-Cards for containment actions achieved via software updates and ensure machines on the production line also received the updated software version.
Liaise with TU EDM Losone and introduce the QR code scanning system for Q-Cards in order to bring traceability of Q-Card applications at TU Milling.
Review the current process for retrofit case management. Identify bottlenecks and enable task assignment and tracking in a systematic way, e.g. via a SharePoint solution.

Monitor and update all open PQI, PR and PI tickets requiring 3rd level software support directly in the TTP. Reconcile existing tickets in TTP with actual tasks in TFS to identify and close tickets that will no longer be worked on.
TU EDM Losone should implement a proactive follow-up process for all machines sold to sales companies without a Setup Protocol being returned to the TU.

Ensure transfer of knowledge from the experienced employee to the new hire. Hire a replacement for the departing employee to increase resources in the team in order to conduct overall verification and cleansing of the data in the variant configurator.
Reassess the 2 intermediaries by using the latest Excel version of Annex 1. Make also sure that every assessment is approved, validated and reviewed as required by CI 1061.

Analyze the services conducted by Globus Technical Ltd. for Iscar to verify the fees paid. In case such services justify the payment of a sales related fee, put in place a formal agent contract with Globus Technical Ltd. defining the nature and compensation of the services provided. Consider to limit payments to Globus for actually rendered services.

Establish processes to systematically identify, collect, report and reconcile data necessary to fulfill environmental as well as health & safety reporting requirements in SIS. Familiarize EHS Manager and local SIS responsible with the SIS definitions and update the Excel calculation sheet for cost and sales of waste.
Ensure adherence to the Finance Manual and record and report assets under construction as non-current assets and not as other receivables.

Define a RFQ procedure and develop a template that outlines the minimum elements to include in the comparison of supplier quotations. Ensure that the new process / template enhances the comparability and transparency in the supplier selection process and ensure a TCO approach. Communicate and implement the RFQ procedure / template accordingly for the selection of sup-pliers related to CAPEX.

Transfer the responsibility for procurement of non T-Systems related IT material and services to the local procurement department to ensure the standardized SAP purchase to pay process is complied with.
Put in place a regular review (i.e. monthly, weekly) of recorded purchasing documents to make sure confirmed delivery dates are recorded in SAP entirely.

Put in place a regular (i.e. monthly) review of purchased material to ensure POs are placed at the condition defined in the corresponding material info record. Ensure that any eventual discrepan-cies are investigated and discussed / resolved with suppliers if necessary. Perform a regular re-view of MRP purchasing records extracted from SAP. The review should involve the Production Planning department to ensure a full alignment on the lead times recorded in the MRP.

Based on last available audit reports, define an audit plan that outlines criteria to define suppliers in scope and frequency of the audits to perform. Also, put in place a monitoring overview to re-port audit findings, agreed due dates and the implementation status of remediation actions.

Based on Division Instruction "4.4 Mutual Rating", put in place an annual performance evaluation of locally-managed suppliers. Make sure to achieve adequate coverage (i.e. considering top sup-pliers per purchasing volume) and to document the annual ratings in a monitoring file / overview. Furthermore, track performance trends and make them available / visible at a glance.
Ensure that access to the external warehouse in the building shared with other tenants is restrict-ed to employees of GFMS System 3R only.
Train the responsible warehouse employees on the proper usage of stock movement types and their handling in SAP (i.e. for withdrawal and transferring processes).

Implement cross-department 5S audits. Address observations in the weekly shopfloor meeting and define responsible employees for corrective actions. Non-action by the responsible employ-ee should be addressed specifically and immediately to ensure the employee understands that 5S is mandatory and not optional.

Determine an hourly rate for Maintenance staff and systematically record time spent for each repair and maintenance activity per machine. Define appropriate KPIs and ensure that a cost and hours spent analysis on machine level is available and necessary actions are derived accordingly.

Cleanup the spare parts inventory through a systematic inventory and item analysis. Determine a safety stock for items considering replenishment periods that could jeopardize the continuation of production (critical spare parts). Implement a process for all spare parts movements as well as the spare parts maintenance in general.

Define suitable criteria (i.e. lead-time, value, or frequency) and processes in classifying spare parts and consumables, so that operational employees could at least follow the standards whenever new materials are requested, instead of just expense the materials upon receipt.
In order to mitigate redundant or unnecessary material codes piped up in SAP, besides the regular clean-up work, define higher criteria in value and frequency so that the total number of material codes of GFCS Suzhou is minimized to a controllable level.
Screen the purchasing data on the defined criteria before the next quarterly meeting, evaluate if any potentially overlooked materials are qualified as spare parts, and create material codes for them with necessary purchasing data such as lead-time, safety stocks, and reorder points.

Perform regular (e.g. weekly or monthly) reviews of all PM orders and make sure scheduled maintenance tasks are completed within a reasonable period (considering the adjustment ac-cording to production plan), e.g. no later than 30 days. 
For completed but not closed orders, data cleaning should also be performed during the review process.

The HR department should coach the department managers regarding how to set the development actions for high potential employees and perform regular monitoring on the status of these development actions. It is also suggested to involve the HR department in the review meetings with employees to provide assistance and necessary guidance to managers.

Assess the compliance and safety risks caused by the excessive overtime situation. 
Taking advantage of the compensation review process initiated by the Corporate C&B Department this year, reevaluate the current weight of overtime in the overall compensation system of the blue-collar workers, in comparison with other targeted companies in the local market and request new FTE or leased employee applications whenever possible.

Based on the latest GFCS divisional DoA, organize joint meetings among all relevant departments to set up feasible and corresponding criteria and thresholds, and finally reach out to the local and BU China ones.
Upon issuance of the new DoAs, arrange training on concerned individuals to understand the new changes and monitor their follow-ups on the DoA in their daily work accordingly. 
In case of changes in the future, clarity roles and responsibilities on who should identify the latest GFCS DoA issuance and initiate the update of local and BU China ones.

Request technical supports from the external service provider to solve all the functionality issues and deliver training to all the concerned operators to better understand the system functions and timely close the production steps.
Set up regular joint meetings between the TTMC Production Planner and the Project Managers to analyze the overdue items and update the forecast dates whenever necessary.
In case of special working orders such as maintenance, set up more feasible dates specifically for the maintenance work instead of copying the original dates of the relevant original production orders.

Re-design the tracking list template to form a centralized contract monitoring system. If Purchasing and Sales contracts must be managed separately, at least the purchasing contract list should include all purchasing contracts signed by all user departments to ensure a complete overview. 
In the meantime, incorporate the signing and expiration/termination dates, product/service pro-vided, price, and payment terms in this overview list template when registering the contracts.
Define rules to clarify in which cases contracts must be signed and ensure a proper contract is in place before business occurs and apply the latest GFCS contract template for VMI suppliers.

Discuss with GFCS Strategic Purchasing and internal departments to assess the feasibility of the current matrix, including:
- the requirements regarding QEV fulfillment and the number of quotes to be requested.
- the categories / thresholds to apply these rules (monetary levels, supplier, product or service categories, etc.), splitting TTMC purchases if necessary.
In case of urgent cases or single-source (e.g. non-standard materials, customer-specified vendors) situations, where the requirements could not be fulfilled, define special approval processes and application forms with explanations and proper approvals. Upon changes of the guideline, deliver training to the operational buyers regarding the QEV and RFQ requirements.
In terms of the specific freelancer services, find alternatives such as asking for the tax bureau to issue VAT invoices on behalf of the freelancers or self-employed.

Implement a formal and documented approach in postponing or advancing delivery dates of both general and TTMC sales orders to assure a traceable OTD calculation with sufficient supporting documentation.

Apply the info-record as the data source of material lead-time in the annual review of reorder points.
Set up a regular evaluation process to assess the lead-time accuracy based on actual material delivery data and correct major gaps whenever necessary.

Screen all the created vendors in SAP for all three GFCS BU China entities (including GFCS Shenyang after operation), streamline the most favorable conditions whenever possible, including the unit prices of common materials, payment terms (including term starting time points), payment methods, special clauses such as incoterms, etc. 
Implement reporting mechanism regarding condition changes by the other two entities to GFCS Suzhou to ensure that in terms of any favorable changes, all the other two entities could benefit from them simultaneously. 
Meanwhile, implement quarterly or at least annual reviews as internal controls to ensure any unnoticed new common vendors with different payment conditions or changes on payment conditions will be detected.

For assets that were transferred and only revealed during the asset count, a separate list should be summarized and given to the receiving department for confirmation to make sure all registered assets are counted. 
People who conducted the asset inventory should be required to sign on the counting sheet after performing the physical count. It is also suggested to align the template of the counting sheet so that the tick marks and remarks on the sheets can be understood easily when reviewing the reports. 
Finance should follow up on the scrapings revealed in the asset count, obtain the approval forms and make the adjustments in the systems accordingly.

Consult the GFCS Divisional Controlling team regarding the correct financial treatment on mold & tooling amortization expense accrual including the relevant SAP and HBII accounts. 
Consider to directly book the subsequent amortization expenses against other liabilities, in-stead of either accounts payable or inventory to mitigate the error-prone manual reclassification work. 
Reclassify the current balances from accounts payable to other liabilities.
Review the adequateness of the current accrual balances as required by the GFCS Directive at least twice a year for the mid-year closing and the year-end closing, and adjust the inventory balances accordingly.
In terms of the mold & tooling inventory balances (GL accounts 1003300, 1003301 & 1003303), better structure the current manual tracking sheets for amortization calculation, so that every single mold (internal order based) could be mapped with its amortization expenses, revenue achieved, as well as expense accrued (booked in other liabilities), so that in the project life cycle as well as project End of Production (EOP), clear traceability overview could be provided.

Clarify the definition of temporary and regular customer credit limit adjustments. For the adjustments that are made just for order release, the limit should be recovered afterwards. For regular adjustments, solid and sufficient justification should be documented and the adjustments should be approved by General Manager regardless of the percentage.
Request supports from GFCS Division to understand the root causes and solve the errors. Perform walkthrough tests upon solutions implemented to ensure the QSYS results are reliable.

Define WIP and internal scrap reconciliation controls including the operational procedures of the reconciliation among different data sources, control frequency, treatments of reconciliation differences and stock take differences as internal scrap, and follow-up roles and responsibilities as well as approval matrix for adjustments as internal scrap.
In terms of the data for reconciliation, assess the data reliability before setting up the internal reconciliation control:
- WIP reconciliation: SAP recorded casted part figures and finished good inbound,
- WIP reconciliation: WMS recorded WIP figures and SAP figures of casted parts.
- Internal Scrap: NCR and SAP internal scrap figures.
Monthly check that all the open differences are cleared upon in the next month to avoid any knowledge loss after a certain period.

Implement a review methodology for open PM orders and make sure all planned maintenance tasks are completed as scheduled. In terms of change of maintenance plans, for example, the reduction of maintenance frequency, it is suggested to obtain approval and make modifications in the SAP plan to avoid outstanding orders. 
Check with IT to embed a mechanism to review and update status of all maintenance plans.
For equipment failure analysis, the actions should be defined in a more detailed way with responsible person recorded, and be followed up until completion with supporting documents obtained.

Discuss and update the internal policy regarding one-time vendor management, including the limits set for the transaction times, transactional threshold, prepayment booking method, and PO creation requirements under the one-time vendor account, and reach a consensus with the GFCS BU China Buyer regarding the internal policy.
Implement a quarterly review control on the transactions of the one-time vendor account to ensure internal usage compliance and in case of any special or urgent cases in breach of the policy, proper approvals from the higher management team should be obtained and documented. 
Require all production-related services to tick GR requirements upon PO creation, to ensure proper service reception before payments, such as service quality, timeliness, and performance.
In terms of one-time subcontracting services, required the vendors to sign quality terms (after consulting GF Legal) to avoid potential legal disputes.

For customer returns, it is suggested to perform a monthly analysis on the goods return rate by product and identify product with relatively high return rate, this could help identify potential systematic quality issues on an early stage. Once actions are defined to tackle the quality issues identified, the actions should be given a clear responsible person and tracked continuously until actions are completed and issues are resolved.
In terms of penalties and compensations, Product Quality Engineers (PQE) should have a clear picture of their responsible customers' penalty policies, preferably in documented form. For example, whether the customer has a vendor management system to display quality issues, compensation and penalty calculation methodology, the compensation is directly deducted from bills or paid to customers separately, and how the appeal process works. 
On a monthly basis, the compensation claims, goods returns, quality issues published on customer websites, and customer complains should be reconciled to make sure no extra compensation is paid. The appeals made should also be recorded and in future used as a reference for contract terms negotiation.

Strengthen the annual bonus payment control in involving BU China Finance Director in the approval Email exchange process so that proper reconciliation among the three data sources (SAP payroll data, e-banking details as well as the original approved bonus document) could be conducted.

Firstly, a risk management mechanism should be established to ensure that, in case of similar cases in the future, the negotiation with the customer can be initiated in a timely manner and follows a clear procedure. In the meantime, the evaluation on potential impact should also be made as early as possible in documented form and be monitored and updated regularly. Based on the evaluation, financial treatment should be made to timely and truthfully reflect the expected compensation amount. 
Secondly, for the specific case of Sinotruck, if no written response (even if not confirmed yet) from the customer can be obtained, financial provision should be made on the best assessment considering scenarios from positive to negative assumption. 
Finally, the returned parts should be properly reflected in the cost of quality, and a reconciliation process of cost of quality should be established and followed (referring to the recommendations of Audit Finding 04).

Arrange joint meeting among all relevant departments, i.e. Sales, Finance, Sales Service and Legal, to clarify and determine the latest terms currently agreed and signed with the customers in the any kind of written form, including sales incoterms, payment methods and payment terms.
Maintain the revenue recognition and payment method in a tracking list like payment terms to identify changes immediately and reflect accordingly in the financial statements and timely raise Legal review request.
In terms of the special Beiben AR overdue case, analyze the call-off situation and book corresponding bad debt before 2021 year-end closing.

A regular material master data review focusing on purchasing criteria including material ABC category should be performed at GFCS Kunshan. In case of redundant material code, perform regular clean-up process to enhance data accuracy.

Make sure all signed contracts are registered in the tracking list with necessary contractual terms maintained. 
Make clear regulations that in which circumstances a contract must be signed. It is also suggested that Purchasing perform an annual review on the accumulated spending of suppliers and if the annual spending exceeds the T CHF 250 threshold, contract should be signed and reviewed accordingly. 
Apply GFCS VMI template contract for all VMI suppliers.

Based on the material ABC category, MRP types and responsible departments, set corresponding review frequencies on safety stock levels and reorder points, such as monthly review for A materials, quarterly review for B materials, and annually review for C materials to ensure accuracy in MRP run
Arrange joint meetings among Controlling, Purchasing, and Maintenance departments, firstly reset correct safety stocks or reorder points in SAP for the identified cases to avoid unnecessary purchases. Secondly, reach consensus in defining a fair and rational calculation methodology of safety stocks or reorder points, so that the monthly review meeting could be processed based on a mutual understanding, thus more efficient and targeted.

For suppliers with regular and high spending but are not categorized as A/B level, for example, the logistics suppliers, a re-sourcing plan should be made and documented in the sourcing guidelines to make sure existing vendors are regularly (every 3 to 5 years) compared with the market. Besides the price, payment method and payment terms should also be proactively reviewed and negotiated with suppliers for continuous improvement. 
Perform regular review on SAP master data to be aligned with the contracts.

To be discussed: Wang, QL decides on the methodology and align operations of SZ and KU, as well as deliver trainings and reviews. 
Define clear roles and responsibilities, as well as the methodology for environmental investment and cost summarization. EHS Manager should review the details of the expenditures to ensure the accuracy of SIS figures. 
Separately report the income and cost of external recycling activities.

Establish a review and systematic approval process of sales project calculation. Ensure the updated manufacturing cost and gross margins are reviewed and signed by the General Man-ager and CFO. 
Clear instructions should be defined and communicated to the KAMs that when submitting sales conditions to the Calculator, the MTZ mechanism has to be either confirmed by the cus-tomer or estimated conservatively.
When conducting the calculations, if leaving out certain assumptions, such as MTZ surcharge, justifications should be documented and approved by the Sales Manager, CFO, and General Manager.

Together with GFCS Suzhou, discuss with GFCS Strategic Purchasing and internal departments to assess the feasibility of the current matrix, including:
- the requirements regarding QEV fulfillment and the number of quotes to be requested.
- the categories/thresholds to apply these rules (monetary levels, supplier, product or service categories, etc.).
Upon changes of the guideline, deliver training to the operational Buyers regarding the QEV and RFQ requirements.

Re-define the scope and calculation methodology for supplier OTD evaluation. In terms of scope, consumable material suppliers and suppliers who provide important services (for exam-ple, repair of production equipment) are suggested to be included. 
The correct calculation methodology for OTD is comparing actual goods receipt (GR) date with required delivery date; this should be monitored, analyzed, and improved accordingly.

Draft and execute a manufacturing agreement with Marquest in consultation with Corporate Legal. 
It is recommended to centralize the management of contracts and maintain a contract register that enables the easy retrieval and monitoring of existing contractual relationships for upcoming renewal or termination deadlines.
Review records in archive against the requirements and exemptions specified in Corporate Instruction 1026. Destruct any records with expired retention period.
Implement the commission structure for agent Dama in SAP to automate the calculation process and eliminate the risk of manual calculation errors.
Adapt internal process to start repairing damaged rental machines without the unnecessary wait for customer confirmation on the repair costs.
Recalculate general warranty provision in accordance with the Division Instruction 3422. Release unneeded general warranty provision to avoid doubling of provision at consolidated division level.

Define a clear process for granting early payment discounts, including authority levels for accepting the discount after the early payment deadline. Any required approvals should be kept on file, ideally attached in SAP to the corresponding customer payment or invoice.

Adapt the current calculation method for bad debt provision. Follow the statistical calculation according to Division Instruction 3432. For additional doubtful accounts, specific identifiable provision can be made in consultation with the BU Controller and should be shown separately in COGNOS form KA31 in the section "Identifiable value adjustments".

Apply the required useful life periods on future machines in the rental pool in accordance with Division Instruction 3440. For existing machines with incorrect useful life period, consult BU and Division Controlling on how to proceed.
Document the root causes for stock discrepancies on the stock adjustment application form. Ensure product scraps follow the correct internal procedure for application and approval.
Implement a process to monitor sales orders with margin anomalies based on internally defined margin tolerances. Sales orders with manually modified prices should be prioritized in this review.

Consistently document the approval for non-standard payment term directly in SAP, e.g. by uploading a copy of the email approval from the MD.
Review existing payment terms during the next credit review and affirm or reduce non-standard payment terms that no longer makes business sense.
Investigate and address the root causes of blocked billing documents. Going forward, review blocked billing documents on a monthly basis to avoid potential missed billing.
Make sure, job descriptions are accurate, up-to-date and available for all functions.

Use SAP transaction YRFI0077 for the calculation of the stock provision.

In cooperation with the CFO of BU Americas decide under what circumstances the items without consumption put to stock for the first time can be excluded from the stock provision and in which cases they cannot be excluded.
Put in place a contract with Estudio Eduardo Schmilovich. Verify upfront if a suitable contract template is available on the GF intranet for such a business relationship.

Make sure the stock taking is performed according to the blind count method. Follow the proceeding defined in SAP for the stock taking. Follow-up large counting differences in the monthly cycle counting and in the annual stock taking. Define and implement mitigating actions accordingly. Communicate the result of the stock taking and the mitigating actions to the Financial Controller on a monthly basis.
Make sure that each lot of plastics raw material is quality checked in a laboratory accordingly.
Implement a password policy on local client PCs in line with GF standards.
Check the migration of the local IT environment to T-Systems and take action accordingly.
Implement OneDrive and migrate the data on Dropbox to OneDrive with support from the IT department of BU Americas.
Review the local DoA of GFPS Brazil and align it with the DoA of BU Americas in terms of structure and value limits. Communicate the revised local DoA accordingly.

Review the documentation of the internal control system on ICS@SharePoint. Add the missing control descriptions to cover all relevant control risks. Make sure that the control descriptions accurately reflect the actual control situation. Where necessary improve the controls accordingly.
Prepare a local record retention guideline in line with Corporate Instruction 1026 "Records Retention" (CI 1026) and statutory requirements and implement it accordingly.
Accrue payments for goods and services issued on an annual basis but consumed throughout the year accordingly.

Perform a systematic review of the credit limits at least annually and adapt the credit limits accordingly. Check whether the service of SPC should not be rendered on a flat fee basis. Check whether the implementation of SPC's automatic notification service of changes in the creditworthiness would make sense, at least for the most relevant customers.
Make sure, a full blind stock count incl. verification of the counting differences and the reconciliation of the values in the systems of TGA and Totvs is performed every year before year-end.
Nominate substitutes for every function and instruct / train them accordingly.
Upload the contracts of GFMS Brazil to the GFMS Contract database on SharePoint and fill in the requested meta data.
Prepare a local regulation of how to implement the Corporate Instruction 1026 on records retention at GFMS Brazil.

Review the local chart of accounts in terms of making it more suitable for the Cognos reporting.

In cooperation with Division and BU Controlling review the Excel tool used for the Cognos reporting in order to reduce complexity and make sure that there are no errors.

Nominate and train a person to take over the monthly reporting in case the Controller would drop out.

Make sure that the data on the Totvs server is backed up on a daily basis.

In cooperation with GF IT define a concept for the backup generations to be kept.

Check performing the daily backups automatically to a cloud service meeting the requirements of GF IT. If this is not possible, keep the backups outside of the premises in a safe place. Retain the backup generations on separate storage media.

Consider replacing the local server by a cloud-based solution meeting the requirements of GF IT.
Send the payroll file to the Managing Director before releasing the payroll payment in eBanking to ensure a preventive control on the payroll can be performed.
Consider the appointment of a dedicated person with a sales background to foster the sales in the areas of rConnect, preventive maintenance and consumables.

Analyze the probability to collect the overdue receivables > 1 year case-by-case. 

Prepare an action plan of how to proceed in each of these cases to collect the money or take back the machine from the respective customers as soon as possible. 

Organize the necessary internal or external resources to fulfill the task within due time. 

Check whether the uncollectible amounts should not be written off.

Make sure that there is an annual full blind count accompanied by GF staff, performed preferably at year end. Analyze the differences in detail and ensure that the origin of mistakes and errors are clearly identified at either TGA or GFMS Brazil.
Develop and implement a template for the assessment and valuation of second hand machines traded in.

Based on DI 7323, define a procedure / template that outlines the minimum elements to include in the comparison of supplier quotations in order to enhance comparability and transparency in the supplier selection process.

Update the current stock taking instruction by including a dedicated procedure for verification / counts of material stocked at external processors. Make also sure the aforementioned procedure includes the performance of onsite physical stock counts based on  a risk-based approach (e.g. value of stocked material / historical discrepancies).

Put in place a regular (e.g. monthly / quarterly) review of production material with safety stocks and ensure all critical production materials are included. Ensure that a documented version of the review is retained.

Put in place stock management review and classification procedures in accordance with DI 5896 methodology. Make sure the review is performed at least twice a year as required by the aforementioned Divisional instruction.

Define criteria to document the review of standard assembly times. Make sure it includes the machine models in scope, systematic comments from preparer / reviewer on recorded deviations and evidence of final decision taken on standard times (e.g. changed / not changed).
Conduct a regular review (e.g. bi-annual) of the ICS matrix. Ensure that the description and ownership of internal controls are accurate and assigned to the proper responsible.

Review the current SoD across all departments. Make sure that issuance of sales orders are segregated from External Sales & Marketing personnel and stock taking adjustments are at least reviewed and approved by the Finance department prior to any posting.

Develop a contract overview file including all key criteria (i.e. start date, end date, potential covenants, notice periods, automatic renewal clauses etc.) from all contractual agreements currently in place with GFPS Sweden. Ensure to maintain it on a regular basis.

In collaboration with the CS department, implement a monthly review of unbilled orders for welding machine rentals. Make sure the review focuses on key information such as the date of orders / contracts, the rental period and planned billing dates. Based on this, ensure that revenue is recorded consistently with the rental period.

Review the current stock taking process and make sure that related adjustments are always reviewed by the Finance department prior to posting on SAP. Authority to post adjustments needs also to be restricted to an adequate limit assigned to the Pre-fabrication Manager. 
Perform regular (e.g. monthly) analysis of stock variances per material and investigate the most relevant / recurring discrepancies. Ensure that stock taking results are recorded on SAP with the appropriate stock movement types ("701" and "702") to increase transparency of the stock adjustments.
Given the volume of pre-fabrication activities, consider the implementation of a stock management tool to improve the inventory controlling.

Put in place a regular (e.g. quarterly) review of open rental contracts of welding machines with expired validity. Make sure the related machines are still used by the customer and update the contractual validity accordingly. Ensure that the Finance department is informed / involved so that eventual clean-up (e.g. sold or missing machines) are accounted for properly.
Define the SAP QM module as standard reporting and management tool for customer complaints. Ensure that adequate training is provided to the CS employee for utilising it.

Put in place a form for reporting material scrapping from either quality tests or welding operations. Such form should include the material number, the quantity / size, the customer order number and the reason for scrapping. Based on that, implement a regular (e.g. monthly) review of scrapped materials and make sure to document it on an appropriate format (e.g. Excel). If possible, locate the scrapped material on a dedicated storage location(s) of the warehouse.

Perform a gap analysis between the current version of the BU DoA and the Division DoA. Develop an updated, harmonized BU DoA version for any identified discrepancies. Communicate the updated version of the BU DoA to all respective employees and entities within the BU.

Improve the understanding of key applicable incoterms (i.e. DAP) by conducting a training with all people involved in the revenue recognition analysis procedures. Implement a process to systematically identify shipments with critical shipping terms (i.e. DAP) shipped before crucial cut-off dates. Ensure that revenue is recognized in the correct accounting period and based on the agreed upon shipping terms.

Assess an option to develop a comprehensive report for the local ERP supporting a "range of coverage" analysis and calculation. Calculate the stock provision at least quarterly. Assess the current stainless steel parts provision calculation for release potential (i.e. by considering the scrap value).
Report the onerous contracts provision in the intended Cognos section. Agree with Division Controlling how to handle / report onerous intercompany transactions in the future.
Implement a regular monitoring and follow up process on open GR/IR items aged over 3 months. Ensure the results of the follow-up action are documented and releases are recorded accordingly.

Apply the cluster analysis as prescribed in Division Instruction 3436 to the current customer base. Derive payment terms harmonization and improvement potential and implement updated payment terms accordingly / where possible.
Consider the credit insurance deductible in the allowance for bad debt calculation. Report the overall value adjustment in the intended section in Cognos.

Ensure that selected useful life periods are in line with the schedule from Division Instruction 7756. Document the selected useful life period by referencing it to the respective rules / useful life period selected from Division Instruction 7756.
Directly expense application software with acquisition cost below T CHF 50 or collect the approval from the Corporate Controlling department before capitalizing the asset.

Take a decision on retrospective correction or not. Conduct an awareness training concerning operating vs. finance lease with key employees involved (i.e. Procurement department, first line management). Implement a properly documented operating vs. finance lease assessment procedure.
Ensure that the four-eyes principle is followed for recounting of stock differences. Collect the approvals for inventory discrepancies resulting from stock taking as prescribed by the Division DoA.

Develop an action plan to reduce the current maintenance backlog by prioritizing machines with high malfunction / breakdown potential. Review the maintenance tasks of all machinery and reconcile the tasks and maintenance intervals with manufacturer requirements. Develop a dedi-cated maintenance plan for the packaging machine aligned to the manufacturer's user manual and include the preventive maintenance intervals for the machine in the ERP system.

Define the single value of the Privilege Club points. With the support of Business Unit and Division Controlling, implement a process to ensure an accounting treatment of such points in compliance with the Division Policy.

In the current provision file, make sure to report margin deduction and refurbishment cost on separate fields. Review the used machines listed in the stock provision file to make sure all refurbishment costs are considered in the calculation.
Develop a skill matrix with all necessary knowledge areas applicable to GFMS System 3R's product technology.
Implement a regular (e.g. monthly) margin control on billable service intervention based data available on SAP ticketing / service order system.

Implement a regular (e.g. monthly) review of goodwill service orders and ensure they are approved as required by the Division DoA. Make sure to record Customer Services tickets / service orders by using the appropriate identification code in SAP.
Implement a regular (e.g. monthly) gross margin review at sales order level to enhance transparency and ensure compliance with Division DoA where required.

Perform a gap analysis between the current version of the local DoA and the current GF Machin-ing Solutions DoA and extend the local DoA by the key competences. Consider to involve GF Internal Audit for consultation. Communicate the updated version of the local DoA to all involved employees. Present planned AR write-offs to the MD on a quarterly basis for the proper AR write-off approval. File the signed approval sheet in the monthly closing documentation.
Develop a contract overview including all key criteria from all local supplier contractual agreements currently in place with GFMS International.

Update the employment contract templates in consultation with Corporate Legal. Seek advice from Corporate Legal on how to handle existing contracts signed containing this outdated word-ing. It is recommended to have an explicit written confirmation from the employee that he or she accepts GF's Code of Conduct. Ensure completeness of personnel files by creating job descrip-tions defining the tasks and responsibilities of all permanent employees.
Create a template to monitor commissions paid to agents on a transactional level and annual turn-over.

Implement an alignment process for social KPI's between the SSC and the local Financial Control-ler before uploading final results to SIS and Cognos.
Ensure the correct interpretation of the Finance manual resulting in equal results in Cognos and SIS and collect all necessary information for an appropriate reporting (i.e. collect training docu-mentation of employees from GFMS Academy).

Reclassify rental machines with contractual terms greater one year from current to fixed assets. Seek advice from Divisional Controlling to determine how to proceed with the reclassification of the identified rental machines to fixed assets.
Conduct a regularly performed (i.e. quarterly) detective review to identify reclassification potential for machines under rental contracts.

Review and understand calculations provided by the Head of Corporate Employee Benefits be-fore recording an additional provision in SAP. Gain a proper understanding whether Austrian and Dutch employees can be excluded from the general calculation template provided.
Update and complete the skills matrix started in 2021. Distinguish the skill sets of each employee according to the respective GFMS technology.
Enforce the local process for sending set-up protocols to the plants in a timely manner. Train and empower installation engineers for a timely submission of set-up protocols to the plants.

Sicherstellung und Einrichtung eines IT-Zugangs für die Mitarbeitenden von GFCS Meco Eckel zum Konzern- und Divisions-Intranet. Abstimmungen auf Divisons- und Business Unit Ebene welche Weisungen für GFCS Meco Eckel anzuwenden sind. Schriftliche Fixierung der Überein-kunft beider Parteien sowie Durchführung einer dedizierten Unterweisung aller Mitarbeitenden von GFCS Meco Eckel betreffend der relevanten Konzern- und Divisionsweisungen.

Gewährleistung der Vollständigkeit der Personalakten durch die Definition von Stellenbeschrei-bungen, in denen die Aufgaben und Zuständigkeiten aller Mitarbeitenden klar geregelt und fest-gelegt sind. Einholen einer schriftlichen Bestätigung jedes Mitarbeitenden für den Empfang des GF Verhaltenskodex. Aufnahme eines Hinweises in die neu gestaltete Eintrittscheckliste, mit dem neue Mitarbeitende den Empfang des GF Verhaltenskodex mit deren Unterschrift bestätigen.

Sicherstellen der korrekten Konsolidierung und Dokumentation der eingeholten Informationen für die Berichterstattung in SIS. Bei Fragen zur Definition von einzelnen Kennzahlen, die hinterleg-ten Weisungen in SIS heranziehen, oder die jeweils Verantwortlichen zu Nachhaltigkeitsthemati-ken auf Divisionsebene kontaktieren.
Sicherstellen einer umfangreichen Kreditwürdigkeitsprüfung von Lieferanten (auch strategisch) vor der Einräumung von Krediten und Darlehen.
Überprüfung der Umsetzbarkeit von gemeinschaftlichen Zeichnungsberechtigungen für den Bankenverkehr.

Diskussion mit dem Konzerncontrolling auf welcher Grundlage die Bildung einer Gewährleis-tungsrückstellung ohne entsprechende Fälle in der Vergangenheit dennoch möglich ist. Abstim-mung des Memos von GFCS zur Bildung von Gewährleistungsrückstellungen mit dem Konzern-controlling. Anschliessende Überführung der Vereinbarung in eine Weisung der Division und deren Veröffentlichung im GFCS Intranet.  Behandlung der Gewährleistungsrückstellung gemäss der neu gefassten Vorgaben.

Implementierung eines formalisierten Prozesses zur Bonitätsprüfung von Neukunden über bei-spielsweise Dun & Bradstreet. Überprüfung einer Zusammenarbeit mit GFCS Altenmarkt bei der Bestimmung eines Bonitätsprüfungsprozesses und der Berechnung bzw. Vergabe von entspre-chenden Kreditlimiten der einzelnen Kunden.

Sicherstellen, dass alle identifizierten Sicherheitsrisiken von den Abteilungsverantwortlichen am Shopfloor beseitigt werden. Überprüfung der eingelagerten Verbrauchsmaterialien und Teilen auf Notwendigkeit. Fachgerechte Entsorgung von nicht betriebsrelevantem und / oder defekten Material. Alle in Zukunft identifizierten Sicherheitsrisiken nachdrücklich mit Sofortmassnahmen beseitigen.

Überprüfung in wie weit ein Arbeitsvertrag im Verhältnis zum geplanten Renteneintrittsalter noch sinnvoll ist.
Festlegung eines geeigneten Zeitraumes (z. B. das Arbeitsverhältnis besteht noch > 5 Jahre) für die nachträgliche Einführung eines schriftlichen Arbeitsvertrages in Abstimmung mit dem Divisi-ons HR in Schaffhausen.
Revision der aktuellen Berechnungsgrundlage zu den Stundensätzen in regelmässigen Zeitab-ständen (z. B. alle zwei Jahre). Entsprechende Aktualisierung der neu ermittelten Stundensätze in Sage.

Review the asset allocation as defined in Schedule 7 "Investment Management". Consider reducing the loan to GF to a value in line with the regulation of the FINMA and preventing a recapitalization of GF Munot Re. Adapt the Schedule 7 and implement the new asset allocation accordingly.

Pricing strategy for parts: update the pricing master data in the quotation tool according to the latest GFMS divisional pricelist and perform double-check upon each update to ensure timeliness and completeness. 
Pricing strategy for consumable sales: set up a methodology or algorithm in the pricing strategy, e.g. cost plus mark-ups, per consumable material or at least consumable group. In case of any adjustment deviating from the pricing strategy, add remarks with the latest modification date to support the price changes, i.e. low realized margins, market trends, purchasing price changes, etc.

Regarding margin approval on quotations for both parts and consumables: evaluate the current discount authorization levels and their impacts on each individual product margin. 
Adjust minimum prices (consumable sales) or maximum discount levels (part sales) on item level whenever necessary to ensure DoA compliance. 
Consider incorporating both the targeted margins per product or category (wires, accessories, etc.) and the DoA margin requirements into the monthly CS analysis to compare them with the realized margins analysis, and define actions whenever necessary.

Set up controls on a monthly or quarterly basis to identify the three types of data inaccuracy.
Collaborate with the GFMS supplying plants on the identified unreasonable plant warranty dates and correct the data whenever necessary.
Investigate the reasons for the incorrect sales warranty dates and serial numbers and correct the data whenever necessary.

Deliver training to outsourced service engineers regarding how to fill in and submit the service report, make sure the service reports are collected and the related data is obtained for analysis. In the meantime, it is suggested to also perform a regular review on the PMC backlog, document the reasons, and perform timely actions.

Define a more flexible pricing strategy for this special PMC type, e.g. differentiating the prices by machine conditions for new customers, and using historical realized costs plus a mark-up percentage for existing customers. 
Consider incorporating maximum service hours in the contract to avoid potential negative margins and monitor the contractual hours during the PMC course, especially for the customers with negative margins in the past.
Apart from the existing DoA requirement on the general PMC contract, define a specific one for such PMC cases, the estimated costs in the quotation phase should be documented with proper rationale and support.
Enhance the data accuracy of the PMC report by regular review from an independent person, such as the Finance team. If possible, propose the contract period the same as the fiscal year from January 1 to December 31, thus simplifying the financial calculation.

Add the manual process of performing the straight-line revenue recognition to the monthly closing checklist and deliver training to the concerned Finance employees on how to perform the calculation according to the GFMS Revenue Recognition Policy.

Set up a monthly or quarterly review process among Finance, Supply Chain and Sales departments regarding the defective parts and consumables and book the corresponding stock provision in addition to the slow-moving materials. 
Correct the algorithm in the manual machine provision sheet to incorporate all the options together with the machine value for depreciation.

Finance and CS should align the categorization methodology of goodwill services and deliver training to hotline engineers and CS assistants. If possible, service tickets should only be created by Hotline Engineers so that the categorization is performed by a limited number of people to reduce the chance of mistakes and the complexity of training. 
It is also suggested to perform a regular FOC service analysis once an accurate list is made available, to identify potential PMC customers.

Define clear roles and responsibilities of the contract (purchasing and compliance documents) overview among departments. 
Build up an effective communication process such as using a common shared folder to ensure timely and complete updates of the contract-tracking list.
Based on the tracking list, consult the iScala service provider for solutions for data extraction and if not possible, perform sample-based material and vendor master data review in a documented manner.

Summarize and determine a list of key performance indicators for warehousing and logistic service providers and have them agreed upon in the service contracts. Establish a regular performance evaluation process. Data during daily transactions should be properly collected, summarized, and utilized to form a quantified result. 
Actions should also be taken according to the evaluation result, such as initiating price re-negotiation for logistics services.

Contact GFMS Logistics Purchasing Department to obtain the latest price list with the logistics supplier and build up effective communication channels whenever there is an update or amendment with the supplier.
Implement a systematic verification process of logistics service invoices where invoiced charges are compared with agreed price lists.

Understand the category definition and data collection method for “Investments in health & safety”, "Cost for health & safety", "Hours worked of leased employees" as well as the "fuel consumption" to report the 2022 SIS data correctly.

Set up targets both on individual and average levels to control the employee overtime situation.
Analyze the root causes of excessive overtime, especially on certain employees, and implement actions to balance the workload among employees.
Assess the needs for additional FTE and raise requests in case of employees in certain positions whenever the overtime situation has been consistently overrun.

Strengthen the bonus verification control for not only the employees via the standard bonus algorithm but also those granted a lump-sum amount in form of a bonus letter. 
Enhance four-eyes principle in verifying each bonus letter with the payroll file.

Maintain the intermediary tracking list with complete scope and information, including the signing dates of compliance annexes for timely renewal.
Reinforce the importance of dating the Compliance Agreements upon signing by both parties and completing the Anticorruption Questionnaire before commencing business with the intermediaries.
Update the agency, distributorship and machine trial contract templates based on the group ones after consulting the China Legal Counsel for possible modifications to both secure GF interests and meet the local needs.

Make sure the overall responsibility for the SIS reporting is clearly assigned to one designated employee. Implement an alignment process between the designated responsible and the involved departments prior to finalizing the reporting on SIS. Ensure that the calculation of social and environmental KPIs is line with the definitions of the SIS reporting manual.

For purchased parts, make sure the evaluation methodology is in-line with the Division Instruction. Perform also an analysis to determine in advance the potential impact on inventory arising from such change in the evaluation criteria.
Define a deadline to fully implement the MRBR transaction and ensure a regular follow-up of the GR/IR items by Operational Purchasing.

Implement a regular (e.g. annual) review of transactions recorded under the OTC accounts to ensure transparency of sales transactions. Define a policy / instruction to formally limit the utilization of such accounts to minor non-recurring sales transaction only.

Review the current credit limit file and make sure every customer has an assigned limit. Ensure that the Financial Controller approves and reviews self-insured limits up to T CHF 50 on the basis of historical turnover and credit worthiness report (e.g. Dun & Bradstreet) as required by DI "Credit Policy". Consider to resolve the technical issue on SAP to enhance the transparency of this key credit control, otherwise, require the spindle sales team refer to the current credit limit file before placing each order to ensure the customer has available credit.

Investigate the cases identified. Resolve the cases in an appropriate matter, e.g. by invoicing the customer for the consignment stock used or transferring the material back to inventory if the physical item was indeed returned. Use SAP transaction MB52 to monitor all stock consigned to customers on a quarterly basis and ensure timely follow up.

Implement an effective verification methodology for invoices from LSPs. In case of high volume of positions and invoices to be verified, establish a consistent sampling method to gain assurance on the accuracy of invoices before releasing. Sample checks conducted should be documented to ensure an audit trail.

Ensure work-in-progress components on the shop floor are clearly identified with the serial number of the destination machine. Machines on the final assembly floor that are in production stop should be visually identified and the pending issues summarized on a documentation attached to the machine. In order to achieve better management overview, it is recommendable to enable the "Notes" function in the Power BI Operation Cockpit for all group leaders so the issues history of each machine can be centrally documented.

Implement solutions in the respective departments to reduce interruptions in the assembly flow. Increase and/or redirect competent resources to analyze and solve root causes of interruptions in a sustainable way to avoid recurrence. This shall help create a positive environment for final assembly to improve productivity and output as well as reduce further turnover.

Ensure production machinery are maintained according to manufacturer recommendations and the maintenance performed are documented. Implement regular checks by management on the production floors to ensure timely maintenance of all production machinery.

Define standardized quality control plans including exact measurements or tests to be taken manually as well as the required sample size for each incoming material requiring inspection. Such definition should be done in conjunction with the competent employees in the Industrialization and/or R&D teams. Ensure details and results of the quality inspections are sufficiently documented and traceable.
Ensure equipment owners follow-up on items with overdue calibration. Actual calibration date should be timely updated in Primas.

Reduce the backlog of quarantined materials by adopting a pragmatic approach on materials that can no longer be identified with or traced back to a corresponding quality notification. Ensure all current quarantined materials are processed timely and consequently to prevent recurring non-conformity as much as possible.
Streamline the supplier audit process within the team to ensure consistent reporting and follow up of corrective actions agreed with the suppliers.
Ensure the latest template versions are being used to draft the new contracts. HR should follow up with the line managers to ensure the job description is signed and archived in the personnel file.
Re-emphasize the requirements within the published divisional HR process amongst the HR Business Partners and the HR SSC payroll team. Ensure process compliance in the next salary adjustment round.

Ensure all employees review and sign their job description with their respective line manager. Retain the signed job description in their personnel file. Any subsequent role or responsibility changes should lead to an updated job description being drafted and signed.
Review archive in storage to create at least a high-level overview of what records are in place. Documents with expired retention period should be safely disposed of.
Ensure supporting documentation is retained for each intermediary assessed via the Anticorruption Questionnaire. Red flagged intermediaries should be subject to approval by the Head of BU.

Maintain an overview of all health & safety related costs and investments throughout the year along with invoices and receipts in order to support the figures reported in SIS. Review and understand the difference in definition between investment in (i.e. capitalized on balance sheet) and cost for health & safety. Ensure the number of female and male employees reported match the actual local headcount.
Consult T-Systems and BU Asia IT regarding how to improve the current situation to reduce the potential risks effectively.
Obtain further training regarding COGNOS reporting from BU Controller or Division Controlling in order to ensure correct understanding and entry on required COGNOS forms.
Align with BU Asia Controller on the best way to reduce the payment security risks posted by the current setup.

Apply the required useful life periods on future machines in the rental pool in accordance with Division Instruction 3440. For existing machines with incorrect useful life period, consult with the BU Controller on how to proceed.
Increase efforts on the follow-up of aged open items on the GRIR account and ensure a more timely reconciliation in the future.
Draft and communicate to all employees a local travel and entertainment policy with clearly defined expense limits applicable to Singapore, Vietnam and the Philippines.

Review all stock adjustment postings on a monthly basis to ensure their appropriateness prior to month end closing. 
Calculate the stock shortage ratio on an annual basis to determine whether compensation can be sought from Dachser.
Review all types of invoice backlogs monthly and ensure timely billing.

Request payroll service providers in Vietnam and the Philippines to submit proof of payments to local tax and social security authorities and reconcile the value with the payroll report for each pay period. Moreover, the corresponding ICS point (I.03.05.020) should be updated to reflect this control step.
Ensure that supporting evidences used during the next assessment of intermediaries in 2023 are maintained.

Identify suitable (and prioritized) enhancement projects for the currently uncovered focus areas from GF's Sustainability Framework. Develop a comprehensive target controlling / progress reporting that covers all open, on-going and closed enhancement projects across all focus areas.

Agree with Division and Corporate Controlling on the assumptions and the exact parameters to calculate the provision for employee service awards. Record the calculated provision value accordingly in the financial statements.

For the aforementioned case, discuss with the Division CFO how to proceed (i.e. retrospective correction of the useful life period to the one prescribed in the Division useful life schedule ac-cordingly). Assess the advantages and disadvantages for retrospectively capitalizing identifiable assets (i.e. 9 welding machines) separately.

Derive an annual supplier audit plan from the results of the already existing internal supplier performance assessment. Conduct the selected supplier audits accordingly and communicate the results to the supplier. Ensure that agreed on mitigating actions are tracked and completed timely.

Put in place stock management review and classification procedures in accordance with the methodology prescribed in DI 5896. Perform the review at least twice a year as required by the aforementioned Division Instruction.

Ensure that received goods are posted timely (i.e. on the day they actually arrive). Use the existing quarantine warehouse location in SAP for goods that are subject to quality inspections instead of delaying the goods receipt posting until the quality inspection procedures have been completed.
Implement a process to systematically follow-up on customer quotations (i.e. after a pre-defined period). Assess the possibility to integrate quotations created in SAP with the existing CRM system.

Re-assess the intended use of opportunities in MS CRM 365. Ensure that (strategically) relevant projects will be focused on when creating opportunities in MS CRM 365 and "bulk" or "loose" orders are monitored in the "Quotes" section instead.

Include maintenance slots in the production planning. Consider to change the current production planning to a more illustrated format (i.e. Gantt chart). Assess the possibility to implement a production monitoring application or an alternative way to gain more timely transparency around the production status.
Regularly perform a cost and hours spent analysis on machine level and derive corresponding actions and corrective measures.

Account for spare parts accordingly by applying an agreed on (i.e. with BU, Division and Corpo-rate Controlling) threshold. Consult with the SAP team to assess the possibility of implementing the SAP Preventive Maintenance (SAP PM) module to record and account for spare parts.
Develop a comprehensive exit checklist and ensure a streamlined and documented exit process.

Use a system generated changes report from the existing payroll application during the review of the monthly payroll. Ensure that all reviewers and approvers (including those in the e-banking application) receive a non-modifiable screenshot of the monthly payroll amount (i.e. net amount to be paid) from the payroll application along with their review documentation.

Include introduction to Corporate Instructions and Division Directives in the standard onboard-ing process for new hires. Instruct employees to familiarize themselves with the policies that need to be closely followed during their daily work based on their role. For existing employees, make a one-time communication to highlight the same topic.

Use the GF Agency Agreement template from Corporate Legal to draft and sign a contract with the agent in India including desired commercial terms. Once business with Russia can resume, ensure a written contract is signed with the agent in Russia before conducting further cooperation.

Conduct quarterly review of the remaining items in storage location 1504 regarding their selling potential and adjust the specific provision for them accordingly and in the same period when the analysis is concluded. Review the current file utilized to calculate the stock provision to ensure compliance with Divisional Directive 44 and related Annexes.
Align the provision for employee service awards to the amount calculated by GF Corporate Employee Benefits.

Define the criteria for determining the customer credit limits and ensure that a systematic review is performed by Finance regularly (e.g. annual). Make sure that verification of credit wor-thiness are conducted as systematic assessment for both new customers and existing ones with worsened payment behavior.

Apply asset tags to all existing assets with a net book value in order to link them to the corre-sponding asset recorded in SAP. Implement a regular counting procedure of the PPE and exe-cute the first full counting before the year-end closing of 2022. Make sure that counting results are reviewed by observing the four-eyes principle and communicated to Finance.

Review the CAPEX reporting process and make sure that approvals are documented on latest credit application form physically or through DocuSign. Implement a project repository accessi-ble to Finance that includes the credit application number and deviation against budgeted ex-penses. Ensure that asset capitalization is based on signed asset approval forms.
Re-start the implementation of 5S across the plant. Live by the 5S methodology including regular 5S audits to be conducted in the production clusters.

Recommendation
Define in written agreements with external processors regarding the tolerance level for scrap and shrinkage above which the supplier shall be held liable to compensate GF.
Include all frequently used external processors in the delivery performance monitoring process.

Document in detail the preventive maintenance activities to be performed on key production machinery by consulting the recommendations in the manufacturer manuals. Ensure the de-tailed activities are recorded in HOLIS and confirmed by the maintenance engineer upon com-pletion of each maintenance order.
Involve Corporate Legal to draft and sign a suitable consignment agreement with the customer. Add the approval requirements for customer consignment contracts in the applicable DoA.
Ensure all hourly rates used in the cost calculation template are based on updated SAP data that are calculated based on actual cost center and hours recorded therein.

Ensure minutes are made following each monthly management meeting including the agreed actions. Follow-up of previously agreed actions should be a recurring topic at the subsequent management meetings.
Review the GFCS Divisional DoA and adapt where necessary to formalize a DoA that is applicable for GFCS Arad. Communicate this DoA to all relevant employees in the company and ensure compliance.
Seek guidance from the GFCS ICS Division coordinator and document the internal controls on the ICS SharePoint site.

Verify and obtain access to all Corporate Instructions and Division Directives for relevant employees. Include the introduction to corporate and divisional policies as part of the standard onboarding process. Ensure employees are aware of the applicable policies relevant to their role.
Implement a solution to generate payment proposal lists from SoftPro. Agree with the bank an option for importing the payments list to the e-banking system in a more secure manner.

Analyze the current equipment reported as AUC to verify any potential utilization. Make sure to involve the GFCS Novazzano's management team and GFCS Division Controlling to implement an appropriate action plan and ensure adequate accounting treatment.

Conclude contracts including appropriate liability definitions with external processing suppliers. Drafting of the contracts should be guided by either GF Corporate Legal or an approved external law firm.

Enter required data in SAP to allow fine production planning per work cluster. This shall enable production to foresee potential resource shortages based on future orders and act proactively to plan additional resource.

Centralize the storage of spare parts in the warehouse. Implement a process to ensure consumption of spare part is recorded in real time in the ERP system to ensure a timely re-ordering of spare parts.
Develop a checklist template for the entry and exit procedures. Make sure that such documents are archived in the personnel file of the involved employee(s).

Retain evidence of the reconciliation between the monthly payroll and correspondent salary payments list per headcount. Make sure such review is performed by either Finance or the MD prior to the payment release in the e-banking system.  Document the control in an appropriate format.
Update the internal stock taking procedure to require blind counting during the full inventory counts. Ensure counting sheets do not show the system quantity.

Based on the machine list of the Production department and the fixed asset list provided by the Finance department, identify the machines needed for preventive maintenance. 
Summarize those identified machines in one sheet as a database and keep updating the sheet in case of new machines are purchased, and define the corresponding PM activities and frequencies.
Define PM documentation templates for each machine type, which should be designed to fit the different PM frequencies and with reference to the summary sheet, so that the Operator could easier tick or remark on the completed tasks, thus preventing any negligence.
根据生产部的设备台账和财务部提供的固定资产清单，确认所有需要维保的机器。 
将这些识别的机器总结在一张清单中作为数据库，并在购入新机器的情况下不断更新该清单，并增加定义相应的维保项目和频率。
定义适用于每种机器类型的维保报告模板，这些模板应设计适合不同的维保频率的，并关联到设备清单，以便操作员可以更轻松地勾选或备注已完成的任务，从而防止任何疏忽。

Consult the external Manufacturing Excellence System (MES) supplier to see if repair activities could be recorded and monitored in the system.
If not, record the start and end dates as well as time points on the offline maintenance sheets so that the responsible person could collect the data for accurate monthly operational reporting. Meanwhile, the record template should be systematically filled out including preventive measures such as operational training.
咨询MES的供应商，看看是否可以在系统中记录和监控设备维修活动。
如果没有，在线下维护报告中记录开始和结束日期以及时间点，以便负责人收集数据，每月进行运营报告填列。同时，报告模板中针对措施的填列应进行填写，包括操作培训等预防措施。

Perform a joint meeting among all the concerned departments, e.g., Finance, Production, and Supply Chain (Operation) to clarify the process controls on spare parts management and the corresponding roles and responsibilities. Meanwhile, the database used for tracking, monitoring (safety stock of key spare parts) and stock counting should be streamlined as one and with a unique reference code.
Perform the quarterly count in a disciplined manner, as the general stocktaking on inventories, which should be a blind count based on the reliable tracking list. Meanwhile, deviations should be recorded, and adjustments need to be explained.
召开财务、生产和供应链（运营）等相关部门的联席会议，明确备件管理的流程控制以及相应的角色和职责，同时将用于记录、监控（关键备件安全库存）和盘点的表格统一为一个数据源，并提供唯一的参考代码。
以更有效的方式进行季度盘点，与正常的存货盘点一样，应当遵循盲盘的方法，并基于一个可靠的记录表单。同时，盘点偏差应当按实际记录，并解释原因。

Request the change of Power of Attorney (PoA) to Lingyun Group. 
Reassess the need of keeping the bank account, if yes, request the bank statements or balance from the bank to complete the monthly bank reconciliation process. If not, request approval from Chinaust Group and inform GF Treasury to close the bank account.
提交凌云集团申请将授权委托书（PoA）变更为新的经理。重新评估是否应当注销或保留该银行账户，如果需要保留，则向银行索取银行对账单或余额，以完成每月的银行对账程序。如果不需要保留，请获得亚大集团的审批，并通知GF资金部，关闭该银行账户。

As a general control point, in case of any new non-end-customer, perform the Compliance Questionnaire first before reviewing the contract or commencing business.
According to the guideline, request approvals from the proper authorities based on the reve-nues and the assessment result.
作为一项控制点，如有任何新的非最终客户，在审查合同和开展业务之前先完成合规性问卷评估。
根据该指导方针，实际销售数据和评估结果，向有关负责任申请批准。

Define one lowest pricelist for small customers or customers without credit, so that any pricing according to or higher than the pricelist, no individual quotation or margin approval is needed. 
For those with lower margins or credits granted, perform the quotation and contract review process and document the margin calculation and approvals before the bidding and contract signing process to ensure all contracts are negotiated based on an approved quotation and margin.
为一次性客户或没有授信的小额客户定义一个最低价格表，这样任何等于或高于价格表的报价，都不需要单独的报价和毛利审批。 
对于那些毛利较低或进行授信的客户报价，在投标和合同签署过程之前执行报价和合同审查过程，并记录利润率的计算和批准，以确保所有合同都是根据批准的报价和利润率进行谈判的。

In preparing for the launch of the NCC system, configure the automatic notification controls to ease the manual workload of the local Credit Specialist and increase efficiencies.
Meanwhile, analyze if the total credit assigned by Chinaust Utility is sufficient or not. If not, request special approval on a higher amount next year from Utility Business Segment to better suit the reality in Sichuan Chinaust.
In the upcoming months of 2022, consider performing a credit assessment and granting temporary credit limits to those customers with good AR collection performance, but not included in the 2022 annual credit assessment, so that focus could be efficiently placed on those with credit risks.
在准备启用新的ERP系统NCC时，配置自动通知控制，以减轻当地信贷专家的人工工作量，提高流程效率。同时，分析事业部分配的总信用额度是否足够，如果不够，提供资料并申请特批下一年更高的审批额度，以更好地适应四川亚大销售的实际情况。
在2022年接下来的几个月里，考虑对部分信用记录良好的但尚未纳入2022年年度信用评估的客户授予临时信用限额，以更有效的进行其他重点客户的信用额度控制。

Update the current quality guideline on re-grinded materials to better adapt to the production and quality needs if the corresponding control points are no longer valid.
Record re-grinded materials generation and consumption during daily operations and reinforce review controls by the Production Manager or Shift Leader to ensure the usage of re-grinded materials is eligible and compliant with related quality standards.
如果相应的控制点不再有效，更新当前关于破碎料的质量要求，以最佳满足生产和质量需求。
在日常破碎操作中，记录回用料的产生和消耗，并由生产经理或生产班长进行审查，以确保回收材料的使用是被允许及符合相关质量标准的。

Solve the functional limitations in implementing the new ERP version NCC or at least configure the batch information in the new stock aging list in the NCC version to enable manual stock aging recalculation and effective stock monitoring and optimization.
在实施新的ERP版本NCC时解决现有的功能限制，或者至少在NCC版本的账龄列表中增加显示批次信息，以实现手动库龄调整和有效的库存监控和优化。

Deliver training to production staff on accurate reporting of the unqualified products in the MES system per production order, which could be further used as the base for operational reports.
Implement monthly controls to identify and analyze the quantity differences between the reported defective products in MES and the offline tracking list of re-grinded materials.
If it is confirmed with the Business Segment that no need to record any trial scraps in either the operational report or the MES system, specify the trail waste among the non-conformities in the offline-tracking list. This helps to ensure proper traceability and a review of whether certain amount of trial scrap is justified.
Discuss the possible solutions with the Business Segment management for the piled materials, including quality inspections and special reuse proportion of the aged ones. Promote sales of water products to potential customers in the market, which do not require any specific ratio in reusing the re-grinded materials in products to both protect the margin and solve the storage pressure.
