Define a periodic alignment meeting with project owners about the status of ongoing projects, if uncertainties in the underlying information exist. Ensure to activate and depreciate fixed assets in a timely manner.

Apply asset tags to all existing assets with a net book value in order to link them to the corresponding asset recorded in SAP. Define a periodic asset counting procedure and execute the first full counting before the year-end closing of 2023 with proper supporting documentation. Make sure that counting results are reviewed by observing the four-eyes principle and communicated to the Finance Department.

Establish a periodic review by the Head of the central warehouse on the scrapping conducted by the warehousing team. Collect systematically the scrapping requests from the local Purchasing department and retain them accordingly.
Assess the possibility to implement an approval workflow in SAP, based on the monetary thresholds from the local guideline.

Define a local guideline describing the process of annual of stock counting. Ensure participation of GFMS CSC representative(s) during the annual stock count and retain the evidence from the stock count.

Define thresholds of approval for any consumables stock write-offs (i.e. weekly adjustments and scrapping). Define and implement a process of expiration date monitoring for consumables. Sell consumables that are expired as scrap metal and / or check the possibility to ship them back to the supplier to recover some value.

Assess the possibility to automate KPI calculations. Request Dachser to calculate the KPIs as part of the service agreement. Consider contacting Division Controlling for support on the calculation of defined KPIs and potential simplification on the data collection. In order to ensure accuracy and completeness, define a process for a monthly update on the defined KPIs. To ensure a timely update of the KPIs, appoint a substitute for the current responsible. Discuss and share the results in the monthly management meetings. Derive proper actions based on the results.

Review the Delegation of Authorities on Market Region APAC and align it with the recent Division DoA. Communicate the new Market Region DoA to all companies mapped to this region. Based on this, derive and create local DoAs for all applicable companies, covering essential key competences.

Define a dedicated entry checklist for new employees. Retain the completed checklist in the employees' personnel files. Include the introduction of Corporate and Divisional instructions in the standard onboarding process and entry checklist for new hires. Instruct employees to familiarize themselves with the policies that need to be closely followed during their daily work based on their position. For existing employees, communicate a one-time awareness reminder (i. e. via Email or a local intranet group).

Timely submit the Managing Director's expense reports to the respective line manager to ensure that his approval is available before reimbursement takes place. Include flights booked with entities (lodge) credit card in the monthly expense reports of the employees to ensure a subsequent approval of the trip.

Familiarize the entity with the requirements of CI 1061 regarding permissible gifts and invitations. Consult with GF's Compliance Officer in case of uncertainties of requirements before the organization of a similar event.

Reclassify and transfer the complete pension fund account in line 920 to other financial assets. Consider creating a non-current assets account "securities for the settlement of pension liabilities" based on the example proposed by Corporate Controlling.

Review all existing bank accounts and determine if unused bank accounts can be terminated. Update an overview of all bank accounts and report the updated list to Corporate Treasury for verification and information alignment purpose. Extend the monthly bank reconciliation with the foreign currency bank accounts.

Contact Divisional and Corporate Controlling to determine appropriate assumptions and exact parameters to calculate the provision for employee service awards. Record the calculated provision value in the financial statements accordingly.

Review all existing customers, to identify customers with unlimited credit limits. Calculate the customer credit limits in line with divisional requirements for the non-machine business. If no information about a new customer can be retrieved, consider only using the advanced payment methodology. Ensure that customers set to advanced payments do not receive any credit to avoid deliveries prior to the payment.
Implement a systematic and regular review process of existing credit limits (i. e. annually) for non-machine and machine business. Make sure to populate the updated / calculated credit limits based on the assessment for both new as well as for existing customers in iScala.
Perform a regular (i. e. monthly) margin analysis on service order level taking all cost and rebates into consideration after a service order is completed.
Implement a reliable process ensuring that set-up protocols (iPDFs) are timely submitted to the plants.

Review and analyze all open and pending service orders in iScala to determine which orders can either be closed or invoiced.
Perform a regular (i. e. monthly) review of open service orders to identify invoicing potential. Inform Field Service Engineers about the importance of a timely submission of a signed service order report.

The way ComplianceXpress is implemented should be reconsidered so that the functionalities of a detective daily control can be changed into a preventive and continuous control. Discuss at what stages of a sales process controls should be implemented, e.g., when a quote is created or before a delivery is released, to ensure effective design and operation of ComplianceXpress.

We recommend a redesign of the process to ensure screening covers all relevant fields (e.g. ship-to-addresses), as well as the creation of a detective control to ensure completeness of data transferred to AEB. Discuss with Corporate Compliance what additional information in detail should be considered and if purchasing information should be integrated into checks performed by ComplianceXpress as well.
Management shall entrust an application owner for CX. An infrastructure design for the Compliance Xpress solution shall be outlined.

Update the script to support Traditional Chinese and Japanese characters instead of current manual workarounds, which requires constant maintenance. Evaluate the feasibility of moving from a script solution to a direct SAP connector.

Run a manual screening as soon as possible. Accelerate the implementation of CX and consider freezing exports to sanctioned countries until screening can be performed adequately. Review sales to Syria, Iraq and the Democratic Republic of Congo with Corporate Compliance.

Review and update the current ICS documentation with respect to existing controls and responsibilities. Ensure that these controls are implemented and / or documented consistently and completely as part of the ICS. Execute the detective review control I.03.03.010 monthly or define a new control instead.

Establish processes to systematically identify, collect, report, and reconcile data necessary to fulfill environmental and social reporting requirements in SIS. Check the contractual possibility with BfU, to potentially insource the reporting of environmental KPIs in SIS entirely and reduce the monthly costs for their services accordingly. Try to centralize the reporting of KPIs in SIS to one dedicated person in the organization (i.e. Health & Safety department or Assistant of Managing Director). Ensure the timely definition and documentation of actions related to Corporate and Divisional sustainability expectations. Conduct a proper follow-up on the defined actions.
Discuss and align the current threshold of 30% with Division Controlling and adjust it if necessary. Revise standard cost accordingly with respect to what was agreed upon with Division Controlling.

Define a formal credit limit calculation method in line with Division Directive 22. Consult Divisional Sales Services in case of uncertainties of the calculation methodology.
Ensure that calculated customer credit limits are updated in SAP. Check whether the automatic customer delivery block can be activated in SAP.

Inform department managers on the use of the asset retirement form to ensure a timely asset retirement in SAP in accordance with the DoA. Establish a regular information exchange for asset retirements between department managers and the Finance department.

Implement regular cross-departmental 5S audits or safety walks. Address observations in the daily shopfloor meeting and define responsible employees for corrective actions. Contact the local fire department to determine if the doors to the ventilation system can be access restricted. Inform the external supplier of fire extinguishers about the current status and request a timely review of existent extinguishers and exchange of expired ones.

Identify potential bottlenecks in the process. Regularly follow up with department managers to ensure timely execution of a feasibility study. Hold steering meetings with department manager, the Lean Manager and the workers' council on a regular basis to timely review employee improvement proposals. If suggestions cannot be processed in a timely manner, i.e. complex pro-jects, inform employees of the reason.
Analyze individual cases of employees with high overtime and define clear action plans to reduce balances. Follow up on action plans with line management.

Agree with the workers' council on an updated employer / workers' council agreement to ensure that the MyPD@GF process can take place in 2024. Use the negotiation as an opportunity to roll out MyPD@GF throughout the organization if this is in the interest of the company.
Ensure that the differences identified stock differences approved in accordance with divisional DoA requirements.

Ensure the calculations of social and environmental KPIs are in line with the SIS reporting definitions to prevent wrongful presentation of figures in the future. Establish processes to systematically identify, collect, calculate, report and reconcile data necessary to fulfill environmental reporting requirements in SIS.

An official announcement should be sent to all employees to re-address the topic of information security, specifying that personal laptops are not to be used in any circumstances. 
Establish policies and procedures to ensure systematic backup of locally stored data. Prohibit the use of private workstations on GFMS Market Region China property. Provide the employees with the necessary equipment to conduct the job effectively and efficiently (i.e., engineering laptops)

Reclassify the current 3 machines older than two years to second hand & used machines in the inventory report. This requirement should be made aware to the Finance department to make sure timely update of machine status is performed.

Address observations to the third party provider and define a GFMS China employee for the realization of corrective actions at the facitlity. The process must be re-designed for partially delivered sales orders, when the items are physically delivered to customer. Conduct at dedicated assessment for usability or repair potential of all non-conformity items stored in both warehouse locations and apply for scrapping in a timely manner. Define a frequency and an ageing threshold (i. e. greater three years) above which non-machine items with no movement has to be reviewed for its actual usability. Perform the review accordingly and apply to scrap for identified cases of non-usability. Once the stock has been reduced, consider / check the possibility of reducing the warehouse space with the contracted service provider in QingYang.

It is suggested to summarize the exceptional terms of the contract template of the sales contract template of customer AVICADE and sent to the corresponding approver for proper review and authorization, The authorization can be valid for an agreed period of time and doesn't need to be obtained for each individual case using the same contract template. In case the customers claims the penalty clause, create a proper provision in a timely manner accordingly.

For the aged open orders as of July 2023, a review should be performed and actions should be taken according to the actual status of the service orders. In future, a process to regularly review the open service orders must be established to ensure that service orders are processed in a timely manner.

Secondly, the OS Sales should also establish a centralized tracking of parts returned for part warranty and exchange to ensure an overview of the status. Finally, a reconciliation between the stock balance and the status tracking sheets of parts must be performed on a regular basis with exceptions analyzed and actions defined.

Review the current local guideline on records retention and align it with Corporate Instruction 1026, especially on the identified deficiencies like commercial, financial, and technical documents.

Identify the SAP frequent users and apply for new SAP user accounts whenever necessary.

Reconsider the methodology for calculating the value adjustment for the repairable inventory.

Include a request related to the valuation of the repairable stock as part of the GFMS SAP 2020 Plus Template implementation.

According to the new "Accounting and Reporting Guidance: Employee Benefits" published in July 2023, align with GFMS Division Controlling on the accounting method, standard template, valuation frequency and discount rate to be used for the provision for employee service awards. 
Assess and report the provision accordingly in the HBI and HBII balance sheets and financial statements at the defined frequency.

Revise the benchmark prices, cost savings, and status of each material in the tracking file for the cost optimization initiative.

Define a timeline for signing EHS agreements with all existing preferred suppliers. 
Ask suppliers to sign the EHS agreement in accordance with the agreed timeline.

Revise the current supplier re-evaluation template by adding a definition for scoring on each criterion.

Address observations in the weekly shop floor meeting and define responsible employees for corrective actions. Conduct a refresher training among the warehouse employees on the 5S. 
Check the contents of the first aid kits for completeness and define a list of minimum contents. Document the results of the weekly EHS rounds. 
Assess the possibility to move metal sheets stored outside into a covered area.
Assess the internal and external requirements for the CMM calibration frequency and carry out the calibration tasks accordingly.

The counting sheet and report should be retained and signed by all the participating staff, especially the independent supervisor, of the annual spare part stocktaking, so that proper segregation of duties, blind counting method, and authorized counting adjustments could be assured.

Understand the current set-ups in the SAP Quality Module, ascertain and change the SAP material master data whenever needed before unticking the "skip inspection" and "automatic usage decision" functions to ensure all inspections will be executed as required while the production process will not be impacted due to any unknown stock blocking issues.
After cleaning up the material master data in SAP, move the current offline process to online to ascertain completeness, enforcement, and traceability before and after the data transfer to the GFMS SAP 2020 Plus Template.
In terms of urgent production needs, amend the current IGI policy to specify when incoming inspections could be compromised and with which approvals.

Work with the GFMS SAP Specialist and the local Supply Chain department together to review the SAP configurations of the open NCR cases and identify the reasons why the quality inspec-tions have been skipped, in either material master data or purchasing master data.
After understanding all the root causes, correct the SAP configurations to ensure that all returned materials would go through the inspection check before turning to unrestricted status.
For the identified 115 open NCRs, check the status of the replacement and close those identified as already assembled and inspect those still in stock by the Quality department.
Incorporate instructions in the current guideline of NCR management on how to extract and review the open NCRs regularly (e.g., monthly) to ensure that all the returned materials are duly inspected upon receipt and the corresponding NCRs are closed once qualified.

In order to avoid any cut-off issues between successive phases, the closure message or meeting memo of each gate should be communicated by the R&D department to the concerned departments, including Advanced Purchasing Engineering (APE) and Controlling, in a timely manner so that purchase orders can be placed under the correct internal orders.
Moreover, the Controller should close or at least systematically block the internal orders upon completion to avoid potential posting errors. 
Any exception needs to be clarified and approved before posting the costs into the past phases.

Based on the current skills matrix, define the assessment items and assessment method for respective levels and carry out the assessment accordingly and regularly.
Based on the assessment results of the skill matrix and the desired skills for GFMS BAC, organize training for the concerned employees.

Chinaust Utility Business Segment should work with GFPS and review the current DoA, compare it to the GFPS DoA and form a final version with both sides' consent. 
亚大市政事业部应与 GFPS 合作，重新检查当前的 DoA，将其与 GFPS 自己的DoA 进行比较，并在双方同意的情况下形成最终版本。

Consider digitalizing the pre-approval applications on the OA platform to eliminate paperwork.
In terms of the identified deficiencies, deliver training to the employees of the concerned departments to reinforce compliance within the entertainment expense reimbursement process.
考虑上线 OA 平台上的预先批准，以减少纸质文档。
针对发现的不足，对相关部门的员工进行培训，以加强招待费用报销流程的合规性。

Understand the category definition and data collection method of the aforementioned errors and use the correct methods for 2023 annual SIS data reporting.
Create an overview and reconciliation file for SIS data collection and comparison with available financial or operational data on hand.
In case of any confusion, consult the GFPS SIS responsible person before finalizing the 2023 SIS annual reporting.
理解上述错误的类别定义和数据收集方法，并使用正确的方法进行2023年度SIS数据的报填报。
创建总览和核对的文件，用以收集 SIS 数据并与手头现有的财务或业务数据进行比较。
如有任何不确认的问题，在关闭2023 SIS年报前联系GFPS SIS的负责人进行咨询。

Establish back-up solutions for the server including method and frequency, e.g., purchase another back-up server and locate it remotely in other Chinaust premises, or in a different location within the same premises, and configure the automatic data transfer frequency, e.g. daily.
Schedule periodic checks of the backup solution that is implemented to verify it is running and backing up the company data as expected.
启用服务器数据备份的解决方案，包括备份方法和频率，例如，购买另一个备份服务器，并将其远程放置在亚大的其他场所或同一场所内的不同位置，并配置自动数据传输的频率，例如每天一次。
安排对已实施的备份解决方案进行定期检查，以验证其是否按预期运行并备份公司数据。

Purchase new Windows operating versions with valid supports for the PCs in use, e.g. Windows 10 and Windows 11 to improve IT security.
In case of equipment-related PCs that need to be operated under Windows XP or Windows 7, disable the WIFI connection and only enable internal data transfer.
Purchase anti-virus software that cannot be disabled by employees and centralize PC admin rights by local IT for all software downloads and retrospective monitoring.
为使用中的电脑购买尚在有效支持内的新 Windows 操作版本，如 Windows 10 和 Windows 11，以提升信息安全。
如果设备相关 PC 需要在 Windows XP 或 Windows 7 操作系统下运行，禁用 WIFI 连接外网，仅启用内部网络数据传输。
购买员工无法禁用的杀毒软件，并由本地 IT 部门集中管理电脑的管理员权限，授权下载所有软件并进行追溯监控。

The change of customer classifications should be properly and sufficiently discussed and reviewed by all relevant departments including at least Sales and Finance. Additionally, a formal authorization process should be defined to change the customer classifications calculated by historical transaction data. 
客户信用风险分级的变更应由所有相关部门（至少包括销售和财务部门）进行充分和适当讨论和审查。此外，还应制定正式的审批程序，以更改根据历史交易数据计算出来的客户信用风险分级。

Formalize the processes from request for quotations, vendor selection (price and quality comparison) to contract review and signing in standard Chinaust template on the Office Automation (OA) platform to ensure control effectiveness, data and decision traceability and transparency.
Keep records of the PE prices quoted from multiple vendors, monitor and analyze the price fluctuations as supporting for the purchase timing and quantity. 
In terms of contract signing, two independent signatures from concerned stakeholders should be provided to ensure proper segregation of duties.
在OA平台上使用标准的亚大模板，确定从询价、供应商选择（比质比价）到合同审查和签署的流程，以确保内部控制的有效性、数据和决策的可追溯性和透明度。
记录多个供应商的每次PE报价，监测和分析价格波动，作为采购时间和数量的支持数据。
在合同签署方面，应由合同相关部门签署合同，以确保适当的职责分工。

Purchasing department should reconsider the list of vendors to be evaluated on a regular basis, to ensure that the evaluation itself could provide feedback to the vendors, improve their performance and optimize the next negotiation with the vendors.
Review the current key performance indicator for the quarterly evaluation, in terms of unsuitable items for certain suppliers, remark not applicable instead of giving the lowest score.
Maintain the data source and supporting documents for the evaluation results.
采购部门应重新考虑定期评估的供应商名单，以确保评估本身能够为供应商提供反馈，改进他们的表现，用于更好地与供应商进行下一次谈判。
审查当前季度评估的关键绩效指标，在不适合某些供应商的项目方面，备注适用与否， 而不是直接给出最低分。
记录维护评估使用的数据来源和相关证明文件。

Strictly follow the DoA pricing authorization requirement and retain all the supporting documentation.
严格遵守事业部的定价授权要求，并保留所有证明文件。

The entity should work with the Chinaust Utility Business Segment to update the pricing authorization form and sufficiently consider the impact that agent commissions will have on the estimated gross margin of the sales projects.
公司应与亚大市政事业部合作，更新定价审批表，并充分考虑居间佣金对销售项目毛利率估算的影响。

A detailed calculation methodology should be defined and clearly communicated to both Sales and Finance department to ensure that all quoted sales projects at the same period are comparable.
应制定详细的计算方法，并明确告知销售和财务部门，以确保同一时期的所有销售项目报价具有可比性。

If possible, analyze and resolve the issues for the new manufacturing application Heihu with the service provider and test the data credibility. Then, activate the interface from Heihu to NCC for RM outbounds and bring all the data online in a planned timeframe.
If not feasible for online solutions, perform offline variance analysis and action follow-ups at the level of work order (or at least material type and material grade) in form of the current monthly report, to eliminate manual month-end RM inventory adjustments.
若可行，与黑湖服务提供商一起分析和解决生产系统的问题，并测试数据的可信度。然后，激活黑湖到 NCC 的接口，进行原材料消耗数据的输出，并在计划时间内将所有数据上线。
若线上解决方案不可行，则在工单（或者至少在牌号）层面执行线下原材料消耗差异或者生成废管数量差异的分析和行动跟进，减少月末原材料的盘点差异手工调整。
Implement the CIRB and place the hardcopy binders in the Taipei and Taichung offices. An announcement to all employees should be made to inform them of the purpose and location of the CIRB.

Review and decide whether to continue the cooperation with all existing intermediaries. For the intermediaries with whom GFMS Taiwan will continue cooperation, ensure a fully completed and approved anti-corruption assessment is made and renewed at least once every 3 years. A valid contract must be in place for each intermediary too.

Enquire within the company which non T-Systems managed computers are still in use and include them in the hardware register. Request employees with administrator rights on their T-Systems managed computers to update the list of additional software installed.
Liaise with Division Controlling to find a solution to ensure correct timing of revenue recognition.

Reduce as much as possible the number of manual journal entries made directly by the Financial Controller. Train the accounting team to take over more types of journal entries. Entries whose calculations involve sensitive salary information such as bonus and untaken leave provisions can remain with the Financial Controller but they should be reviewed by the MD before posting.
Implement a documented cash counting before each replenishment request is made. The counting should be performed and signed by 2 employees following the four-eyes principle.
Liaise with iScala support team to disable the functionality of changing sales invoice due dates.

Review customer credit limits to ensure only those approved by COFACE are entered into the ERP system to achieve the back-to-back risk transfer afforded by the credit insurance. Finance should take a more active role in reviewing and suggesting updated credit limits based on historical turnover and payment behaviors.
Enforce blind counting method during the full stock counting process in Taichung.

Adapt the existing process setup to allocate the task of goods receipt posting to the warehouse employee. The warehouse employee should verify the physical materials received against the PO information in the ERP system before posting the goods receipt.

Re-distribute the existing tasks for spare parts and wear parts amongst the 2 existing employees to improve segregation of duties. For example, 1 employee can take the tasks of sales order creation and picking for both spare and wear parts and the other employee performs the billing.

Draft up a standard terms and conditions of sales document including retention of title clause in consultation with the Corporate Legal department, which should be either included in the quotation and sales agreement or referred to therein (e.g. a hyperlink to the standard terms and conditions on the GFMS Taiwan website).
Implement a credit note approval process in accordance with the DoA. Finance should not issue a credit note in the ERP system until the required approval has been obtained.
Download the monthly breakdown of the direct debit from the website of the labor bureau and reconcile the employee contribution value with the locally calculated value deducted from the payroll.

Revise the list of service tools and devices to include information regarding calibration. For items out of calibration, they should be sent to competent suppliers to be calibrated and certified before being released for use.
Implement a formal approval process for free-of-charge delivery of goods and services. Each individual case should be pre-approved in a documented manner according to the DoA.

Ensure sales quotations are made in a written manner and copied to the order processing team. The quotation should be reconciled with the customer PO by order processing before a sales order is created.
The monthly margin review should include a review of all individual sales orders below expected margin.

Establish the process to systematically identify, collect, report and reconcile data necessary to fulfill environmental investments and costs reporting requirements in SIS. Separately report the income generated from external recycling of non-hazardous materials.
Clear responsibilities should be assigned to the Order Processing and the Service departments to ensure a diligent investigation and resolution of all pending items on the order backlog.
Use the R&D NDA template published by Corporate Legal with all external R&D consultants. NDA signing should be established as a standard onboarding process for any R&D consultant.
Define a standard process amongst Finance, Order Processing and Service departments to calculate and communicate the necessary parameters needed for the 2nd year warranty provision.
The Purchasing department should sign framework purchase agreement with all subcontractors using the contract template designed for external processing published by Corporate Legal.

The usage of after-the-fact PRs should be limited as much as possible. The Purchasing department should re-emphasize the requirements to all end user departments regarding the permitted usage of the after-the-fact PRs. When converting the manual PRs to purchase orders, the buyers should pay attention to the PR value and notify the manager of the end user department if the value has exceeded the limited allowed by the Purchasing Policy.

Each month after receiving the production variance report, the Production department should sort out those that have not been investigated using the problem report and follow up on them. An overall responsibility should be established to monitor the articles until the root cause is identified and corrective actions are implemented.

A dedicated R&M management tool is suggested to be considered for the overall process management of repair and maintenance, which is able to directly link the maintenance plan with completed maintenance tasks, therefore provides a straightforward view of the unfinished tasks. A machine should be included in the maintenance plan as soon as it has been installed and suppliers should be proactively contacted for preventive maintenance.

Firstly, the Ticketing Tool should be fully understood by the company to start monitoring and follow up on open tickets. Once a monitoring process is set up, the performance indicator re-garding Ticketing Tool can be established. The open claims from both IssueCat and the Ticket-ing Tool should be regularly reviewed to make sure that both intercompany and external cus-tomers receive feedback in a timely manner. 
Secondly, the QComplaint report should be regularly reviewed by related departments to tackle all quality deficiencies within a reasonable period.

The SQE is suggested to work with Research & Development and possibly SAP Super Users to understand the root cause of the missing master data and consequently the missing inbound inspection strategy for raw materials. After resolving this issue, which prevents the setting up of the inspection strategy, the SQE should continue to check for the inclusion of materials that were previously not included in the SAP inbound inspection control scope.

The Quality department should regularly review the outstanding inspection lots awaiting a usage decision. They should also assess whether the company needs to claim compensation from suppliers when clearing old items for which the supplier invoice has already been paid in full.
The Quality department and Operations team should work together to close the identified process gap to make sure that SAP records of non-conforming parts are updated in a timely manner to be in line with the physical goods movements.

Investigate why the MbO evaluations were closed without the next level manager approvals and how HR in GFMS Biel calculated and paid the bonus to the Flawil based employee without the MbO evaluation being completed at all. For the 2023 cycle, the HR Manager should check in Me@GF that all MbO evaluations are completed and approved by the next level manager before calculating the bonuses.

Review the machines that are classified as inventory regularly (i.e. quarterly) and account for the machines in line with the Division Policy and assess the necessity to capitalize machines. Prepare credit application forms for the machines that require capitalization.
Mitigate currency risk by passing on the cost to the end customer and / or using currency hedg-ing. Consult Division Controlling and Corporate Treasury on how to structure future agreements.
Align internally on what are the key supplier quality issues and how this should be reflected in the supplier evaluation.

Based on the physical stocks at the leased external warehouse and the bills charged by the ex-ternal service supplier, clarify all the physical stocks on hand by material. Identify the needs for further consumption or sales possibilities with documented remarks. Once the ownership of the stocks is clarified and confirmed by the internal parties, document it in the tracking list per materi-al code. Dispose of the stock with no ownership identified to eliminate extra storage costs. Start the re-charging process or ask the external warehouse provider to directly invoice the concerned entities

Update the current stocktaking procedure and specify that inventory differences should be ap-proved based on the absolute value. Restrict postings of inventory differences to only the Head of Logistics and / or Finance and Controlling team. Set a tolerance rate for material-level root cause analysis. Define action plans, responsible stakeholders, and deadlines to resolve the iden-tified issues. Address the issues in a timely and traceable manner. Align with the Division Chief Financial Officer on the optimal channel of being informed about the stock adjustment.

Access rights should be reconfigured in SAP to prevent unauthorized users from changing mate-rial master and other critical master data records. Where access revocation is not possible, a re-view of change log control should be designed and implemented. Alternatively, consider defining one department (i.e. Industrialization) responsible for maintaining material master data changes.

For the reorder point analysis, include the current year's sales forecast in the analysis file in addition to the previous year's annual sales to jointly justify the quantity to be set up in SAP. Meanwhile, once the MRP type of reorder point is determined, adjust the MRP type of the material in SAP. For the lead time analysis, ask for the suppliers’ confirmation or estimate, and conduct the actual delivery time analysis using past data as a reference. Document the lead time determined in the analysis sheet and justify the large gaps in terms of new information received from the market. For the materials analyzed, determine the applicable plant codes. For materials applicable for multiple plant codes, safety stock level and lead time should be reviewed separately and updated in SAP.

Consult Corporate Legal to either draft contracts with the identified vendors with specific con-signment stock related clauses or update the existing GTC attached with the purchase orders sent to vendors.

Based on the results of the weekly meetings with the Sales & Marketing department, modify the planned pro-duction dates in the SAP system to ensure that MRP runs are based on the most up to date in-formation and to avoid any potential material overstocks or shortages.

Redefine work steps for Mill S machines in order to be able to collect meaningful data about the actual hours spent on each work step. Align the work steps between the Time Gate and SAP. Perform an analysis of the differences between the standard hours and the actual hours spent on a quarterly basis and adapt the standard cost where applicable (i.e. for cases where the difference is higher than 10%).

Create an overview of all equipment requiring preventive maintenance and its frequency, and schedule PM activities into the time slots based on the production planning calendar. Mark the completed PM activities in the summary spreadsheet to properly track the deferred or open activi-ties.

Conduct a clean-up exercise on aged quality notifications and close cases that are no longer fea-sible to keep active. Review quality notifications that do not have a team assigned and define a re-sponsible team for quality notification resolution.

Revise open PQI tickets and assess whether the issues are feasible to resolve. Define new reso-lution due dates. Periodically revise the status of the open PQIs and resolve any potential bottle-necks. Establish a periodic meeting with the representatives from sales companies. Collect input from the sales team and define priorities based this information.

Document all supplier audit actions plans in Allegra and embed a reminder notification functionali-ty for the action plans. Create a tracking list of all internal quality audits with an overview of the actions necessary to be completed and store this list in a dedicated folder on a share point. Time-ly address the corrective action requests and retain evidence.
Document agreed on action items and due dates from management meetings (i.e. in an appendix). Ensure that agreed on action items are followed-up during the beginning of the next management meeting.

Develop an overview for all individual sales contracts which have deviations from the General Terms and Conditions for Sales. Once completed, assess the legal, business (i.e. warranties) and financial risk (i.e. revenue recognition timing) and implement corresponding mitigation action(s).

Align the Business Unit Travel & Entertainment Policy with the requirements from Corporate Instruction 1061 and the DoA or document an exception approval granted by GF’s Compliance Officer. Communicate the new requirements to all employees concerned.

Develop a comprehensive Excel overview that indicates which values are reported in SIS vs. which values are not reported to prevent duplicates in reporting. Also implement a corresponding folder structure on the local share drive to collect, compile and consolidate social and environmental relevant data in a single place. Ensure that the audit trail from reported KPIs to supporting evidence (i.e. invoices) is in place.

Consequently use standard incoterms in sales orders and corresponding invoices based on the set of incoterms established by the International Chamber of Commerce. Ensure that GFPS CP Shawnee's GTCs link on invoices is fixed. Implement a process to systematically identify shipments with critical Incoterms shipped before crucial cut-off dates. Ensure that revenue is recognized in the correct accounting period and based on the agreed upon Incoterms.

Consult GFPS Division and discuss how to define warranty cost and cost of quality and how differentiate between both. Ensure that an identifier according to the agreed on definition will be used to classify warranty and quality cases in the SAP QM module. Develop a periodic reporting of incurred warranty claims and costs based on available SAP QM data. Establish a regular information exchange between the Finance and the Quality department to ensure that all eligible incurred warranty costs are considered for the warranty provision calculation. Identify the warranty cost linked to the currently accounted for "special events" warranty provision and exclude them from the "base business" warranty provision calculation. Record the missing base business warranty provision accordingly.
Develop a policy that defines volume (i.e. only below USD 1'000) and / or number of transactions (i.e. maximum 2 per year per customer) criteria for the usage of the one-time customer account.
Perform a systematic review of the credit limits (supported by external credit worthiness reports such as Dun & Bradstreet) at least annually and adapt the credit limits accordingly.
Ensure all travel and entertainment expenses are supported by receipts displaying the nature / type of the expense as well as the tax amount.

Develop and maintain an up-to-date contract overview file, including at a glance view of key criteria (i.e. start date, end date, potential covenants, notice periods, automatic renewal clauses, minimum spend requirements) for all agreement types.
Review the supplier evaluation process input and criteria to accurately reflect reality and align with Strategy 2025 goals.

Analyze the stock difference occurring in cycle counts of recent years in detail and investigate what root causes might result in these discrepancies. Derive suitable measures and implement corresponding correction actions.
Implement a process to systematically track and follow-up on customer quotations (i.e. after a pre-defined period). Add the quotation validity date when submitting a quotation to the customer.

Address the aforementioned observations in the shopfloor or management meeting and define responsible employees for corrective actions. Consider additional CAPEX for the racks in the warehouse to be replaced and the outside storage area to ensure safety and that stored material is protected against the elements.
Document the spare parts management procedure and implement a yearly spare parts count. Capture all spare parts in SAP and setup a barcode and scan gun system for inventory movements going forward.

Have the system generated payroll changes report reviewed by a person not having any rights to change payroll data (i.e. the Managing Director) or adapt the rights in payroll system so that members of the HR department cannot change their own payroll records.

Ensure full compliance and transparency in sales transactions. Sales discounts should only be made to the direct customer and not routed via an unknown third party. The Corporate Compliance Officer should be consulted in case of any exception.

Set up an individual account for each of the employee who actually enters social data in SIS at each plant.
Correct the errors in reporting logic of social KPIs to improve accuracy of data in SIS.

Sales should confirm reasonable contractual agreements with customers in written form, especially with regards to incoterms and timing of billing with regards to both regular parts and tooling. Third party warehouses should be required to provide timely stock transfer information at month end. Revenue recognition should be based on prudent approach, not earlier than the confirmed timing of transfer of control of parts or customer acceptance of sample parts for tooling.

The Finance & Controlling department at GFKU should get in touch with division controlling for another training of D44 to understand how corrections should be performed directly in SAP without manual adjustments. The scope, method and documentation of corrections must be agreed in written form to avoid potential manipulation risks.
GFKU must never change the payment terms/payment days in SAP except for the MTZ invoices. Any exception to this should be subject to approval by the Division CFO.

The financial figures can never be manipulated for reporting. If there is solid justification that certain case can be collected even with long overdue period, the case-by-case revaluation rule can be applied to do an individual revaluation, however, such individual revaluation must be sufficiently supported and approved according to the division directives.
Ensure all entertainment expenses are subject to pre-authorization. Conduct a training on CI 1061 in the company and ensure full compliance therewith.

Firstly, SQE should still perform the on-site inspection and finish the evaluation report according to GF standards. In the report, the evaluation results as well as recommendations should be listed in detail and communicated to the external processor sufficiently with documentation (e.g., signatures). If the inspection result is not pass, it must be officially communicated to Sinotruk to achieve clear separation of quality issues caused by GFKU or the external processor. Where possible, GFKU must make the effort to convince the customer to change the appointed suppliers to GF authorized suppliers.

Firstly, GFKU should check the categorization of the spare parts and correct any errors in SAP; once the categorization is correct, the depreciation of the spare parts should be corrected and booked as SAP-generated at the next closing. If there is indeed a need for GFKU to apply different rules other than the currently depreciation rule described in D46, the authorization from division must be obtained. Moreover, sufficient and solid supporting documents should be provided for different depreciation rates, or any items excluded from the depreciation list.

Finance should calculated the hourly cost rates at the beginning of each year based on actual cost center expenses from the previous year. This information should be provided to R&D, which should then determine the standard selling rates for the year based on a reasonable markup.

Firstly, an approval process should be established up to the General Manager for stock count difference adjustments. Before SAP posting, the documented approval for the General Manager must have already been obtained. Secondly, significant stock count differences or abnormal scenarios must be closely reviewed and analyzed, where necessary, improvement actions should be proposed and communicated to the third-party warehouses.

The Sales department and the Logistics department should work together to sign the agreements with the 10 customers without a consignment agreement in place. 
Considering the underlying risks, the GFCS Division DoA should require consignment stocks to be approved by the Division President in the same way the DoAs of GFPS and GFMS do.
The physical stock count performed or supervised by GFKU must be ensured at least once a year at the year-end closing. For the specific case where GFKU was denied access to the third-party warehouse, communication should take place with the Sinotruk to avoid disputes in the future.

Consider creating an allowance for bad debt for outstanding MTZ invoices as defined in GFCS Directive 22. If there is solid justification that certain receivables (for example the MTZ) can be collected even with long overdue period, the case-by-case revaluation rule can be applied to do an individual revaluation. However, such individual revaluation must be sufficiently supported and approved by the Division CFO according to GFCS Directive 47.

Update CI 1071 to reflect current legal requirements and internal processes. Emphasize the advantages of reporting through the GF Transparency Line for whistleblowing complaints, as it facilitates the protection of the whistleblower by redirecting all communication to a subject matter expert. Ensure appropriate feedback is given to whistleblowers as required by local laws.

CCO to ensure the review of the legal requirements of all countries where the 50-employee threshold has been reached and implement stricter directives where applicable. Assess the applicability of additional local requirements on an annual basis, by having an overview of when the 50-/250- employee threshold is reached.

Implementation of the four-eye principle for the GF Transparency Line (BKMS) with individual access data. Establish an approval workflow in BKMS to ensure a review process for adequate and timely closing (archiving) and deletion of reports.

Establish a clear guidance, such as a decision tree, for the handling of reports of misconduct and non-compliance whistleblowing cases. This guidance should cover the different phases of the process, particularly if, when and who to involve, as internal and external third-party subject matter experts, and adequate forms of escalation.

Leverage the tool to improve internal reporting and ensure timely feedback to the whistleblower. Define a period (e.g., 6 months) for regular review of unclosed cases. Analyze local whistleblowing regulations and other applicable laws such and determine a specific deletion period.

Ensure full traceability of cases through BKMS by uploading all case files to the GF Transparency Line, for cases that were made to the CCO even if a report is made through a different reporting channel. Ensure the documentation of phone calls and other interactions with the whistleblower and other stakeholders in the Transparency Line.

Develop a training (e.g., an e-learning) to ensure that selected employees (e.g., management, HR) are aware of how to handle whistleblowing cases and investigations, how to protect against discrimination and retaliation, and how to ensure the anonymity and integrity of the whistleblowers. Conduct trainings on a recurring basis.

Align with the General Counsel on the recipients of the reported KPIs and a defined frequency (e.g., quarterly, half-yearly). Define the preferred KPIs to be reported on together with the recipients. Align with Corporate Sustainability on which KPIs and documentation are expected from EcoVadis in relation to whistleblowing. Take this into account when selecting KPIs.

Strengthen the communication of the GF Transparency Line in line with best practice (for ex-ample trainings, posters on site in factories, bi-annual communication in We@GF), including information on what is considered misconduct. CCO to ensure that employees are informed respectively and, on the ability, to report misconduct externally (as required by applicable laws).
Management shall integrate Confluence requests into Service Now. The application interal requests will be redirected to the GF Confluence team.

Management shall implement a system to document and track all exchanges and agreements related to the change process. The quotations received from the vendor should include estimations in man-days or other relevant metrics. The maintenance contract with the supplier should clearly state the daily or hourly cost associated with the activities provided.
Management shall improve the CMDB management, particularly in terms of accurately capturing and maintaining server information, including their respective locations.

Management shall rectify the malfunctioning cookie banner to ensure that cookie identifiers are not stored prior to obtaining explicit visitor consent. Additionally, the website should be reviewed the number of cookies reduced to only those that are necessary for proper website operation. Consent mechanisms should be implemented for the non-essential cookies, and compliance with data transfer regulations should be ensured.
Management shall re-consider the ticket reopening option and\or review the resolution mail notification to the business users.
Management shall implement a process to track and monitor requests received by the IT coordinators.

Management shall develop a robust Disaster Recovery Plan that encompasses all necessary elements for effective recovery in the event of a disruption or disaster. This includes defining recovery requirements for business applications, establishing clear criteria for invoking the DR plan, documenting detailed recovery procedures, and ensuring secure and appropriate storage of data backups.

Management shall take prompt action to address the detected vulnerabilities. 

One possible solution to improve the situation is to explore the option of updating servers through a centralized distribution system. 

This would streamline the patching process and help ensure that updates are consistently and efficiently applied across all servers.

Management shall improve the AD management and ensure data accuracy. It is recommended to:

Align the AD structure with the organizational structure, ensuring that OUs accurately represent areas, countries, and locations.

Establish a dedicated OU for disabled accounts to facilitate better organization and management.

Address the incomplete or missing information in user accounts, such as department, job title, and office fields, to maintain accurate user profiles.

By implementing these measures, GFIA can enhance the integrity and organization of the AD, streamline management processes, and ensure accurate user data for Epe accounts.

Management shall promptly uninstall the identified unnecessary Microsoft applications from the workstations to ensure a secure and optimized production environment. 

This process should be carried out in coordination with the IT team, adhering to established change management procedures and ensuring minimal disruption to production operations.

Management shall configure the network profiles to disallow simultaneous wired and wireless connections. 

This can be done by setting policies or configurations that enforce the use of only one network connection at a time.

Management shall implement a Network Access Control system to enforce security policies and validate the health and compliance of devices before granting them access to the corporate network. 

Additionally it is recommended to implement a device registration process where private devices need to be registered and authorized before accessing the corporate network. 

This can involve the installation of a security certificate or the use of device-specific credentials for authentication.

Management shall maintain locked the server room itself or install an access control system for to ensure proper security. 

Flammable materials should be removed immediately to minimize the risk of ignition and ensure compliance with safety regulations. 

Additionally, the network cabling colors should adhere to the GF Infrastructure Standard color definition. 

Following a standardized color scheme helps in identifying and troubleshooting network connections quickly and accurately. 

Decommissioned servers should be removed from the server room, and disposed of or repurposed according to appropriate IT asset management procedures.

Management shall enforce the lock and key policy for the electrical panels in the office area, ensuring that panels remain securely closed at all times. 

Additionally, the responsible personnel should be reminded of the importance of keeping keys secured and not left unattended. 

Cables and unused extension cords should be removed from the floor of the electrical cabinet, adhering to the ban on storing such materials in the area. 

A lock should be installed on the production area's electrical cabinet that contains circuits with high voltage hazards.

Management shall isolate the production network from the corporate network. 

This can be achieved by implementing appropriate network segmentation, firewalls, and access controls. 

Internet access for administrators and production devices should be restricted.
Management shall change the system passwords following the complexity criteria explained in the GF password guidelines.
Management shall inform the vendor regarding the relevant misconfiguration, initiate the process to fix them and obtain a confirmation upon the final resolution.

Management shall update the BCS password policy to align with industry best practices.

Passwords should meet the GF Password Guideline, requiring a minimum length of ten characters, including a combination of upper and lower case letters, as well as at least one number. 

A password expiration policy should be implemented to ensure that passwords are regularly updated and prevent the use of outdated or compromised passwords.

The second authentication factor should be enabled as a mandatory requirement for accessing the HR system. 

This will provide an additional layer of security and reduce the risk associated with weak passwords.

Management shall install the USB HD webcams with autofocus on each workstation, positioning them on top of the main monitor as specified in the standard. Additionally the two workstations that lack the required two monitors should be upgraded.

Management shall ensure that the finalized version of the ISMS documents is released and effectively communicated and disseminated to all relevant entities within the GF organization. This includes conducting training and awareness programs to ensure that employees understand and adhere to the policies and procedures outlined in the ISMS. Regular reviews and updates to the documents should also be implemented to reflect changing organizational needs and evolving security threats.

Management shall implement secure printing measures in the HR department cto mitigate the risks associated with the handling and printing of sensitive documents, protect employee privacy, comply with regulations, and maintain the confidentiality and integrity of valuable information.

Management shall implement a comprehensive user authorization process within the GF ticketing system, ServiceNow. This will enable the proper recording and tracking of user authorization requests, modifications, and revocations. Additionally, it will enhance accountability, transparency, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

Management shall add the unrecorded devices to the Shawnee server asset list and integrated into the GF Active Directory, following the established IT Infrastructure Standards. Legacy servers that are still active should be recorded in the GF Active Directory for proper tracking and management or decommissioned. The new servers, once activated, should be promptly recorded in the asset list to ensure comprehensive tracking and control. Supporting devices should also be appropriately documented and managed.

Management shall develop a robust Disaster Recovery Plan that encompasses all necessary elements for effective recovery in the event of a disruption or disaster. This includes defining recovery requirements for business applications, establishing clear criteria for invoking the DRP, documenting detailed recovery procedures, and ensuring secure and appropriate storage of data backups.

Enforce proper access controls, implement strong authentication mechanisms, regularly monitor and audit access to backup files, and encrypt sensitive data to maintain its confidentiality and integrity.

Management shall reconfigure the network setup to ensure that the CCTV cameras are not directly connected to the office network and are instead connected to the dedicated network established for the CCTV systems. This segregation prevents potential security breaches and unauthorized access to the office network through the cameras.
It is advisable to enforce secure communication protocols such as Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) to encrypt the data transmitted between the administrative interface and the cameras. This ensures the confidentiality and integrity of administrative interactions.

Management shall disable the unauthorized Wi-Fi network using only the networks provided by Corporate GF. Additionally, employee awareness and education on the risks of connecting to non-standard Wi-Fi networks should be emphasized to promote responsible network usage within the corporate environment.

Management shall ensure that all switch cabinets are securely locked and accessible only to authorized personnel. Regular inspections, monitoring, and adherence to physical security policies must be enforced to maintain the integrity and security of the production network.

Management shall take prompt action to address the detected vulnerabilities. The servers running outdated and unsupported operating systems (OS) in the local IT scope should be decommis-sioned immediately. All missing application and OS patches should be promptly installed on the servers. These patches are crucial for addressing vulnerabilities and improving the overall securi-ty of the systems. A comprehensive review of the installed software should be performed to de-termine if they are truly necessary for the functioning of the servers. Unnecessary software should be removed to reduce the attack surface and minimize potential vulnerabilities.

Corporate IT shall investigate with the T-system supplier the reason why the corporate managed servers were not replaced before the Operating systems became obsolete and take corrective actions. The servers running outdated and unsupported operating systems (OS) in the Corporate IT scope should be decom-missioned immediately. These obsolete systems pose significant security risks and should be replaced with supported and updated OS versions.

Management shall improve the AD management and ensure data accuracy. It is recommended to:
- Establish a dedicated AD Organizational Unit for disabled accounts to facilitate better organization and management.
- Address the incomplete or missing information in user accounts, such as department, job title, and office fields, to maintain accurate user profiles.
By implementing these measures, GFIA can enhance the integrity and organization of the AD, streamline management processes, and ensure accurate user data for Shawnee accounts.

Management shall take immediate action and address these default credential vulnerabilities by changing the default passwords to unique and strong ones for each peripheral device following the complexity criteria explained in the GF password guidelines.

Management shall implement a MAM system on the Shawnee mobile phones to enhance security measures and ensure proper management of mobile applications. This would provide centralized control, improved security policies, and enforce the necessary restrictions to protect corporate data and prevent unauthorized access or data leakage.

Management shall update or replace the unmanaged workstations with standard ones under the IT department responsibilities. Admin rights for operators should be removed and a robust IT management system should be in place for all the production workstations, including regular updates, security patches, access control policies, and monitoring mechanisms to ensure the security and stability of the production environment.

Management should update the ADP password rules to align with industry best practices.
- Passwords should meet the GF Password Guideline, requiring a minimum length of ten characters, including a combination of upper and lower case letters, as well as at least one number.
- The second authentication factor should be enabled as a mandatory requirement for accessing the HR system. This will provide an additional layer of security and reduce the risk associated with weak passwords.
- The vendor should be notified about the security misconfiguration and the absence of a cookie banner on the website.

Management should ensure that each workstation, including those in open spaces, is equipped with the required USB HD webcams. These webcams should be positioned on top of the main monitor as specified in the standard. The workstation that lacks the required two monitors should be upgraded to meet the standard's specifications.

Consider creating an identifier in the ERP system that supports distinguishing an intermediary account from other types of accounts. An agent account should have an identifier that distinguishes it from other supplier accounts. This identifier should be used when compiling the complete overview of intermediaries.
Ensure that supporting evidences used during the next assessment of intermediaries in 2023 are maintained.
Review the current intermediary tracking for data consistency and update inconsistent or missing information such as the signing dates of compliance annexes for timely renewal. Reinforce the importance of singing Compliance Agreements for high-risk intermediaries. In case of intermediaries' reluctance to sign Compliance Agreements, address this topic with GF's Compliance Officer to find a suitable solution.

Update the now existing overview file with the information about which ISO certificates are in place and valid. Depending on the results, ensure that all suppliers have local permits and are ISO certified or select alternative waste and recycling suppliers.

Implement a documented review process for statutory (in IFS) manual journal entries which ensures compliance with the 4-eyes-principle. Reconcile the received local GAAP trial balance Excel file with an unalterable supporting document, such as a screen shot or a PDF version of the local GAAP trial balance from IFS, before proceeding with the Swiss GAAP FER (HB2) compilation.

Calculate the customer cashback accrual regularly (i.e. at least annually) and account for it ac-cordingly. Determine whether late payment interest apply for the aged customer cash back ob-ligations from 2020, 2021 and 2022.
Determine the portion of currently reserved accounts receivable covered by letters of credit or bank guarantees regularly and exclude them from the bad debt provision.

Identify the gaps between the local GAAP and Division useful lifetime schedule. Ensure that selected useful life periods are in line with the Division useful lifetime schedule (7756) for Cognos reporting purposes. Document the selected useful life period by referencing it to the respective useful life period selected from Division Instruction 7756.

Instruct and train counting teams about the requirement to count every article in the warehouse annually. Share the stock taking guidelines from Corporate Controlling with the counting employees. Define a proper monetary and quantity threshold in the local stock taking guideline to trigger a recount in case of counting differences. Ensure that identified stock differences are approved and properly documented in accordance with DoA requirements.

Review all current batch sizes systematically and consider applying the Andler formular to determine accurate batch sizes. In case of uncertainties, contact Divisional Operational Excellence team for support. Ensure that the determined batch sizes, documented in IFS, are applied in work orders accordingly.

After a Kaizen project’s implementation, assess the achieved savings with supporting evidence. Implement a 4-eyes-principle review, independent of the Supply Chain and Operations Director, before submitting savings into "Spex".

Define individual cost centers for each machine and equipment in IFS to ensure a proper and precise cost allocation for spare parts and repair, and maintenance activities. Perform an over-all cost and hours spent analysis on a machine and equipment level. Derive appropriate actions, such as determining whether purchasing a new machine is the better economic option.

Train production employees on how to handle out of tolerance measurement results. Ensure that all defined quality controls, as defined in the corresponding quality control plan, are executed, and documented accordingly. Establish a regular information exchange of the results be-tween the Production, Quality and R&D department to adjust the quality control plans as well as the BoMs accordingly.
Use the already existing payroll master data changes report form the local payroll application and have it reviewed by the HR Director or the (ad interim) Managing Director consequently.
Define and train deputies for all key first line management positions in the company. Document the deputies of the key positions in writing, e.g. on the organizational chart.

Review the controls in the ICS and update the description and control frequency. Controls that are not relevant for the business activities of the entity should be removed. Assign a responsi-ble employee for each applicable control. In case an employee leaves the company, timely replace the control responsible in the ICS and communicate the change to the new control owner.

Create a local records retention policy. Ensure compliance with the local regulation and re-quirements from Corporate Instruction 1026. Arrange destruction of the hard-copy and digital records that exceed the retention period.

Communicate, discuss and analyze the identified pending legal cases with GF's Legal depart-ment as prescribed by Corporate Instruction 1070 and follow agreed next steps. Use the en-gagement letter template published by Corporate Legal (Corporate Instruction 1070 Annex 2) in future engagements with external lawyers.

Use annual spending as a key criterion to prioritize signing and acceptance of GF’s Code for Business Partners by suppliers. Align with the Market Region and Division management on sufficiency of assessing a vendor’s Code of Conduct in cases where suppliers refuse to sign GF’s Code for Business Partners.

Upon the renewal of lease contracts for non-EVs, prepare a form and justify the vehicle selec-tion. Inform the Division CFO, the Division Head of Human Resources and the Division Head of Sustainability. Discuss with the Division Head of Quality and Sustainability alternative means of documentation if a case-by-case form submission is not practical. Define a roadmap on GFMS France's transition to the EVs.
Implement a corresponding accounting process (i.e. revenue deferral) for the Privilege Club loyalty program. Inform the customers about the terms & conditions of the program.

Align with the Service + Success department on the percentage of product failures in the first year. Update the agreed percentage in Board template and calculate the accurate amount. Up-date the percentages of products sold with 2nd year warranty in the current year and previous year accordingly in Board.

Conduct a refresher training on GF Machining Solutions credit policy handling. Establish a pro-cess of monitoring customers credit exposure and for the cases where the exposure exceeds T EUR 50 obtain the approval from the Business Unit or Division Management.

Redesign the bad debt calculation file by adding a column with the amount of receivables in-sured by Coface and / or leasing arrangement. In case receivables are not insured, exceed insurance limits or the company loses the right to file a claim with the insurer. Apply 50% and 100% ratios for receivables aged over 90 and 180 days respectively. Discontinue using the reduction of 50% due to the longevity of equipment repossession process in France.

Conduct a refresher training on the Division DoA requirements within the Finance department. Introduce a formal and documented process for receivable write-offs. Retain evidence of approval, consultation and information from the BU and Division Management.

Develop an internal stock taking guideline. Include all inventories held by GFMS France in the annual stock take. Document the results of the stock taking, investigate the root causes of the differences identified and derive suitable actions and timely follow-up on them. Provide a sum-mary of the stock count results for review and approval to the first line management. Revoke the access right to record stock adjustments in SAP from the Logistics Manager or Logistics Manager Assistant. Any adjustment of the stock in SAP has to be well documented, justified and approved.

Prior to the physical and accounting scrapping of equipment, justify the reason for scrapping and obtain an approval from the Managing Director. Assess the possibility to establish an automatic workflow for scrapping based on the monetary thresholds in SAP.

Remind ENSAM’s management about the need to sign the agreement. Obtain the insurance certificates for the machines stored at ENSAM. In case EMSAM refuses to sign the agreement, either reconsider the relationship with the partner or insure the machine with own funds.

Use the template for long-service award published on Corporate Controlling’s page (We@GF) to calculate the provision for employee service awards. Record the calculated provision value in the financial statements accordingly.

Remove ADP admin rights for the Managing Director and identify a person from the Division HR department who would be a suitable candidate to have an administrative right to change the master data in ADP’s portal. Alternatively, submit MD’s payroll calculation for a review to the Head of Market Region Europe & South America.

In cooperation with the GFMS SAP team clean up the open service orders and tickets. Define a process of weekly follow up for the service interventions. Timely follow up and close open ser-vice orders to ensure a proper invoicing process in a timely manner. In case of capacity re-strains define a rule to prioritize follow up on open service orders (e.g. based on value of order or importance of customer).

Define a process of periodic evaluation of skill levels for FSEs and retrofit workshop techni-cians. For the latter, develop a skill matrix with all necessary knowledge areas applicable to GFMS technologies.

Assign a responsible for tracking tools and equipment used in the process of machine installa-tion and repair. Identify all the equipment available and its location. Update the last date of cali-bration and periodically review the file for overdue tools. Calibrate equipment that is overdue.

Until the implementation of the electronic invoice exchange system, request email addressed from the customers with most frequent sales. Update the email addresses in the customer mas-ter data. Send invoices via email to customers. Create a tracking file of all invoices dispatched via post. Conduct a periodic reconciliation between the tracking file and invoices generated in SAP.
The Corporate Tax department should assess, if the future usage of the group taxation in France is optimal given the existent credit notes mechanism and operating expenses of GFH France.

The local Financial Controller should revise the tax loss carryforwards and correct the reported figures in the KA24 form. Consider recharging the costs incurred by GFH France to GFMS and GFPS France.
Align the local DoA with the current divisional version, including format and responsibilities, and adapt to current business responsibilities. Communicate the updated version to local employees.

Develop and maintain an up-to-date contract overview file, including an at-a-glance view of key criteria (i.e. start date, end date, potential covenants, notice periods, automatic renewal clauses, minimum spend requirements) for all agreement types.
Ensure that changes to agent commissions are documented. Investigate whether management of commissions directly in SAP would result in higher process efficiency and consistency.
Ensure compliance with CI 1079 by informing the Divisional CFO, Divisional Head of Human Resources, and the Divisional Head of Sustainability prior to a combustion engine vehicle lease.
Include the off-site manufacturing workshop operations in the next ISO 9001 renewal. Prepare and schedule certification for ISO 14001 and 45001.

Reconcile petty cash amounts with SAP GLs periodically and investigate reconciling differences. Ensure travel advances do not become de-facto employee loans. Consider moving towards GF corporate credit card model.
Apply asset tags to all fixed assets. Perform a full count to ensure all assets are recorded in SAP and implement a periodic counting procedure.

Review stock taking procedure options and select the most suitable one for the nature and extent of GFPS Spain's off-site manufacturing workshop (i.e. full stock count). Perform the selected stock taking procedures accordingly. Assess the implementation of scan guns to ensure ease of usage and traceability of movements in SAP.

Define a materiality threshold and monitor actual costs incurred. Use actual costs as a base for future project cost forecasts. Implement a unique identifier system that would allow for easy traceability of all off-site manufacturing projects. Consider a single storage location for all project related documentation.
Conduct a complete review of the measurement and calibration tools in the Fab Shop. Identify and monitor the calibration due dates for each. Tag the tools for easy identification and monitoring.

With the implementation of a new risk management tool in 2024, reassess the current risk exposure of GFPS Fluorpolymer in detail and extend and update the existent risk portfolio with current risks and define proper mitigating actions
Once the Divisional- and Business Unit DoA have been updated and released in Q1 2024, align, and update the BU DoA and derive an aligned local DoA from it.
Ensure the calculations of social and environmental KPIs are in line with the SIS reporting manual and supporting evidence are filed accordingly.

Derive appropriate production labor and machinery tariffs from the budgeted costs for machinery and personnel on cost center level in line with DI 4724. Update the consolidated data in SAP. Perform the standard cost and price calculation in SAP twice a year in accordance with DI 4724. Consult Division Controlling to ensure a correct execution of the standard cost calculation for 2024 in case of uncertainties.

Assess the local tax requirements to register supplier invoices for claiming the VAT receivable. Based on the gained information, tailor a local GR/IR account handling guideline and define a GR/IR account review frequency. Consider contacting Division Controlling in case of uncertain-ties. Conduct a review of all line items in the GR/IR account and release aged items in accordance with local requirements.
Define a periodic alignment meeting with project owners about the status of ongoing projects. Ensure the timely activation and depreciation of fixed assets.
Assess and review the actual cycle times in the finishing and packaging department. Based on the result, update the cycle times accordingly to ensure a more precise standard cost calculation.

Implement and define a timesheet tacking file for maintenance and repair activities on production machinery and equipment level. Track the times required for each repair and maintenance activity. Perform an overall cost and hours spend analysis on machine level. Utilize accumulated repair and maintenance cost to proactively decide on purchasing new machines or maintaining existing ones in the future. Define an annual maintenance plan for all production machinery and equipment. Involve local production planning in the preparation of the annual maintenance plan to ensure transparency and availability of production machinery and equipment.

Create an overview of spare parts and define a dedicated access restricted spare parts storage location in line with DI 9049. With the selection of a software in 2024, integrate the overview of spare parts and classify them accordingly.

Create an internal quality audit plan and appoint and train independent auditors, who are not directly responsible for the system. Define the audit scope and frequency for internal process audits. Summarize internal audits in dedicated reports with actions, responsibles and deadlines for the auditees. Share the reports with the auditees in a timely manner. Conduct a proper follow-up on actions for identified observations.

Establish a multi-year monitoring list detailing the audit results, corrective actions, due dates and status (i.e. resolved / open) to provide an at a glance-overview of open items. Ensure a proper and timely follow-up of agreed corrective actions. 
Maintain an overview of open supplier non-conformity reports. Follow-up on the reports within a dedicated timeframe to ensure reports are filled out and corrective actions are implemented by the supplier.

Review existing anticorruption questionnaires completed for intermediaries and make sure all those completed more than 3 years ago are renewed. Questionnaires showing red flags should be escalated to the BU Head for approval.
Renew the distributorship agreements with the intermediaries using the latest contract template published by GF Corporate Legal.

Ensure a summary worksheet is maintained for each key figure reported in SIS, which provides a trace record that allows efficient reconciliation. Documents that support the individual figures kept in the summary worksheet should be saved in designated folders that allow easy searching.
Implement and document a review process of manual journal entries that adheres to the 4-eyes principle. Ensure the ICS control description reflects actual practice.

Establish a process to record all warranty costs incurred including both replacement product costs and credit notes issued as compensation. The resulting warranty cost should be used as one of the input values in the calculation formula prescribed in DI 3422 to derive the appropriate warranty provision. Provisions related to specific business events should be pre-approved by the Division CFO in accordance with DI 3422.

Work together with the Purchasing team to increase efforts on the follow-up of open items on the GR/IR account. Retain follow-up comments and justifications on items aged above 3 months if not cleared.
