Ensure all entertainment expenses above EUR 100 per person are subject to documented pre-approval. Such pre-approval should be attached to the expense claim as supporting document. Names of the participants of entertainment events should be individually listed in the expense tool. The line manager and the Finance department should review such information prior to approving and releasing the expense claim for reimbursement.

Draft and sign frame contracts with all key suppliers and subcontractors that address the inherent business risks based on the model of cooperation. Contract templates published by Corporate Legal should be used and where applicable, Corporate Legal should be consulted according to criteria defined in the Corporate Instruction 1070, e.g. when suppliers wish to change standard clauses in the contract templates.
Review companywide the types of purchases being made without using SAP POs. Centralize such purchasing activities as much as possible to the Purchasing team and via SAP issued POs.
Investigate and resolve the materials with different lead times in PIR and MRP2. Perform a quarterly monitoring in order to identify and resolve such issues going forward.
Review scrap costs on a quarterly basis to identify unexpected transactions and investigate for root cause.

Investigate potential adaptations to the payroll process to achieve preventive control on payroll payments. For example, request the BCS to only re-open payroll based on request from the FC or the MD.

Include introduction to Corporate Instructions in the standard onboarding process for new hires. Instruct employees to familiarize themselves with the policies that need to be closely followed during their daily work based on their role. For existing employees, make a one-time communication to highlight the same topic.

Conduct the anticorruption assessment on the 2 intermediaries in Costa Rica. In case of red flags, the assessment should be approved by the BU Head. For any future cooperation with intermediaries, even for one-off deals, the same requirements of CI 1061 have to be followed.

With the support of the GFMS US team, develop a monthly, automated download and matching process of customer and supplier master data against the sanction lists in AEB Compliance Xpress. Ensure that identified matches are resolved timely.

Consult GFMS US and implement the stock provision calculation process in the ERP system for slow moving consumables in accordance with the GFMS Stock Directive. Book the calculated provision on a monthly basis.
Review the unmatched items on the control account to clarify and resolve aged items above 3 months regularly.
Review vendor master data and ensure the payment term is maintained according to actual agreement with each vendor.

Strictly adhere to credit risk management processes of the division for all future sales transactions. COFACE insurance must be applied for in accordance with the insurance policy and the STAR request must be submitted prior to quoting out-of-policy credit and payment terms to customers.
Consult Corporate Legal for an appropriate approach to mitigate such risks from future sales. Consider using specific contract for machine sales.

Implement a credit note pre-approval process. Credit notes issued in the ERP should be reviewed and approved by the MD prior to the "stamping" process in the Mexican tax system in order to timely detect and prevent any potential mistakes.

Liaise with GFMS US to adapt the GFMS Mexico Great Plains client to be suitable for creating quotations directly in the ERP. A complete list of all quotations issued should be available as the basis to perform follow-up on open quotations.
Meanwhile, adapt the wording regarding the estimated nature of the quoted hours on the quotation template for service interventions.
Consult the consumables specialist in GFMS in implementing a suitable material surcharge process in the sales of wires.

Review existing anticorruption questionnaires completed for intermediaries and make sure all those conducted more than 3 years ago are renewed. Questionnaires showing red flags should be escalated to the BU Head for approval. Follow up with the remaining intermediaries to have them sign the GF Compliance Agreement.
GFPS Business Unit Americas should take the lead in liaising with Corporate Tax to clarify the current setup and related risks. Implement a solution that mitigates the potential risk exposures.
Draft and sign private label agreements based on the contract template published by GF Corporate Legal.
Follow the DI 3422 to calculate the appropriate warranty provision. Seek approval from the BU Controller prior to booking the provision.

Ensure the overdue AR figures reported on KA31 form reflect the true figures in the local ERP system, both in absolute values and ageing structure. Customer invoice due dates should not be changed to reduce the overdue ageing of the invoices. Individual exclusions of overdue invoices from bad debt provision cannot be made unless prior documented approval has been obtained from the BU Controller in accordance with DI 3432.

Review and update planned delivery lead-times in both the MRP2 and PIR for all materials. Consult the Supply Chain experts in GFPS if required. The root cause for the non-inclusion of certain materials from the MRP run should be identified and resolved for all materials to prevent recurring issues.

Implement a process to monitor sales orders with margin anomalies based on internally defined margin tolerances. Sales orders with manually modified prices should be prioritized in this review. Reasons for the margin anomalies should be documented as part of this review process.

Ensure the process for following up on open sales quotations include those from previous months until they have been either closed or converted to sales orders.
Update the SAP quotation and order confirmation templates to enclose or include reference to the GFPS general terms & conditions of sale on the GFPS website.
Use the SAP transaction YRSD0004 Backorder Handling List to monitor and review aged backorders. Individual reasons should be documented as part of this review.
Implement a backup solution supported by BU Americas. Ensure the backup support is sufficiently equipped and trained to handle the process in case the Financial Controller is absent.
Revise the local DoA and align it to the BU EMEA DoA. Share the local DoA with the employees of GFPS Austria.

Update the local records retention policy to ensure compliance with requirements from Corpo-rate Instruction 1026. Communicate the updated local records retention guideline to applicable employees of GFPS Austria.

Organize a refresher training on CI 1061 among GFPS Austria's employees. Establish a pro-cess of documenting and storing evidence of any exceptions obtained for gifts to customers and invitations of customers to events / meals that are not in line with CI 1061. Produce a local expense regulation that would describe travel rules and reasonability of the expenses. Any permanent deviations from CI 1061 must be approved by the Chief Compliance Officer and retained for audit purposes.

Finalize CI 1061 requirements using the risk-based approach defined for the BU EMEA. For Annex 1, complete Anticorruption Questionnaire and obtain approval from Head of BU EMEA where required. Ask intermediaries to sign Annex 2 or obtain and assess the intermediary’s code of conduct. If none of the options is feasible, obtain a written approval from the Head of BU EMEA exception. Sign a distributorship agreement in line with GF’s template. In case the intermediary refuses to sign the agreement, contact Corporate Compliance Officer and / or Le-gal Department to assess possibility to use general terms and conditions instead.
Create a calculation and tracking file for the number of employees and FTEs in the company. Use the file as basis for SIS reporting of HC, FTEs and performed workdays.
Implement and document a review process of manual journal entries that complies with the 4-eyes principle.
Formalize a monthly bank reconciliation and define a responsible employee, who is not involved in payments, to review the reconciliation. Retain the evidence of the review.
Re-assess the need for a warranty provision by complying with the requirements from Division Instruction 3422. In case of missing evidence for currently T EUR 61, release them accordingly.

Upon the annual bonus payout, revise the amounts that were over-accrued and release unnec-essary bonus provision. Accrue for the bonus on a monthly basis using the 1/12 increment from the previous year bonus until more accurate calculations are possible to be produced. Periodi-cally revise the bonus provision in relation to GFPS Austria’s and Business Unit EMEA perfor-mance. Redefine the process of overtime and unused vacation accrual calculations for the overtime and unused vacation so that accurate numbers are reported at the half-year reporting.
Remind employees of current DoA approval thresholds. Define a process where no credit note will be issued unless a prior written approval is available as per DoA.
Define targeted actions for employees with very high unused vacation balances. Encourage employees to avoid accumulation of high unused vacation balances and take vacation throughout the year.

Define a template with the list of questions for the exit interviews. Document the outcomes of the exit interview, define action plans, and implement these, where applicable. Align the process of exit interviews with the BU EMEA’s Human Resources Department.
Develop rules how to treat (customer) gifts which are in line with the requirements from Corporate Instruction 1061. Communicate the new requirements to all employees concerned.
Define and formalize measurable targets for the already ongoing sustainability initiatives. Track and assess their performance and derive lessons learned and future improvement potential.
Implement a documented 4-eyes-principle compliant process for the preparation and the review of monthly bank reconciliation.

Consider stopping the usage of the one-time customer accounts or reduce the current number of one-time customer accounts. Ensure that one-time customer details (i.e. name) are entered consequently in the corresponding SAP field. Periodically (i.e. semi-annually) analyze the one-time customer account(s) for compliance with the local policy.
Define suitable customer complaint KPIs and develop a periodic reporting based on available SAP QM data.

Consult with the Corporate Compliance Officer regarding the appropriateness of providing the gifts and entertainment to the relevant recipients. 
Reinforce the training on the CI 1061 to concerned employees and request appropriate approv-al for upcoming T&E reimbursements.

Establish a systematic approach to identify, collect and report sustainability figures necessary for the SIS reporting requirements, and in case of questions, reach out to GF Corporate Sustainability or GFCS Sustainability teams for clarification and support.
Improve the data quality in the Excel overview for reconciliation purposes and ensure a proper audit trail by maintaining all the supporting documents such as invoices on the local share drive for retrospective review and analysis.
Implement four-eyes principle controls in reporting the SIS figures to eliminate potential manual errors and improve traceability of the data sources.
Correct the identified classification deficiencies to fully align with the standard GOGNOS requirements.

Review the divisional controlling guidelines and translate the German ones into English for better understanding by the local team in China.
In case of updates of the directives or personnel turnover, deliver proper training to the concerned employees.

Strengthen the monthly closing reviews on all the deliveries signed in DAP or DDP incoterms, especially for November and December, and to customers overseas to ensure that revenues are recognized based on the agreed commercial incoterms, i.e. date upon risk transfer. 
Consult with GFCS Divisional Controlling and IT teams to figure out a feasible method (either automatic or manual) to correctly book the identified shipped-not-billed cases.

Organize joint meetings with the GFCS Controlling team to clarify the definition and analysis procedures as required in the divisional guideline.
According to the stock value of the "Cost Bearers" identified with "Valuation Factors 1 & 2" out of the defined range, prioritize the analysis on the top deviations based on the stock value and remark the reasons in case no action is needed after analysis, or systematic errors and further actions such as modification of cycle time, etc.

Align with the GFCS Divisional Controlling team regarding the correct financial treatments on mold & tooling amortization accrual including the relevant SAP and HBII accounts. 
Reclassify the current balances from accounts payable and inventories to other liabilities.
Add the bi-annual control review of the mold and tooling accruals in the monthly finance checklist to avoid any potential oversights amid mid-year and the year-end closing, for adjustments of both the negative inventory balances and remaining accrual balances in accounts payables.

Enhance collaboration with the GFCS Divisional Controlling team in case of new guideline issued without training.
In case of vague definitions or unclear statements in the divisional directives, contact the concerned divisional stakeholders for clarification and support.
Implement the required method and template to justify the capitalization date (operational readiness) of the high-value assets in a documented manner.

Ensure accurate and complete baseline data as the foundation for effective analysis. 
Data maintained manually should be checked for accuracy as it is entered.

The current Supplier Management policy should be reviewed and certain controversial requirements should be clarified to provide a straightforward instruction for execution. The purchasing contract must only be signed after the supplier has been fully approved internally.

The OTD calculation methodology currently only applicable to A-level suppliers could also be introduced as a general OTD KPI for suppliers in scope. 
This methodology gives a buffer of up to 3 days to consider the time difference between physical GR and system data input. The PO requested delivery date modification should only be made when the requesting department specifically requires changing the requested date or when the supplier has asked to change the date during the PO confirmation phase.

The SAP PM module should be fully utilized including work center master data update, maintenance strategy set up, maintenance task generation, allocation, and closure after actual maintenance work has been performed.
Each time a new machine is installed and put into use, the work center master data must be updated accordingly to make sure the machine is timely enrolled in the maintenance strategy. 
A regular review should be performed on the open maintenance orders for the Maintenance Engineers to follow up on the delayed tasks. The cancellation of certain tasks should be supported with clear comments and solid evidence as well.

Incorporate the signing of GF CoC as part of the employee onboarding process and archive the signed CoC together with the personnel files.
在员工入职流程中加入签署GF行为准则的环节，并将签署的行为准则与人事档案一起存档。

Purchase new Windows operating versions with valid supports for the PCs in use, e.g. Windows 10 and Windows 11 to improve IT security.
As a temporary countermeasure before updating the operating system, disable the WIFI connection to the external network for the identified PCs and only enable data transfer in the internal network.
为使用中的电脑购买尚在有效支持内的新 Windows 操作版本，如 Windows 10 和 Windows 11，以提升信息安全。
作为更新操作系统前的阶段性措施，禁用这些电脑的外网连接功能，仅启用内部网络数据传输。

Establish backup solutions for the server including method and frequency, e.g., purchase an-other backup server and locate it remotely in other Chinaust premises or in a different location within the same premises, and configure the automatic data transfer frequency, e.g., daily.
Schedule periodic checks of the backup solution that is implemented to verify it is running and backing up the company data as expected.
建立服务器数据备份的解决方案，包括备份方法和频率，例如，购买另一个备份服务器，并将其远程放置在亚大的其他场所或同一场所内的不同位置，并配置自动数据传输的频率，例如每天一次。
安排对已实施的备份解决方案进行定期检查，以验证其是否按预期运行并备份公司数据。

Based on the current purchasing activities, update the current tracking list of valid contracts with start and end dates including effective renewal dates to identify any overlooked purchases without contract agreed.
In case of regular purchases without an agreed contract, renegotiate the commercial terms with the suppliers and urge them to sign the appropriate contract template suggested by Chinaust Legal.
根据当前的采购活动，更新适用中的合同台账以及合同开始和结束日期包括有效的续约日期等，以识别任何被忽视的未签订合同的采购。
对于没有签订合同的长期采购，与供应商重新谈判商业条款，并敦促他们签署亚大法务建议的适当合同模板。

Arrange a joint meeting among Supplier Quality Engineers (SQE), Production and Purchasing department to clarify the rating methodology of supplier quality issues in the local procedure and its algorithm configured in the SRM system.
Once agreed upon, report the identified NCs caused by suppliers with its corresponding ratings in the monthly SRM evaluation.
组织供应商质量工程师 (SQE)、生产部门和采购部门参与会议，明确当地程序中的供应商质量评级方法以及 SRM 系统中配置的算法。
达成一致后，在 SRM 系统的月度评估中正确填报已确认由供应商造成的质量问题及其相应评级。

Clarify the concept of full-scope stocktaking and the blind count method in the local stocktaking guideline. For example, all the existing storage locations should be incorporated into the stock-taking plan. The distributed counting sheets should exclude the stock quantity balance from the ERP system, not the material code. Stop using the bar-coding system when performing stock-taking process to comply with the blind count method.
Strengthen the supervision role (“4-eyes principle”) of the Finance team in terms of the quarterly stocktaking process: one to count items and the other (from a different department) supervises and records the counts.
Enhance the stocktaking recording process, that all original hand-written documents should also be archived as supporting documents, indicating how the stocktaking is performed, either by weighing or by individual counting.
Consider implementing the cycle-count method instead of the quarterly full-scope stocktaking. As long as all materials are covered over the year, corresponding risks are to a certain extent mitigated.
在当地盘点指导性文件中明确全盘和盲盘的概念。例如，将所有现有货位都纳入盘点计划，分发的盘点表应剔除 ERP 系统中的库存数量，而不是物料编码，在盘点过程中停止使用先扫码后盘点的方法，以确保盲盘。
加强财务团队在季度盘点过程中的监督作用（"四眼原则"）：盘点一人负责清点物品，监盘一人（非同一部门）负责监督和记录清点结果。
加强盘点记录的程序，所有原始手写文件也应作为盘点文件存档，能说明盘点是如何进行的，是通过称重还是通过单独清点得出的结论。
季度性的全盘要求，特别是对于那些件数多的外购件，若无法实际达成，则应采用循环盘点的方法，只要在一年中覆盖所有物料号，相应风险也一定程度上被规避。

Formalize a standard procedure for MPR runs, including a calculation format with links to all the relevant data sources including lead-time, stock in-transit, material demand forecast, and lot size, to provide the basis for MRP runs.
Based on the MRP results calculated by the logic set up in the format, manual adjustments could be made whenever necessary but only with proper justification.
Improve the logics in the MRP format and update the data sources in a regular basis to enhance MRP accuracy. 
规范物料需求计划的相应标准程序，其中包括与所有相关数据源（包括交货时间、在途库存、材料需求预测和批量大小）链接的计算逻辑，为 MRP 运行提供依据。
根据格式中设定的逻辑计算出的 MRP 结果，可在必要时进行手动调整，但必须有适当的理由。
改进 MRP 格式中的逻辑，定期更新数据源，以提高 MRP 的准确性。

Organize joint discussions with the Business Segment to clarify the current expectations on sales margins, the customer bidding methods, and systems that Chinaust Auto Wuhan Branch is confronted with.
The final agreement with the aforementioned parties should be documented and used for further discussion with Chinaust Group and GFPS in updating the DoA, the pricing and quotation policy and workflow that could both ensure appropriate authorization and increase operational feasibility. 
Before the new policies and DoA are issued, follow the current DoA requirements by providing both expected and bottom-line margins in the first quotation calculation and request the approvals on the OA platform beforehand. In case of exceptions, where the approved bottom line could not be met, after verbal approval, post-approval should also be documented as per the required process.
组织与事业部的讨论，以阐明当前对销售利润的预期及武汉分公司所面临的客户招标方式和制度。
与上述各方达成的最终协议应记录在案，以供与亚大集团和 GFPS 进一步讨论来更新 DoA、定价和报价政策及工作流程，以确保适当授权并提高操作可行性。
在新政策和 DoA 发布之前，应按照现行 DoA 的要求，在首次报价计算中提供预期利润和底线利润，并事先在 OA 平台上申请批准。若出现紧急情况，竞标价格不得不突破报价底线，口头报批后应及时按流程审批。

Based on the routing comparison list, define a tolerance range such as 30% over or under-efficiency rate. In case of deviations out of the range, document the analysis and define actions if needed to solve issues such as correcting the routing setup in NCC or potential oversight in monitoring the actual routing.
根据工时比较的结果，确定容差范围，如效率超出或低于 30%的标准幅度。如果出现超出设定范围的偏差，应记录分析的结果，并在必要时制定解决措施，如纠正 NCC 中基础工时的设置或监控实际工时时可能出现的疏忽。

Map the coding methods of the two data sources in either the fixed asset register or the PM master data in the software for reconciliation purposes.
On a monthly basis, the Maintenance department should reconcile the financial record of fixed assets with the PM data in the software to avoid potential oversight.
在固定资产清单或设备维保软件主数据中对两个数据源的资产编码方法进行核对并一一对照。
设备维修部门应每月核对固定资产的财务记录和维保软件中的设备数据，以避免可能的设备疏漏。

All records related to the process of recruitment and final selection of the employee, whether hired or considered but not hired, must be retained for a minimum years after the conclusion is made and incorporated into the local record retention policy.
所有与招聘和最终遴选雇员过程有关的记录，无论是已聘用的还是考虑过但未聘用的，都必须在作出结论后至少保留一定年限，并将其纳入当地的文档管理办法。

The IT equipment list should be updated timely once the IT Specialist is notified about the exit of employees. In the meantime, the offline tracking list should be compared to the 360 Safeguard on a regular basis to make sure that additional equipment is included in the scope of software control and monitoring.
一旦 IT 专员收到员工离职通知，应及时更新 IT 设备清单。与此同时，应定期将离线跟踪清单与360安全卫士中的资产清单进行比较，以确保软件控制和监控范围内包括其他设备。

The related employee should stop using the personal laptop and ensures all documents saved in the local drives are removed permanently. If the company assigned laptop cannot fulfil the need for work, a new one with higher performance can be applied and purchased for use. In the meantime, IT trainings should be delivered to all employees that personal computers cannot be used for work purposes.
相关员工应停止使用个人笔记本电脑，并确保永久删除本地驱动器中保存的所有文件。如果公司分配的笔记本电脑无法满足工作需要，可申请并购买性能更高的新笔记本电脑。同时，应向所有员工提供信息技术培训，使其了解个人电脑不得用于工作目的。

The safety stock for externally purchased parts/components in the lineside cabin should be reviewed and redefined considering the importance, usage frequency, deliver batch size and whether they are wear parts or not. Accordingly, the safety stock should be properly maintained in WMS, meanwhile, instructions should be given to the external warehouse to timely replenish the cabin following the calculated requirements.
补数仓内存放的零部件的安全库存应根据其重要性、使用频率、交付批量以及是否为易损件等因素进行审查和重新定义。因此，应在 WMS 系统中对安全库存进行适当维护，同时应向外部仓库发出指令，使其按照计算出的需求及时补货。

Review the logical correspondence between the WMS and NCC systems. It is crucial to pay attention to and test the correspondence between the old and new systems when launching a new one.
Approved goods should undergo approval for scrapping before they are transferred to the "obsolete warehouses", while goods in "obsolete warehouses" that should not to be scrapped should be promptly returned to the regular warehouse.
梳理WMS和NCC的系统逻辑对应方式。当上线新的系统时，应关注新老系统之间的对应关系，并对其进行测试。
对于转移到”备品备件库“的产品应经过报废审批。将”备品备件库“中实际不应报废的产品及时转回正常仓库。

Modify the inventory shelving logic in WMS to avoid direct transfer of regular stock to the "obsolete warehouse" due to manual errors. When transfer is required, it should be done according to the approval.
修改WMS系统中库存上架逻辑，避免由于人工错误将正常库存直接转移到“备品备件仓库”。当需要转移时，应根据审批的结果进行移库操作。

The current raw material management should be reviewed jointly by Supply Chain Management, IT, Purchasing and Finance & Controlling. If the VMI business mode is to be continued, the WMS stock movement process should be redesigned to support VMI, otherwise Purchasing should negotiate with the supplier to potentially replace VMI with other favorable commercial terms.
目前的原材料管理应由供应链管理部门、信息技术部门、采购部门和财务部门共同讨论确认：如果要继续采用 VMI 业务模式，则应重新设计 WMS 库存移动流程，以支持 VMI，否则，采购部应与供应商协商，以其他有利的商业条款取代VMI。

Changshu branch should perform a review on all the authorization forms currently implemented at DingDing and consider combining them with the OA platform to streamline with Chinaust Shanghai Auto. For the routing changes that includes the involvement of outsourced processes, a remark should be made as well to differentiate with other process changes, in the meantime, Purchasing should also be included in the approvers of such requests to make sure the intended outsourced product is covered by the agreement with the outsourced manufacturer.
常熟分公司应对目前在钉钉实施的所有授权表单进行审查，并考虑将其与 OA 平台相结合，以便与上海亚大汽车进行简化。对于涉及外协流程的工艺路线变更，也应进行备注，以区别于其他流程变更。同时，采购部也应纳入此类申请的审批人，以确保与外协厂商的协议中涵盖了预定的外协产品。

Fill in the production KPIs based on the system data to ensure they accurately reflect the actual production situation. If there is data that need to be modified, R&M department can add a remark instead of modifying without any traceability.
根据系统中的数据按实填写生产绩效指标的数据，以准确地反映真实的生产情况。若存在需要修改数据的情况，维修维保部可以通过增加备注的方式，而不是通过无修改痕迹地修改数据的方式。

Ensure the integrity of equipment list. Review the production line acceptance process and notify the local maintenance department when production line is commissioned. Assess the comprehensiveness and appropriateness of the PM plan. If arranging the PM plan according to the type of production line cannot meet the maintenance requirement, it is feasible to arrange the maintenance plan by equipment. Ensure that maintenance records remain relevant to the PM plan. Revise the PM report format and instruct employees to record the results based on maintenance circumstances if additional functions are not available on the "Dingding" system.
确保设备清单的完整性。审查生产线验收流程，并在生产线投入使用时通知当地维修部门。评估预防性维修计划的全面性和适当性。若按生产线类型安排预防性维修计划无法满足维修要求，则可按设备安排维修计划。确保维修记录与预防性维修计划相关。如果”钉钉“系统没有该功能，则修改预防性维修报告格式，并指导员工根据维修情况记录结果。

Create a list of critical spare parts and define safety stock. If possible, identify this critical spare parts in ”YIDA“ and monitor their inventory. Stock count management of spare parts should be strengthened: record the time of the inventory, sign on stock count result by responsible personnel and analyse the stock counting results.
创建关键备件清单并确定安全库存。如有可能，在"宜搭"中确定这些关键备件并监控其库存情况。应加强备件库存盘点管理：记录盘点时间，由负责人员在库存盘点结果上签字，并分析库存盘点结果。

The IT department and Quality department should meet to sort out the detailed requirements of inbound inspection and implement the functions before final acceptance of WMS system. In the meantime, the current system implementation process should be improved to ensure that each stakeholder is involved with a clear accountable person.
在 WMS 系统最终验收之前，信息技术部门和质量部门应召开会议，对进货检验的详细要求进行梳理，并落实各项功能。同时，应改进当前的系统实施流程，确保每个相关部门都参与其中，并有明确的责任人。

Review and revise the GP-12 planning procedure to ensure timely and accurate tracing of the products which need GP-12 inspection or exit GP-12 process.
审查和修订 GP-12计划的方式，确保及时准确地追踪需要 GP-12检验或退出 GP-12流程的产品。

The IT Specialist should reach out to IT department of Chinaust Shanghai Auto and obtain the already available training material for new hired employees. The training record should be maintained together with the employee personal files.
常熟的IT负责人应联系上海亚大汽车公司的 IT 部门，获取已有的新员工IT培训材料。培训记录应与员工个人档案一起保存。

The annual leave per employee must be calculated and recorded in formal documentation. The leave balance should be tracked and communicated to the department managers and the employees themselves on a regular basis. If the annual leave is not used up for the year, it should be rolled to next year.
每名员工的年假必须计算并记录且正式留档，年假余额应定期跟踪并传达给部门经理和员工本人。如果当年年假没有用完，应滚动留存到次年。

Each employee compensation scheme has to be clearly documented, officially approved, and communicated to the concerned employees. The calculation of the bonus should be double checked and confirmed by the HR Manager to avoid conflict of interest or mistakes.
每项员工补偿计划都必须有明确的文件记录、正式批准并告知相关员工。奖金的计算应由人力资源经理进行双重检查和确认，以避免利益冲突或错误。

Management shall establish procedures for regularly reviewing and updating distribution lists to ensure that they accurately reflect the current membership and responsibilities within the organization. This may involve assigning responsibility for list maintenance to specific individuals such as the IT coordinators and implementing processes for regularly confirming and updating the list membership.

Management shall maintain up-to-date network documentation, including network diagrams. Regular reviews and updates to network diagrams should be performed to ensure that they accurately reflect the current state of the network, enabling IT staff to work more effectively, enhance security, maintain compliance, and support future growth and changes in the network infrastructure.

Corporate Compliance shall coordinate the creation of a comprehensive Technical and Organizational Measures (TOMs) documentation in order to mitigate Data Protection risks and to exhibit compliance and dedication to safeguarding personal data in accord-ance with the GDPR. Additionally, guidance should be extended to interested GF coun-tries to assist them in developing their own TOMs policies.

Corporate IT shall document the technical-organizational measures (TOMs) for the centralized services and applications they have employed and that are used for daily operations by local GF entities to protect personal data in accordance with the GDPR.
Management shall document the local TOMs, (e.g., physical security measures or local vulnerability management) for the local entity following the guidance from the Corporate.

Management shall regularly monitor and track SLA performance in the corporate ticketing tool. This allows organizations to proactively identify and address SLA breaches, ensuring timely resolution of issues, maintaining customer satisfaction, and upholding contractual obligations. The use of dedicated reports and discussion in regular service reviews should be enforced.

Management shall develop Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Plans that encompasses all necessary elements for effective recovery in the event of a disruption or disaster. This includes defining recovery requirements for business processes and applications, establishing clear criteria for invoking the BCP and the DRP, documenting detailed recovery and fall back and resumption procedures and ensuring secure and appropriate storage of data backups.

Management shall investigate the reason of the communication issue to ensure that all devices, including the newly acquired HP laptops, are effectively protected against malware, ransomware, and other advanced threats. This will significantly enhance the resilience and security of the endpoint environment within the Albershausen network.

Management shall disable the unauthorized Wi-Fi network using only the networks provided by Corporate GF. Additionally, employee awareness and education on the risks of connecting to non-standard Wi-Fi networks should be emphasized to promote responsible network usage within the corporate environment.

Management shall improve the AD management and ensure data accuracy. It is recommended to address the incomplete or missing information in user accounts, such as department, job title, and office fields, to maintain accurate user profiles.

Corporate IT shall  integrate the request form for new AD account creation into ServiceNow. By making important information such as job title, office, office location, department, and cost center mandatory fields in the form, the accuracy and completeness of user data can be ensured from the start. This streamlined process will enhance overall operational efficiency and improve IT support capabilities.

Management shall take prompt action to address the detected vulnerabilities. The servers running outdated and unsupported operating systems (OS) such as Windows Server 2012 should be decommissioned immediately. These obsolete systems pose significant security risks and should be replaced with supported and updated OS versions. All missing application and OS patches should be promptly installed on the servers. These patches are crucial for addressing vulnerabilities and improving the overall security of the systems. A comprehensive review of the installed software should be performed to determine if they are truly necessary for the functioning of the servers. Unnecessary software should be removed to reduce the attack surface and minimize potential vulnerabilities. The patching windows should be reconsidered.

Management shall install fire stops and emergency lights in the server room. Storage materials should be removed to minimize the risk of ignition and ensure compliance with safety regulations. Additionally, the network cabling colors should adhere to the GF Infrastructure Standard color definition in the facility room. A lock and key policy should be enforced for the electrical panels in the office area, ensuring that panels remain securely closed at all times. Additionally, the responsible personnel should be reminded of the importance of keeping keys secured and not left unattended.
Management shall change the system passwords following the complexity criteria explained in the GF password guidelines.

Management shall update or replace the unmanaged workstations with standard ones using standard GF devices under the IT department responsibilities. Admin rights for operators should be removed and a robust IT management system should be in place for all the production workstations, including regular updates, security patches, access control policies, and monitoring mechanisms to ensure the security and stability of the production environment.

Management shall ensure individualized access credentials, enforce proper registration protocols, provide temporary badges for visitors, implement a centralized logging system, use unique access credentials for different areas, and comply with GF PS Infrastructure Standards for the server room.

Management shall enforce the use of longer, more complex passwords, the organization can significantly reduce the risk of password-related vulnerabilities. It is advisable to update the Ectool HR system's password policy to align with the GF Password Guideline and provide clear instructions to employees on creating and managing secure passwords.

Management shall ensure that each workstation is equipped with the required USB HD webcams. These webcams should be positioned on top of the main monitor as specified in the standard. The workstation that lacks the required two monitors should be upgraded to meet the standard's specifications.

Division IT shall reinforce communication regarding workspace standards to all PS entities, emphasizing the importance of maintaining an optimal workspace setup. This includes ensuring the consistent use of the prescribed equipment to uphold the standard across all office workspaces.
Management shall implement a formal service request process for needed shared client types to avoid operational inefficiencies, delays in implementation and negative impact on production.

Ensure that the first line management specific job descriptions are drafted and signed by each management team member. Such signed document should be kept in the employee's personnel record. Prepared a succession manual that describes key activities of the first line management. Agree and document the deputies for the first line management positions in writing, e.g. on the organizational chart.

Submit draft distributorship agreements for review by GF's Legal Department and obtain written feedback. Agree next steps with GF's Legal Department and propose amendments to the distrib-utorship agreement (if necessary).
Update the Norwegian version of GTCS. Align the GFTCS with GF’s Legal Department and up-load a revised version to the Norwegian version of the GFPS website. Include, as part of the or-der confirmations clause, that customers agree to the GTCS upon accepting the order. Include a reference to GTCS on the order confirmation.
Use the anticorruption questionnaire to reassess the intermediaries in line with CI 1061 requirements. Retain the supporting evidence used in the reassessment of the intermediaries.

Use annual spending as a key criterion to prioritize signing and acceptance of GF’s Code for Business Partners by suppliers. Align with the Business Unit and / or Division management on sufficiency of assessing a vendor’s Code of Conduct in cases where suppliers refuse to sign GF’s Code for Business Partners.

Review the purchase price variance account on a monthly basis. Investigate the reasons for the variances and appropriate actions. Update the standard cost of materials on a periodic basis. Re-classify purchase price variance accounts from RPB 7 account 710 to 714.

Align the amortization calculations for the fusion machines to the requirements from DI 3440. Classify and mark machines that are for indoor and outdoor usage. Maintain an accurate list of machine purchase prices and dates. Create an asset in SAP for each machine and useful life in accordance with the requirements from DI 3440. Release the current excess inventory provision. Expense tools on the ongoing basis as they are purchased.
Redefine the process of unused vacation accrual calculations. Include in the calculation days not used in the current financial period.

Review the current SoD in the purchase-to-pay cycle and assess the possibility of reassigning the responsibilities related to PO creation, goods receipt posting and invoice verification. If due to the limitation of resources SoD is not feasible, assign an independent employee to perform a subsequent sample-based verification. Define a monetary threshold for PO releases in SAP and document it in the local DoA. Discontinue using TIS manual payments forms for foreign pay-ments. Include foreign invoices as part of the automatic payment run in SAP. Use SAP transaction MRBR to investigate the differences between the invoices, GRs and POs prior to release for payment.

Update the purchasing info records with actual material prices. Put in place a regular (i.e. month-ly) review of purchased material to ensure POs are placed at the condition defined in the corre-sponding material info record. Create necessary condition types for maintaining landed costs for items purchased based on POs.

Define a local threshold for the bonus related credit notes review and approval. For credit notes with significant amounts obtain the approval from the Managing Director prior settling the credit notes. Obtain approvals from the Head of BU EMEA where prescribed by the local DoA.

Inquire the Division HR if the MD’s salary is reviewed and validated on the monthly basis. If this is the case, consider granting system administrator access rights to the Managing Director as back-up user. If such arrangement is not possible due the risk of self-review, create a back-up instruc-tion and grant an administrative role to a Financial Controller in the North Europe Region.

Appoint a person who is not involved in the payroll reconciliation as a reviewer of the payroll rec-onciliation. Evidence the reconciliation and the review process. Alternatively, consider automating the process of accounting posting of payroll between SAP and VISMA Payroll.
Review the ICS matrix periodically and ensure that controls are assigned to the responsible actors. Include a review of the control descriptions to ensure their adequacy as well.
Reach out to GF's Legal department to obtain their written approval for engagement with Bierens Incasso Advocaten. Ensure Annex 2 of CI 1070 is signed.
Ensure supporting documentation is archived as required for completion of Anticorruption Questionnaire for Assessment of Intermediaries available in Annex 1 of CI 1061.
Ensure GT&Cs are embedded in the first quotations and orders. If not technically possible, ensure the link to the GT&Cs is working and that conflicting terms are explicitly rejected.
Ensure items are reported in the correct Cognos lines. Calculate the employee service awards provision yearly and account for it accordingly.

Establish a periodic review of the master files. For customer accounts tagged with "@@", ensure blocks are in place. For suppliers, review duplicate cases and delete or inactivate unnecessary accounts.
Centralize all customer complaints information in SAP QM module. Ensure workflows are implemented according to DoA for credit note approvals.

Ensure meeting minutes are taken following each monthly management meeting including the agreed actions. Follow-up of previously agreed actions should be a recurring topic at the subsequent management meetings.

Update the existing contract overview file including all key criteria (i.e. start date, end date, covenants, notice period, automatic renewal clauses etc.) from all contractual agreements currently in place with GFMS US. Consider using SharePoint technology to track existing contracts. Re-view the contracts overview on a regular basis.

Develop rules how to treat (customer) event invitations which are in line with the requirements from Corporate Instruction 1061. In case such events are subject to taxable fringe benefits, develop terms and conditions for both GFMS US employees as well as guests including the reporting requirement of such invitations towards the employer. Update the current Travel & Entertainment policy with the requirements from Corporate Instruction 1061. Communicate the new requirements to all employees concerned.
Conduct due diligence activities for all intermediaries in scope. Implement a process and define roles & responsibilities to ensure future intermediaries will be assessed accordingly.

Identify suitable enhancement projects for the currently uncovered focus areas from GF's Sustainability Framework. Develop a comprehensive target controlling and progress reporting that covers all open, on-going and closed enhancement projects across all focus areas. Establish processes to systematically identify, collect, report, and reconcile data necessary to fulfill reporting requirements in SIS.

Locate the missing IT service offering agreements with Hodgson. Develop a migration plan to already available GF service offerings in close collaboration with Division and Corporate IT considering applicable notice periods for termination of the agreements in place with Hodgson. Ensure acceptance of the GF-agreed low-IT-involvement operating model for commodity workplace services. Terminate overlapping IT service offerings with Hodgson in line with the agreed-on migration plan and migrate GFMS US' services to GF's standard IT offerings accordingly.

Determine the Privilege Club redemption rate (cost in USD per point) at least annually. Update the corresponding accrual and deferred revenue accordingly. Alternatively, discontinue the Privilege Club program.
Fully depreciate the remaining value of the damaged MILL P 500U machine. Upon getting a confirmation that the claim is accepted by the insurance company recorded a corresponding receivable.

Adjust the current calculated provision value in the financial statements accordingly. Use the tem-plate for employee service award payments published on Corporate Controlling’s page (We@GF) to re-calculate the provision for employee service awards annually as of 30 September and adjust the recorded provision accordingly.

Redefine the process of accruing the unvouchered liabilities. Release the T USD 500 historical accrual. Accrue on the monthly basis amounts related to freight, utilities and marketing costs based on the historical experience. Reverse the accrual upon receipt of the invoices. On the bi-annual basis revise the accuracy of the accrued amounts.

Conduct a refresher training on GF Machining Solutions credit policy among the Service + Success and Sales Administration teams. Establish a process where the Sales Administration department sends a list of all open orders to the Accounts Receivable department prior to placing a new machine order. When the credit exposure exceeds T EUR 50 obtain the required approvals from Business Unit or Division Management.

Redesign the bad debt calculation file by assessing the ageing at the level of individual customers and by adding a column with the receivables insured by Coface. Configure the ageing report to include the range of aged receivables from 90 to 120 days. Indicate receivables that are not in-sured, exceed insurance limits or the company loses the right to file a claim with the insurer. Ap-ply 50% and 100% ratios for receivables aged over 90 and 180 days respectively. Discontinue using the reduction of 50% due to the longevity of equipment repossession process in the USA. Take advantage of credit insurance extension, whenever possible.
Development of a business case for entry into the second-hand machine market in the US. Take a decision whether second-hand machine market opportunities should be pursued or not.

Recover the Excel tracking file which lists tools and respective calibration due dates. Verify the existence of all tools and their timely calibration. Periodically review the file to identify overdue tool calibrations and take necessary actions. Include the RMA verification as part of the exit checklist.
Remind the Field Service Managers about the requirement to periodically update the skills levels of each FSE. Embed the level of expertise of each FSE in FSM.

Establish the minimum margin limit for consumables based on limits defined in the DoA. Revoke the access right from Service + Success employees that are not involved in the consumable sales process. Include additional sourcing costs such as freight and duty in the gross margin calculation. Calculate and upload the list prices to MS GreatPlains on the monthly basis.
Create an overview of the local and corporate retention requirements per document type. Dis-pose of the documents with expired retention periods.

Prior to engaging with external law firms obtain a written approval from GF's Legal department. Use the engagement letter template published by GF's Legal department (Corporate Instruction 1070 Annex 2) in future engagements with external lawyers.

Establish project overviews for all sustainability initiatives. Include essential elements such as responsible, timeline, budget, KPIs. Map the already running projects to GFPS' Sustainability Strategy.
Implement restricted access to the hazardous goods room using employee badges. Display clear warning signs and ensure proper ventilation to prevent the accumulation of hazardous fumes.

Rearrange the responsibilities related to preparation and posting of the manual journal entries. Respect the 4-eyes principle when posting the manual journal entries. Review of the manual journal entries should be always done by an employee who neither is a preparer of the calcula-tion, nor is responsible for the manual postings.

Obtain an agreement from Corporate Controlling on leaving the intangible assets capitalized that are below the established threshold. Consult and agree with Corporate Controlling any fu-ture capitalizations of intangible assets with the value less than T CHF 50.

Take a decision on retrospective correction or not. Conduct an awareness training concerning operating vs. finance lease assessment with key employees involved (i.e. Procurement de-partment and first line management). Implement a properly documented operating vs. finance lease assessment procedure by using Corporate Controlling's Leasing template available in We@GF.

Define an action plan with the external warehouse provider on addressing the 5S issues. Clarify existence of the insurance for the stock stored at the warehouse. Define the expectations on contractual clause of gross negligence.

Include at the year-end stock count employees from different departments. Shadow the stock count process performed by the fin-ished goods warehouse provider. Analyze the stock differences occurring in cycle counts. De-fine a threshold for the investigation of stock adjustments. Conduct a root-cause analysis and document rationale for these adjustments. Derive suitable measures and implement corre-sponding corrective actions. Ensure the discrepancies are communicated and approved ac-cording to the local DoA.

Perform a plausibility check of the quantities invoiced by the external warehouse provider. As-sign the Head of Production Planning and Supply Chain as responsible for the invoice verifica-tion of BRT in xFlow.

Conduct a comprehensive review of key MRP criteria for all materials. Consult the Supply Chain experts in GFPS if required. Document the analysis performed. Identify opportunities for the stock optimization and take respective actions.
Upon the receipt of boxes with raw materials conduct a sample weighting. Perform the inbound quality check upon reception of the raw materials at the CR Autotrasporti warehouse.
Draft and sign a contract that governs responsibilities on stock and quality with the third-party sub-contractor.

Conduct a comprehensive stock count of all spare parts and consumables available at the spare parts warehouse. Reflect the actual stock situation in the Microsoft Access database. Account in the database all items that are stored, but do not have a location assigned. Remove unnecessary items from the spare parts warehouse. In case the location will be used for the purpose of storing non-conformities, separate and clearly label that stock. Perform inventory turnover analysis and define fast moving and slow-moving spare parts. Upon finalizing the as-sessment of spare parts criticality add this criterion to the database.
Review the Divisional DoA and adapt it to local requirements. Use the standardized DoA template to create a local DoA. Communicate the DoA to all involved employees.

Include the assessment on the requirements of compliance e-learnings as part of the entry checklist. Define a monthly process where newly hired employees are included in the annual loop for compliance e-learnings completion. Inform supervisors about the need to achieve a 100% completion of the compliance e-learnings within the established due dates. Revise the existing list of employees and enroll them into compliance e-learnings accordingly.

Reassess the conditions specified in the memo for determination of the warranty costs. Analyze the customers claims and determine the costs of reworks for each accepted claim. Based on the total cost associated with the addressing the customer claims, determine the cost as a total per-centage of sales. Apply this ratio to calculate the warranty provision. Offset future warranty relat-ed costs against the provision.

Prior to installation of fixed assets, assess the potential internal effort related to installation costs. In case significant efforts will be required, establish a process to capture hours of direct labor hours worked on installations. Allocate direct labor costs to the assets under construction. Revise the approach to capture the transportation cost related to the deliveries of the equipment. Include these costs as asset under construction and capitalize them once the asset reaches operational readiness.

Consider sending GFCS' Novazzano employees to the suppliers with the highest stock level to verify accuracy, completeness and existence of GFCS Novazzano inventory in the course of the annual stock count. Alternatively, request a written confirmation of WIP inventory stored at the remaining suppliers premise at year end.

Determine an hourly rate for maintenance staff together with the local Finance department. Set up a cost center structure by defining all machines as individual cost centers. Allocate the costs and time spent for repair and maintenance work to the individual cost centers in SAP accordingly. Check, if "Rebecca" can be interfaced to SAP. Populate the defined hourly rate in "Rebecca". Perform an overall cost and hours spent analysis on machine and equipment level. Utilize accu-mulated repair and maintenance costs to proactively decide on purchasing new machines or main-taining existing ones in the future.

Review the assets recorded in Rebecca and align mapping of the assets to SAP’s fixed assets register. Define in Rebecca if preventive maintenance activities are required. Redefine the pro-cess of preventive maintenance planning. Use a Gannt Chart for the purpose of scheduling and indicate for each equipment the timing of the planned maintenance. Align the schedule with the relevant Heads of Production Areas.

Conduct a cost-benefit analysis to assess the reasonability of costs split between external and internal repair and maintenance providers. Support the analysis with cost calculations considering the complexity of repair operations and requirements of keeping knowledge in-house or sourcing it externally

Define a contractual agreement with all major external processors covering all key elements such as liability for damages or losses of materials, inventory verification, prices, intellectual property etc. and consult Corporate Legal to ensure compliance to GF business standards.
Update the current overview of contracts in the MS Access database by including all relevant contracts. Make sure that key contractual information is complete and available at a glance.

Ensure that supporting evidences used during the next assessment of intermediaries are maintained. Review the current intermediary tracking for data consistency and update inconsistent or missing information such as the signing dates of compliance annexes for timely renewal. Reinforce the importance of singing Compliance and Distributorship Agreements. In case of intermediaries' reluctance to sign Compliance Agreements, address this topic with GF's Compliance Officer to find a suitable solution. Update the local travel and entertainment expense policy, in line with CI 1061.

Define roles and responsibilities for the manual upload of customer and supplier master data to the ComplianceXpress database. Ensure that these uploads are carried out regularly, i.e. at least weekly.

Implement a process to systematically collect warranty costs incurred with Layher and reflect these in the warranty provision calculation accordingly. Update the social security percentages in the vacation provision calculation. Re-design the current semi-retirement obligation provision calculation scheme considering the present value. Alternatively, have the semi-retirement obligation provision calculated by an external actuary.
Resolve all aged quarantine stock cases. Define roles and responsibilities to regularly review the quarantine stock location and timely resolve its associated cases.
Implement a workflow approval process or a regularly (i.e. quarterly) performed manual detective review control for inventory adjustments.

Prior to the physical and system-wise (accounting) scrapping of inventory, justify the reason for scrapping and obtain an approval from the Managing Director and the BU Head according to the DoA guidelines.

Assess the possibility to implement the already existing CRM solution (MS Dynamics 365) or an alternative application GF Uponor is using. In case the existing CRM solutions will not be implemented, ensure that customer specific knowledge will be documented and accessible.
Implement a process to systematically track and follow-up on customer quotations (i.e. after a suitable period such as 1 week before the quotation's validity date).
Implement a regular reporting of aged customer complaint cases and take necessary corrective action to timely resolve and close such cases.

Collect key production requirement planning input parameters such as sales forecast figures in higher frequency (i.e. quarterly) from GFPS sales companies. Update the production requirement planning timely. Increase the frequency of the inventory analysis to at least quarterly.

Review the maintenance tasks of all machinery and reconcile the tasks and maintenance intervals with manufacturer requirements. Prioritize machines which approach the end of their warranty period followed by those which are still within their warranty period. Update the maintenance system with the aligned maintenance tasks and execute them accordingly.
Categorize business-critical positions requiring succession in the near future. Identify possible deputies and successors and develop training plans for adequate knowledge transfer.
Complete the CIRB for GFMS CZ. Place the hardcopy binders in the offices. Ensure key employees are made aware of the purpose and location of the CIRB.
Upgrade the network infrastructure and redesign the network configuration, moving aligning with corporate standard. Reach out to Corporate IT for assistance with the technical implementation.

Agree with Divisional and Corporate Controlling on the assumptions and the exact parameters to calculate the provision for employee service awards. Record the calculated provision value in the financial statements accordingly.
Ensure no unencrypted personal information is transferred outside the GF network.

Ensure at-a-glance reporting between invoices and SIS is possible. Perform adjustments to reported values once actual figures are available and avoid double reporting. Consider using the attachment function in SIS to store invoices digitally in one location.

Calculate the respective hourly cost rate for 2nd level support and applications departments based on the budget figures of the corresponding cost centers of the current year. Update SAP with the resultant cost rates in order to ensure more accurate allocation of cost related to billable and warranty services.
Liaise with Division Controlling to agree on a solution to address the deviation in the start of depreciation.
Include costs incurred by 2nd level support for warranty services in the overall TU warranty cost calculation. Use the complete cost figure in the calculation of warranty provision going forward.

Issue a reminder to all employees that the travel pre-authorization process must be followed. Require all line managers to verify that the pre-authorization is in place prior to approving the travel expense claims, in order to timely identify cases where the process was not followed.
Physically segregate hazardous materials, restrict access to them and ensure they are stored on drip pans. Display warning signs prominently.

Document the governance rules for stock at external locations, including responsibility in case of loss and damage and the defined procedure on stock counting. Amend the contracts with SM System and Litz to formalize those rules. Consider direct GF participation in the yearly stock counting on-site with Litz.
Correct the reporting error in RPB6 in the next reporting period. Ensure RBP6 reporting is fully aligned with actual consolidated costs of the Falcon/Spot project.
Use the latest "Non-Disclosure Agreements for R&D" contract templates published by GF Corporate Legal for all existing and future cooperation with external parties that support R&D activities.
Redesign the HR documents storage, ensuring one storage location is used for given document types. Stop the usage of the unencrypted external hard drive.

Update the onboarding plan template for new hires with a mandatory introduction to Corporate Instructions slot. Instruct employees to familiarize themselves with the policies that need to be closely followed during their daily work based on their role. For existing employees, make a one-time communication to highlight the same topic.
Develop a skills matrix covering all necessary skills and knowledge areas applicable at GFMS Laser. Define target skills and develop training plans to address gaps and support succession planning.
Document approvals for bonus calculations before payments. Ensure evidence of approval is stored in a secure location and can be easily retrieved.

Establish a process for defining the job descriptions for each employee, not only for vacancies to be filled, but also for existing employees.
Ensure that all employees review and sign their job description with their line manager and retain the signed job description in their personnel file. 
Regularly reconcile the latest job descriptions with the tasks agreed and assessed annually in MyPD@GF, with regard to any changes in roles or responsibilities, and have them updated and signed in a timely manner.

According to CI 1026, define the local guideline for records retention and follow the retention requirements.
Incorporate data protection clauses in the contract signed with the external warehousing provider and implement physical access restrictions to the aged files stored externally.

Reinforce the contract review process together with the Corporate Legal department for the existing intermediaries to finalize the templates for different intermediary types.
Push all the intermediaries to sign the new contract template and terminate the old or simplified contracts especially at the end of the contract term for renewal.
For the agent identified with only commission memo agreed, complete the two annexes required by CI 1061 before commencing the next transaction with them.
Reassess all the existing intermediaries in terms of the compliance-related risks according to the standard checklist in Annex 1, and retain the color version for greater transparency and clarity for management review and approval.
Contact the dealers for the machines missing end-use information and update the end-user data in the iScala system for a more effective compliance checks by using the AEB screening tool.

Reinforce the process for data collection, reconciliation, retention and reporting of the SIS social indicators of both the headquarter office and the two branches. 
Keep regular communication with the corporate and divisional sustainability stakeholders in terms of questions and uncertainties on the reporting definitions.

Collaborate and agree with Division Controlling on the assumptions and the parameters to calculate the provision for employee service awards based on the employee handbook and the Corporate Accounting and Reporting Guidance on Employee Benefit.

Define internal credit assessment controls, including roles and responsibilities, control methodology and control frequency to ensure that all the credit limits set up in the systems are based on the latest customer information or even forecast on hand. 
For example, taking into account the sales volumes for non-machine sales only, the historical payment behaviors and public information, etc.
Implement and document the defined controls including the credit limit calculations, rationale for the adjustments on the calculated limits and the final agreed and approved credit limits to be uploaded in the system as master data.

Take advantage of any supplier requests for price changes to renegotiate with the three suppliers to amend the existing evergreen contracts with terms favorable to GF for the existing warehousing and logistics services.
Consult the Corporate Legal to add certain clauses stating that damage and loss of inventory will be insured or covered by the external warehouse providers, or at least send the suppliers GF's General Terms and Conditions including the Supplier Code of Conduct to the suppliers.

Define internal controls on periodic reviews of the open sales quotations in the CRM system, such as control over frequency, scope of the open and invalid quotations, documentation requirements, etc.
In terms of quotations which already expired and indeed lost, change the status from "open" to "closed" in a timely manner to ensure database accuracy.
For quotations that are invalid but are still under discussion with the customer, record the reasons and follow-up results in the CRM system as a reminder for next quotation.

Reinforce the controls in timely delivering iPDFs to the manufacturing plants, maintaining accurate warranty data in the IMD and continuously collaborating and communicating to the plants.
Set up controls on a monthly or quarterly basis to detect and correct the data inaccuracy identified.
Update the list of service tools and measurement devices to include information regarding calibration requirements, calibration frequency and the last and next calibration dates.

Set up annual controls for margin analysis on the maintenance contracts completed and document the analysis and action plans for those with relatively lower or higher margins than targets.
Discuss the current DoA requirements on margin controls of maintenance contracts with GFMS Customer Service to implement a feasible method either beforehand based on historical cost data, or afterwards based on the realized costs to ensure internal compliance.

Establish a margin calculation control on the local level before offering discounts to customers for billable services.
If a system-enforced approval workflow could not be realized, offline approval via Emails should be retained in a documented manner and post analysis should be performed on order level.
In case of margins below the DoA threshold, ask for proper approval before sending the quotation with discounts to the customers.

Based on the priority of different type and status of the service orders, define a schedule to clean up the aged service order in iScala. For example, most urgent orders could be external orders to customers and job already completed to be invoiced.
Develop and implement a regular review process to ensure that the service order database in the ERP system iScala is relatively up-to-date for proper and efficient follow-ups.

Reinforce the instructions and requirements in the GFMS Returns Directive to both internal concerned parties and external agents and dealers.
Perform regular reviews and follow-up actions on the open cases from the tool report.

Perform due diligence analysis of all intermediaries by using annex 1 of CI 1061. Ensure that these assessments are renewed according to CI 1061 and that supporting evidence is archived with respect to each individual assessment.

Align local travel expenses practice with corporate standards. Ensure global tools such as Egencia and the corporate Bank of America credit card are used if feasible locally. Consider implementing an expenses tool to ensure effective approvals control. Review the local travel policy to align it with current company setup. Ensure details of third-party participants are captured and that thresholds for pre-approval are respected.

Update the PO template to ensure the GPCs, and consequently the GF Code for Business Partners, are communicated to suppliers. Review the Code for Business Partners available at gfps.com/pt-br to ensure it is applicable to local specifics.

Install temperature and humidity monitoring systems, one additional HVAC unit and a fire/smoke detection and suppression system. Label cables and ensure they run through cable trays. Log access to the server room. Consider improvements to the structural integrity of the room, by raising the floor and ensuring walls are fire resistant. Improve the backup procedures with a enterprise-level solution and ensure a backup schedule is implemented.
Reconfigure the network to isolate CCTV cameras on a dedicated network, preventing potential security breaches.

Complete and formally approve the DRP, considering all the requirements set in the GF Corporate Standard on Data Backup and Recovery. Perform an initial test of the plan to verify its effectiveness, operational capability, and appropriateness. Establish a yearly review of the plan.

Ensure warranty costs are defined and tracked. Based on that information, apply the formula for the calculation of the warranty provision as stated in DI 3422 and include it in the financial reporting.

Consider contracting the alert function of Serasa and update customers accordingly in SAP Business One. Ensure the credit limit function of SAP Business One is used for all customers. Ensure business data is hosted in locations where access and control are owned by the organization and not by an individual employee.

Ensure first level approval is performed by a different person than the one submitting the payment proposal. Utilize system controls within the e-banking platform to enforce segregation of duties and approval workflows.
Perform a risk assessment of suppliers. Develop an audit calendar, perform on-site supplier audits, communicate the audit results and follow-up on the implementation of corrective measures.
Create a specific virtual location in SAP for quarantine items. Establish a periodic review of the virtual and physical quarantine locations.

Consider reducing the number of logistic companies and establishing a few key logistics partners, with contracted KPIs to ensure quality and performance standards are met. Develop a performance monitoring system to regularly assess the logistics partners' adherence to KPIs and overall service quality.

Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) for both distributors and agents, including sales targets, customer satisfaction, compliance with company policies, and efficiency in logistics. Implement a regular review schedule based on risk assessment and sales strategy. Establish a two-way feedback mechanism. Consider aligning performance incentives with the results of these evaluations.

Ensure two-hand control devices are installed to protect against mechanical hazards. Consider installing protective shields on machines working with plastic parts or replacing the machines altogether. Install protective barriers for rack posts. Complete installation of protective barriers in the remaining extruders. Consider setting up a vending-machine style PPE dispenser(s) that work 24/7. Ensure hazardous goods are adequately stored and potential risks contained through containment devices. Evaluate using side-loader forklifts designed for long loads to move larger diameter pipes safely.

Conduct an asbestos survey to determine if the material is present. Ensure that an adequate fire protection system is in place. Enhance physical security. Redesign the current raw material circuit. Ensure hazardous goods are adequately stored and potential risks contained through containment devices. Evaluate using side-loader forklifts designed for long loads to move larger diameter pipes safely. Consider an alternative solution for lunch that assures hygiene standards for food are met. Evaluate the effort and cost of updating the current Recife location to comply with GF H&S standards and consider alternative solutions.

Align preventive maintenance checklists and schedules with manufacturer specifications. Consider implementing a computerized maintenance management system (CMMS) to track maintenance activities, schedule preventive tasks, and monitor machine health.
Systematically document and track all spare parts. Define critical parts and minimum stock levels according to a risk assessment. Ensure items are tagged and counted at least once a year.
Obtain ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 certifications for both the Cajamar and the Recife plant. Obtain the ISO 9001 certification for the Recife plant.

Define key quality performance indicators, including, but not limited to rejection pieces per million, customer and supplier complaints. Accurately capture cost of quality and include it in production cost assessment.
Ensure employees have at least one 24-hours period of consecutive rest per week. Review the needs of production against current capacity and ensure positions are adequately staffed.

Ensure that purchase requisitions are created in SAP which then are acted upon by the Purchasing Specialist. Transfer the purchasing activities currently performed by operational departments (e.g., Engineering, Maintenance, etc.) to a dedicated Purchasing Specialist. Ensure that requests for proposal and requests for quotations are performed and decided upon as a responsibility of the Purchasing function.

Perform a comprehensive review of the SAP settings and controls to ensure that payment terms set at the purchasing organization level are accurately applied when processing invoices. Ensure deviations justified by business reasons are approved via workflow.
Review and adjust the link to the GPCs in the purchase order template to ensure that suppliers and service providers have the opportunity to read and accordingly accept the GPCs.
Maintain a list of major local suppliers and evaluate them on a regular basis in order to improve their performance and service quality.
Ensure adherence to GF's Health and Safety Standards. Consider implementing a weekly Gemba Walk, discuss the results on the shopfloor meeting and implement corrective actions.

Restrict physical access to the spare parts location or implement a detective control of unauthorized access. Complete the spare parts inventory process and ensure the highest value and/or critical items are identified as such. For the spare parts processes as such, ensure that future movements of the inventory are recorded in Ultimo to always reflect the actual spare parts inventory.

Management, in collaboration with the IT department, shall restrict internet access on the operator PCs. Additionally, measures for enhancing physical security such as a policy  to support expected use cases for remote workers should be taken into account.

Management should implement a robust vulnerability management process, which is critical to mitigating security incidents and related negative impacts on the GF organization and maintaining a secure corporate network.

The Division SAP teams shall thoroughly document the automated processes involved in transmitting payments from SAP to SWIFT-related components. This includes creating a detailed infrastructure and systems diagram, as well as outlining the communication configuration to prepare a dedicated Standard Operating Procedure (SOP). It is crucial to ensure compliance with SWIFT requirements through thorough verification.

Management shall enforce the use of longer, more complex passwords, the organization can significantly reduce the risk of password related vulnerabilities. It is advisable to update the SAP system's password policy to align with the GF Password Guideline and provide clear instructions to employees on creating and managing secure passwords.

Management shall prioritize the integration of backend systems provided by the DXC service provider into the GF Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) process. Related documentation should be promptly prepared to ensure comprehensive system monitoring and logging.

Corporate HR shall integrate the activation of mandatory training for new employees immediately after the hiring process is completed. Additionally, a reminder process should be implemented to ensure that employees comply with the mandatory training requirement. An escalation process in case of repeated non-compliance must also be foreseen.

Purchase new Windows operating versions with valid supports for the PCs in use, e.g. Windows 10 and Windows 11 to improve IT security.
In case of equipment related PCs that need to be operated under Windows 7, disable the WIFI connection and only enable internal data transfer.
Purchase anti-virus software that cannot be disabled by employees and centralize PC admin rights by local IT for all software downloads and retrospective monitoring.
为使用中的电脑购买尚在有效支持内的新 Windows 操作版本，如 Windows 10 和 Windows 11，以提升信息安全。
如果设备相关 PC 需要在 Windows 7 操作系统下运行，禁用 WIFI 连接外网，仅启用内部网络数据传输。

IT training should be provided to all employees to re-emphasize the importance of backing up data and files, and business files should be stored on the shared drive with access control or the specific path of the folder that enables automatic backup. It is also suggested to increase the backup frequency to daily. 

应向所有员工提供IT专项培训，再次强调备份数据和文件的重要性，业务文件应存储在设置有访问控制的共享盘上，或存储在可实现自动备份的文件夹的特定路径中。我们同时建议将备份频率提升至每天一次。

The credit actually granted to new customers must be sufficiently supported by valid information (e.g., the size of the customer, estimated turnover of the won project, business reputation of the customer) and proper internal authorization. NCC workflow must be configured according to the actual situation. In the case of the observation, the new customer should be given a credit limit master data record with credit limit set to zero, which will activate the approval request from the Regional Sales Director.
Meanwhile, if the current policy requirement not to grant credit to newly introduced customers is not realistic due to the relatively low bargaining power of Chinaust Utility companies, it should be reviewed whether the requirement can be changed to be more practical to the actual situation. For example, if there is solid information to support a good credit history and reputation of the new customer, a credit limit and be granted to the new customer. 
实际授予新开发客户的信用额度必须有充分的有效信息支持（例如客户规模、中标项目的预计收入、客户的商业信誉等）和适当的内部授权。必须根据实际情况设置NCC的审批工作流程。根据审计发现中描述的现状，新客户应首先配置信用管理主数据记录，并将信用额度设为零，才能激活区域销售总监的审批要求。
同时，如果由于市政业务的议价能力相对较低，现行政策要求不给新引入的客户任何信用额度不现实，则应审查是否可以改变该硬性要求，使其更符合实际情况。例如，如果有可靠的资料证明新客户有良好的信用记录和声誉，也可以给予新客户一定的信用额度。

The financial department should extract the prepayment aging report on a monthly basis to systematically identify the aged prepayment and remind responsible department accordingly. In the meantime, emphasize to the business departments that once the equipment has been accepted and put into use, the invoice should be submitted to the finance department timely.
Implement a quarterly review control on the transactions of the one-time vendor account to ensure internal usage compliance and mitigate any duplicated payment risks.
财务部门应按月导出预付账款账龄报告，系统性地识别账龄较长的预付账款，并相应提醒责任部门。同时，应向业务部门强调，一旦设备验收合格并投入使用，应及时向财务部门提交发票。
对一次性供应商账户的交易实施季度审查控制，确保内部使用合规，降低重复付款风险。

The purchasing team should follow the standard purchasing process to make sure that before any contracts are signed or payments made, the required review must be performed, and approval must be obtained. For single source suppliers of direct production material, the decision should be fully supported by the confirmation from requestor, for example E-mail from customers who designates a particular supplier.
采购团队应遵循标准的采购流程，确保在签署任何合同或支付任何款项之前，必须进行必要的审查并获得批准。对于直接生产材料的单一来源供应商，应通过请求者的确认（如指定特定供应商的客户的电子邮件）来充分支持这一决定。

The purchasing process, from sourcing to invoice verification should be enforced to the Production team that is currently responsible for the purchase of consumables. A purchase threshold could be determined and every purchase above the threshold must be properly reviewed for price and quality comparison and purchase contract.
For consumables that are centrally sourced by Chinaust Utility, the most updated price list should be maintained and checked against for each purchase.
目前负责采购低值易耗品的生产团队应当严格执行标准的采购流程，从供应商比质比价到发票的审核。可以确定一个采购金额限制，每一次超过限额的采购都必须经过适当的比质比价审批，并签订采购合同。
对于由市政事业部集中采购的消耗品，应保留最新的价目表，并在每次采购时进行核对。

The company should enforce the intermediary contract approval process especially to the Sales department that the approval must be obtained prior to entering into the business relationship. The commission must also be fully considered when approving the estimated gross margin. 
公司应严格执行中介合同审批程序，特别是销售部门必须在建立业务关系之前获得批准。在批准估计毛利率时，还必须充分考虑佣金。
