A comprehensive review of the installed software should be performed to determine if they are truly necessary for the functioning of the servers. Unnecessary software should be removed to reduce the attack surface and minimize potential vulnerabilities. It should be ensured that all servers have the GF Corporate antivirus software installed to protect against viruses and malware.

Management should reconfigure the network setup to ensure that the CCTV cameras are not directly linked to the office network but are connected to the dedicated network specifically designated for CCTV systems. This segregation effectively mitigates security risks and thwarts unauthorized access to the office network via the cameras.
It is recommended to implement secure communication protocols like Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) to encrypt the data exchanged between the administrative interface and the cameras. This measure guarantees the confidentiality and integrity of administrative interactions.

Management shall perform a thorough review of all external user accounts to identify accounts that are no longer necessary or are inactive. A process for conducting periodic reviews of external user accounts should be established to ensure that access rights are up to date and aligned with business requirements. A schedule for regular account reviews should be defined to maintain the integrity of user account management.

Management shall promptly address the existence of unsupported and vulnerable operating systems in the production environment, as these systems present substantial risks to the availability, confidentiality, and integrity of IT systems. Establishing and maintaining a secure and compliant IT infrastructure is essential to effectively mitigate these risks.

Corporate IT shall troubleshoot and resolve replication issues with the servers that were not successfully replicating. By ensuring proper replication of the GPO across all relevant servers, the intended security measures can be effectively enforced to safeguard against unauthorized access and maintain network integrity.

Management shall enforce the creation of a dedicated Group Policy Object (GPO) to prevent the simultaneous connection of the corporate wired network and the Wi-Fi network. This GPO aims to address the issue of bridging connections that could potentially widen the attack surface and increase the risk of unauthorized access.

Management shall remove the large wooden arch structure supporting the door in the primary server room to eliminate any potential obstructions and fire risk to the server room. An immediate inspection of all fire extinguishers in the offices and production site to ensure they are in good working condition should be conducted. Maintenance and servicing of the fire extinguishers to bring them up to date with the recommended intervals, such as every 24 months, should be scheduled and the next revision dates on the fire extinguishers to reflect the latest maintenance activities should be updated.

Management shall promptly take actions to enforce password changes, implement robust password practices in alignment with the GF possword policy, and proactively address the security vulnerabilities associated with default passwords across all evaluated devices.

Management shall establish isolation between the production network and the corporate network. This can be accomplished through the implementation of effective network segmentation, access controls, and security measures to ensure the protection of the production systems and data, preserving their integrity and confidentiality.

Management shall enforce robust access controls, implementing strong authentication mechanisms, regularly monitoring and auditing access to backup and application files, and encrypting sensitive data to uphold its confidentiality and integrity.

Management shall evaluate the implementation of a Remote Desktop Services (RDS) solution to address the performance problems experienced when using the GFOS Smart Manufacturing software remotely via a VPN. By introducing a remote desktop client or utilizing an existing RDS infrastructure, the organization can provide a more efficient and reliable way for users to access the software remotely. RDS can help improve the performance of the software by allowing users to access a virtual desktop environment that is hosted on a server with better resources and connectivity.
Post calculated bad debt provision values on a monthly basis in SAP as required by the Division Instruction 3432. Consider implementing the 4-eyes-principle during the yearly credit limit update.

Ensure completeness and timely renewal of the intermediaries assessment (Annex 1) without any threshold definition, using the latest version of Annex 1 of CI 1061. Put a focus on the collection of a signed version of Compliance and Distributorship Agreements for the intermediaries. If intermediaries refuse to enter Distributor Agreements or to sign the GF Compliance Agreement, consult the Head of Corporate Compliance for a discussion of further proceedings.
Ensure suppliers agree to the GF Code for Business Partners and GFPS GPC's prior to entering the business relationship. Utilize standard PO's for external purchases.
Review and adjust the sales agent contract. Ensure that changes to agent commissions are documented contractually.

Consult the service provider to find a solution securing payroll payments against mistakes or po-tential manipulation. Consider introducing a detective control instead if the payroll provider cannot support in securing the payroll file.

Request MTI to sign the Compliance Agreement or provide a copy of their own CoC that is equivalent to the GF CoC. Conduct the Anticorruption Assessment on MTI and in case of any red flags, the cooperation must be approved by the BU Head in accordance with CI 1061.

Introduce safety helmets requirements for work involving cranes. Reorganize hazardous materials storage and shelves, inspect and restrict access. Replace damaged first aid boxes, introduce regular checks and distribute them throughout all areas of the facility. Install functioning drip pans setup in the warehouse for liquid storage. We suggest to increase the ROSS rounds to cover the entire facility once a month.
Manual exclusions of calculated slow moving stock provisions should not be made unless otherwise permitted by the GFMS Stock Directive or approved by Division Controlling.
Required figures by the BOARD warranty provision calculation tool should be computed based actual warranty sales and service data. The supporting calculations should be documented and retained.
Implement access restrictions in the external warehouse to employees of GFMS Liechti only and implement regular checks of compliance with fire regulations.

Conduct regular review on all materials stored on location 4000 based on the SAP report instead of the manual tracking report that only contains red zone materials. Each case should be accompanied by a clear comment on its reason for being in location 4000 and ideally also its physical location. The ageing and quantity of materials on 4000 should be kept to a minimum. Materials that are determined to be usable for Customer Service purposes should ideally be transferred to the dedicated plant code CH02 for Customer Service stock.

Ensure complete counting of all internal and external storage locations in 2024.
Implement a documented validation and approval process of the inventory differences identified in cycle counting prior to making the adjustments in SAP.

Introduce an effective invoice verification methodology. Particularly in the case of collective invoices, a standardized sampling procedure should be introduced to check the accuracy of invoiced costs before they are approved for payment.
Review the contract with Ischer to include specific written agreement on the rental charges. Request for approval for all real estate rentals and renewals in accordance with the DoA.

Review and identify gaps in availability of assembly work instructions for all machine types in the 2025 budget. Write up the missing assembly work instructions. Timely review and release work instructions that are still in draft status.

Review overdue cases shown in the maintenance overview file and order the required maintenance services via the PO process going forward.
Maintenance providers should be requested to always provide maintenance certificates and sticker labels after the maintenance service is performed. Such documents should be retained and affixed to the respective equipment.
Checklists should be defined and completed for maintenance tasks performed in-house.

Implement a suitable SAP dynamic skip-lot strategy for incoming inspections. A consistent sampling methodology such as the AQL-1 should be applied and followed by the Quality Engineer. Inspection records should clearly record the quantity of the sample taken. Define the list of materials requiring an incoming inspection plan in cooperation with R&D and ensure such materials have a suitable incoming inspection plan documented.

Reinforce the importance of ensuring NDAs are signed by leased employees within the GFPS R&D organization. Each global R&D portfolio head should include the NDA requirement in their onboarding checklist. Additionally, request that all existing leased employees sign NDAs.

Involve the Innovation Lead EMEA in the process of drafting R&D strategies. Clearly show the monetary contribution of business fields and existing and planned R&D projects into achieve-ment of the BU’s sales targets as part of the strategy’s roadmap.

Implement a standard visit report that should be completed as a mandatory document following each customer visit performed. All completed visit reports should be stored in a central file loca-tion accessible to the relevant teams. Consider using the JIRA - Confluence solution to docu-ment and store meeting minutes from customer visits for the whole BU Industries.

Establish a phase-out Division Directive and designate a process owner. Define the key steps, roles, and responsibilities involved in preparing for the product retirement. Consider developing a template for submission, which will be reviewed and approved by the division’s senior man-agement in line with the Delegation of Authority. The template should include the following re-quirements: total cost associated with the phase-out, rationale for phasing out the product and timeline.

Develop and agree on a standardized PLC template for documenting the full product lifecycle. Implement a centralized repository that will allow all relevant departments (e.g. R&D, product management, finance etc.) to have real-time access and make relevant updates. Maintain his-torical data and ensure that product insights are easily accessible to prevent knowledge loss and measure effectiveness of previously define strategies and actions. Periodically evaluate PLC and update the key parameters as necessary.

Develop and implement a formal project prioritization framework that evaluates projects based on key criteria such as strategic alignment, resource availability, business impact and timelines. En-sure this framework is consistently applied to guide decision-making and resource alloca-tion, enabling the organization to focus on the most critical initiatives.

Retrospectively, collect signatures for project Lotus’ M3 stage gate checklist. Establish a pro-cess of saving back-up calculations for the economic appraisals. Reinforce the requirement of an economic model review prior to each stage gate release. Reassess assumptions for manu-facturing costs and sales quantities for project Lotus. Use the functionality of budgeting by phase and analyze variations from the budget. Document the lessons learned from the variance analysis.

Define a time-based and monetary threshold for innovation and sustaining activities that will require the creation of a project order. Upon reaching a certain duration, project leads should either justify that the spend did not reach the monetary threshold or ask the Finance depart-ment to create a dedicated project number and report the actual costs under the project num-ber. Ensure that the budgeted and actual figures are timely reported in Smartsheet.

Determine hourly rates for professionals involved in R&D projects. Budget hours for R&D pro-jects per phase. Use applicable hourly rates to determine the OPEX budget for projects. Period-ically control the total budget and budget per phase to determine whether R&D projects (will) exceed their budget. Consider using the global project planning template.

Assess costs and benefits of implementation of a capacity planning tool for centrally shared re-sources in Technology Center and Global Tooling department. In case the tool implementation is beneficial, allocate resources based of the capacity planning to avoid any potential bottlenecks and project delays.

Implement a standardized process for recording and maintaining minutes for all steering com-mittee meetings. Assign a designated individual to take minutes and attendance ensuring that key decisions, action items and deadlines are clearly documented. Distribute the minutes to all relevant stakeholders in a timely manner.
Create a records retention schedule to ensure compliance with the CI 1026 and local require-ments. Arrange destruction of the hard-copy and digital records that exceed the retention peri-od.
Update key figures with actuals when available. Store supporting evidences centrally, and consider using the SIS upload function for that effect.

Respect the 4-eyes principle when posting manual journal entries. Review of manual journal en-tries should always be done by an independent employee.  Remove the user access right of posting manual journal entries from the Finance Controller.

Revise significant transactions and verify if the nature of these transactions matches the account where it is posted. In case existing accounts are not sufficient, activate additional group accounts.

Assign a responsible from the Purchasing team to conduct a monthly review of aged entries in the GR/IR account. Define actions for each entry older than 3 months. Follow up on the actions taken on a monthly basis.

Revise the credit limits for the existing customers considering the historical sales, potential future sales, history of payment behavior and external credit risk reports. Define credit limits that will allow to balance the credit risk and operational efficiency.

Assign different employees for preparation of payment proposal and recording invoices. Assign an employee that is not involved in payments and invoice recording processes to make chang-es to the vendor master data in SAP. For manually prepared payment proposals, verify that the bank account information in the payment list matches vendor master data in SAP. This control should be performed by an employee who is not involved in the preparation of the payment proposal or recording invoices. Consider automating the payment process in SAP to reduce manual intervention.
Apply DI 8772 for calculating savings. Alternatively, obtain exception for the existing method from the Division’s Procurement team.
Complete SAP setup, including definition of approval workflows according to the DoA. Replace current offline approval process with SAP workflow.

Clean the supplier master data, ensuring critical fields such as incoterms and payment terms are aligned with agreed terms with the suppliers. Eliminate the manual entry of purchase conditions in POs and rely solely on the supplier master data to ensure consistency and accuracy. Implement a periodic review of the master data to ensure accuracy.
Use the delivery date field in SAP in lieu of the Excel sheets and ensure that information is kept up-to-date. Use the information to support supplier evaluations and fine tuning of lead times.
Redesign the supplier assessment program locally, and align with Global Procurement, to ensure full coverage of key suppliers. Perform on-site audits for key suppliers.

Define and formalize a stock taking process. Align the process with DI 3437. Apply blind counting methodology. Consider implementing a WMS solution and SAP tools to strengthen both the counting process and day-to-day stock management.

Leverage the SAP MRP function to optimize production demands and raw material inventory. Ensure that the necessary inputs for the MRP system are accurate and up-to-date. This includes, maintaining detailed purchase and sales orders information, updating delivery dates, timely and accurately recording inventory movements (receipts, issues, returns). Regularly review the MRP inputs to ensure alignment with actual market conditions and probabilities of winning projects.
Update the current model to better capture demand variability using XYZ classification, as specified in DI 5896. Reconsider the current approach of static service levels and lead times.
Establish a sample-based peer review verification control of sales orders entered and created. In case the sample shows deviations, verify all sales orders raised by the Inside Sales Specialist.

Create a centralized tracker of the status and decision taken for orders that are older than 3 months and have no delivery clearance from the customer. In case of unsuccessful internal / ex-ternal follow up, escalate the issue to the Managing Director. Document any decisions taken for old orders with reserved stock.

Resolve the issues with the calculation of the production OTD and define OTD targets. Periodi-cally monitor and assess performance. Take actions if necessary. Establish a process of timely escalation of the sales with unfavorable conditions, ageing of debt from large key accounts and delayed production deliveries.
Establish a periodic review of the quarantined stock. Define time-bound decisions for the quaran-tined stock. Finalize the decision for the copper wire that is currently in quarantine.

Apply the blind count methodology when counting spare parts. Retain the count sheets for the audit purposes.   Develop and maintain a centralized file with stock count differences. Based on the monetary threshold conduct a root cause analysis for identified stock counting dif-ferences. Send the differences along with an adjustment’s proposal to the Finance department for review and accounting. Either expense the spare parts in line with the DI 9049 or obtain ap-proval from BU Controller and GFPS’s CFO to continue with the current methodology.

Document the root-cause analysis for the most important observations in the NCR. Define time-bound action plans and an employee responsible for the action plan completion and observation remediation. Create a tracking list of all internal quality audits with an overview of the actions nec-essary to be completed and remediated and store this list in a dedicated folder on a SharePoint.

Consult with the Division Controlling on the methodology for calculating cost of quality. Define a methodology for calculating cost of quality. Quantify replacements of products for customer claims. Calculate the warranty provision is line with the DI 3422 – Handling of Warranty Provision.
Ensure no unencrypted personal information is transferred outside the GF network

Review control descriptions, types, frequencies as well as responsible persons and adapt the documentation accordingly to ensure its appropriateness and completeness. Perform a self-reflecting ICS validation and ensure that the controls are assigned to the responsible users.

Use GFMS’ contract SharePoint to ensure all contracts and service fee agreements are collected, reviewed and stored in a single, accessible location. Implement a routine review process to verify that all contracts are complete, signed by both parties and up to date.

A formal procedure must be defined and implemented to ensure that applicable compliance training is consistently assigned to the right target groups. A responsible person should oversee enrollment and sales staff should be assigned and complete the required compliance training until the defined due date.
Reference and update the GPCs on the purchase order template.  Ensure the inclusion of the Code for Business Partners.

Investigate the reasons for the discrepancies and adjust SIS reporting for Q3 2024 to ensure correct reporting methodology, data collection processes and archiving methods in relation to the errors identified

Make sure, the invoices date of machine sales and its revenue recognition corresponds to the transfer of risk according to the applied Incoterm.
Issue deposit invoices in a timely manner to ensure a timely collection and avoid overdues. 
Collect the proof of delivery from the appointed freight forwarders and utilize it as a basis for issuing the invoice.

Implement a documented review of manual journal entries that complies with the 4-eyes principle. Ensure the preparation of these postings includes the sufficient underlying calculation documents (such as screenshots, pdfs, etc).
Define a dedicated responsible for the review and clearing of the GR/IR account. Define a regular review frequency. Conduct a review of all line items in the GR/IR account and release aged items.

Implementation of a formalized process using Coface for the credit worthiness process and the calculation of corresponding credit limits for customers who exceed the self-insured credit limit. Revise the credit limit determination process to align with the GFMS Credit Policy by assessing the specific credit risk for each customer over a 12-month period.

Implement a documented dunning process to ensure timely follow-up on overdue payments. If automation via SAP is not feasible, establish a standardized manual process with clear documentation and regular monitoring to track and escalate outstanding receivables.

Implement regular controls to ensure that orders exceeding credit limits are placed on hold. Additionally, regularly update credit exposure data to reflect current invoices and purchase orders, ensuring compliance with the GFMS Credit Policy.
Apply provision rates in line with divisional guidelines and obtain prior written approval from Division Controlling for deviations.
Consult your bank and implement an enforced preventive control in the bank payment system using a payment file upload function that is secured against manipulation.

Redistribute the tasks, ensuring that creating purchase orders and posting goods receipt are performed by different employees. Ensure the bank account reconciliation is performed by an employee not involved in the payment process.
Create the invoices for prepayments in SAP. Make sure that the prepayments received are reclassified to liabilities at the end of each period and reported accordingly in Cognos.
Include a reference to the general terms and conditions in the quotation template for services and consumables.

Relocate hazardous materials to their designated, properly labeled storage containers (separating the toxic and flammable ones) to ensure compliance with safety guidelines.
Secure the gas cylinders by chaining them to prevent tipping and ensure they are capped when not in use and restrict access to gas cylinders to authorized personnel only, and clearly label the storage area with safety instructions.
Remove the grill and gas cylinder from the warehouse and store them in a designated, safe area away from flammable materials.
Provide training to employees to reinforce the importance of complying with GF Safety Standards and regularly raise awareness among department managers to establish a control environment.

For this special type of purchase order used for intercompany fixed asset purchases, the Finance department should conduct a thorough review of the incoming invoice and hold off on posting it until the physical goods have arrived and acceptance is confirmed.

In consultation with the Corporate Legal team and local law firms, review and update GF purchasing terms and conditions to ensure compliance with GF standards and effective application to Australian regulations.
Configure the SAP system to generate the latest GF GTC automatically as the attachment of the purchase orders, or at least referred appropriately on the website to clarify legal and operational terms with suppliers.
Develop and implement a specific subcontracting or fabrication agreement with ACP to clearly define the obligations and rights associated with subcontracted services and full alignment with the divisional requirements on the fabrication process.

Establish a routine review process for the stocks with the stock type 904 to timely adjust the stocks.
Based on the defined process, provide specific training for Purchasing and Customer Service staff on the correct procedures for posting returns and inventory adjustments and correct the currently identified cases with the stock type 904.

It is recommended that a standardized process is implemented to systematically block and monitor non-conforming goods across all warehouses. This process should include automated inventory controls to flag and segregate suspect items immediately upon identification, both for physical stock and SAP records.

Firstly, it is recommended to apply the cycle count process to all storage locations. At the final count of the year, thorough checks should be conducted to ensure the entire stock has been counted, or that any uncounted stock falls within an acceptable tolerance. Secondly, the local stock take policy should be amended to incorporate the four-eye principle throughout the entire stock take process, particularly when reviewing and posting stock discrepancies. Finally, a systematic analysis process for stock adjustments should be established and consistently executed.

Apply the info-record as the data source of material lead time in the annual review of material master data.
Set up a regular evaluation process to assess the lead time accuracy based on actual material delivery data and correct major gaps whenever necessary. The BIPS report could be used directly to reduce manual work.

It is recommended that a standardized process is implemented to ensure that all discount authorizations are documented in a systematic and consistent manner. Before creating a sales order with an additional discount, the responsible customer service agent should check the approval and file the documents systematically.

Resume the monthly local management team meetings to ensure all department managers are aware of and can support on ongoing matters. Meeting minutes should be kept to document topics discussed, decisions taken as well as follow-up actions to be performed by the responsible employees and their deadlines.
Agree with Market Region management on whether 4DX should still be uploaded retrospectively for 2024 for GFMS India. Apply the methodology for 2025.
Seek support from the GFMS ICS Coordinator and ensure the specific ICS for GFMS India is validated and documented on the ICS SharePoint site.

Include introduction to Corporate Instructions in the standard onboarding process for new hires. Instruct employees to familiarize themselves with the policies that need to be closely followed during their daily work based on their role. For existing employees, make a one-time communication to highlight the same topic.
Use the GFMS Contract Management tool for its intended purposes to enhance the local contract management process.

Ensure all requirements of CI 1061 are complied with. Finance should validate each agent commission to be paid against the Agency Agreement. Any deviations should be escalated and resolved prior to payments. The agreement with the intermediaries should also include sections that address the operating model where they act as distributors.

Implement a process for evaluating customer credit limits and payment terms, e.g., based on credit rating of the customer, past turnover and payment behaviors. Review existing customer master data and update their credit limits and payment terms on an annual basis.
Further segregate the tasks of PO issuance and goods receipt posting within the Logistics department.

Conduct a full review on slow moving materials prioritizing on high value items. These should be promoted for sale to either customers or to the corresponding TU wherever possible. Once all viable options for sale are exhausted, follow the scrap approval procedures for remaining obsolete items. Going forward, a quarterly review of slow moving materials should be made to take timely actions.
Amend the standard terms & conditions featured on the template for quotations and order confirmations to eliminate contradictions.
Implement a routine management review of all aged open service tickets and service orders on a monthly basis to ensure timely resolution and billing where applicable.
Book aged unrecovered spare parts to the P&L to timely recognize the expenses. Ensure current cases are timely followed up to avoid having aged uncovered spare parts going forward.
Update the text regarding delivery charges on the quotation template for consumables to reflect current practice.
Ensure full compliance with CI 1061. Any exceptional handling must be pre-approved by the BU Head in consultation with the GF Corporate Compliance Officer.
Ensure a summary overview file is maintained in the 2024 reporting cycle to enhance reporting accuracy. Supporting documentation for reported figures should be retained.
Consult other GFPS companies with OSM operations on how to properly analyze high production variances and implement corrective measures to ensure the variances are kept at a reasonable level.
Consult the SAP Consultant on how to suitably clear the current unclosed POs. For future POs related to services, consider removing the goods receipt requirement.
Ensure figures reported on form KA31 reflect exactly the records in SAP including ageing structure. Retain SAP reports used to fill out KA31 as part of the month end closing documentation.

Place POs to vendors for components needed for OSM only based on confirmed customer or-ders. Consider implementing the SAP PO release strategy according to the applicable DoA to enable system control.
Follow the advice from the SAP consultant in adjusting the launch date setting of the third party produced materials in SAP to trigger correct stock provision calculation.

Finance department should send the slow moving stock report to the OSM Branch Manager on a monthly basis. OSM Tainan should review the slow moving materials and define and implement specific follow-up actions to improve the situation.

Strictly ensure the timely posting in SAP according to physical movements of goods. Delivery note should be generated at the time of physical goods shipping. Billing should be made according to the incoterm for correct timing of revenue recognition. MIGO should be booked within 24 hours of goods receipt.
Record the root causes and corrective actions for each order with margin anomaly.

Replace the simplified and non-standard sales contract with the GF standard contract to reduce the risk of potential contractual disputes.
Implement a rigorous presignature review and approval process for all machine sales to ensure a more robust contracting process.

Review the current customer master data and correct the identified customers with credit limit set up as zero or to T CNY 300, but in the meantime set up payment term as COD.
Deliver training to the concerned Finance employees on how to correctly set up credit limit in the iScala system for new customers with COD term agreed.
Review the supplier accout in iScala. Change the accout name to "no use" to avoid misunderstandings. Perform reconciliation each time a new account is created or updated.

Consider using automated tools such as the Quote Manager to streamline the quotation process, at least for the more standardized single-machine automation projects, to reduce reliance on error-prone manual spreadsheets and increase efficiency.
If not ready to be realized on Quote Manager, breakdown all relevant costs associated with the automation products and configure them as itemized cost options in the costing and introduce an independent review process for better transparency and accuracy for margin calculation and approval.

It is suggested that Service + Success team performs a training to the Sales Engineers that all offered terms must be sufficiently included in the quotation to accurately reflect the estimated gross margin.

For the Shanghai office, it is suggested to check the account list of the access control system. Ideally, the employee number should be recorded as the only employee identification number, or at least the account name must be able to be matched with the employee list. Generic accounts need to be avoided in general or should be changed to match the employee who entered the face/fingerprint recognition information.
For the Dongguan office, a regular review of the access control system account list is required. For those who have left the company, the account should be deleted in a timely manner. The key account should be limited to the necessary role. And the training on access control including information security awareness should be provided for employees.

Deliver training to the concerned employees on the new instruction and the correct data to be input in the iScala systems for machines installed in and after 2024.
Execute data cleaning on the existing machine information in the iScala system to correct the identified deficiencies based on the actual agreed warranty terms with the plants according to the new instruction.
Review the pricelist updated each month before sending out to Order Processing team. Review and improve the price updating process to avoid manual mistake.

Understand the category definition as explained in the SIS portal and follow data accuracy re-quirements by Corporate Sustainability for SIS reporting.
Correct the identified reporting deficiencies and report the 2024 SIS year-end data according to the SIS guidance.
Quality department as the key user for the SIS portal should sample-wise recheck the data ac-curacy when receiving data from HR, Finance and HSE departments, before inputting into the SIS system.

Collaborate with Corporate IT for possible solutions and further guidance to both meet the local operational needs and mitigate the potential information risks on the non-DXC devices, e.g. install mandatory firewall and anti-virus software on all the devices in use to enhance data pro-tection and mitigate the risk of malware attacks. 
For the 24 non-DXC managed PCs, reassess the needs and the compatibility issues with the local software.
Strengthen Wi-Fi access controls by adding approval controls to the "GF Employee" for only verified compliant devices to prevent unauthorized access and software downloading.
As a temporary data protection measure, disable the Wi-Fi connection to the external network for the identified PCs and only enable data transfer on the internal network.

Entrance to the quality storage area should be controlled so that only authorized personnel can enter.
Attach a quality label to materials stored in the quality storage area, indicating the reasons for storage, the date of storage, the person responsible, and the action plan.
Check the quality storage record regularly and ensure that the material is handled in a timely and proper manner.

Consult the GFMS Division SAP team to support proper material grouping and indication on the new SAP platform and perform the classification accordingly.
For the quality and purchasing controls relevant to the material grouping, consult the concerned departments before formalizing the groups into the SAP master data.
Deliver training to the concerned departments on the upcoming new material classification and the correct inputs in SAP.

Establish regular controls for reviewing lead times and safety stock levels for IMPC material to ensure full coverage within a fiscal year. This should include clear documentation outlining the data and evidence considered for such changes.
Clarify the methodology with SCM department of the Machine Assembly business for determin-ing lead times between material master data and purchasing info-records. If purchasing info records are the key for lead time maintenance, proper source list should also be reviewed on a regular basis to ensure the fixed vendor is configured for lead time implementation.
Improve the lead time accuracy in SAP and minimize the manual changes on order level.

Strengthen the on-site 5S and safety management controls on the shopfloor and warehouses to better ensure compliance and continuous improvement.
For non-conformities found during the site tour, define corresponding corrective actions with a feasible deadline, e.g. provide training to the concerned employees on where to store the haz-ardous materials, the importance of wearing helmet when working in the warehouse; clarify the roles and responsibilities of the expensed materials stored in the main warehouse, visualize the quantity and status of materials by using a proper labelling method; and segregate materials that are hazardous, still usable or to be scrapped.
Obtain approval before making any routing changes. Review the implemented routing change to identify unauthorized changes.

Define a standard timeframe for a usage decision and establish internal controls for periodic review of quality notifications to ensure that actions, such as supplier complaints, are taken and followed up on in a timely manner.

Develop the IMPC inbound inspection strategy and walk through the inspection lot setting in the SAP material master.
Maintain inbound inspection records.
Review the inbound inspection conditions for each IMPC material to avoid redundant or incor-rect inspection content.
Standardize the inbound inspection template to include all required criteria.

Review the estimated inspection processing time target pre-set in SAP and modify the unrea-sonable targets. Arrange the inbound inspection plan to ensure inspection efficiency and timely inspection.
Confirm UD in SAP timely after inspection.

Tracking list with complaint details and analysis should be established.
Complete tracking list and follow up on unresolved customer complaints in a timely manner. Develop feasible action plans for customer complaints and implement them accordingly.

Discuss and clarify with the GFMS Divisional and TU R&D teams to agree on the following ac-tions and methods based on priority.
Based on the agreed methods, budget the following R&D projects by phase to enable effective monitoring and controlling of individual project phase and early detection of R&D projects ex-ceeding the approved budgets.
For the internal resources contributed to the R&D projects, record the actual costs and compare them with the corresponding budgets in the monthly controlling process.

The management is required to develop a thorough and meticulously documented patching process that delineates the protocols for testing, implementing, and monitoring patches on the 85 workstations within the production environment. This documentation must encompass detailed instructions, timelines, assigned responsibilities, and best practices for patch management. Additionally, a Patching Register should be established to meticulously document all applied patches, noting the date, patch type, affected workstation, and any pertinent details. This register acts as a crucial resource for monitoring patching activities and facilitating auditing procedures.

Management shall improve asset management practices to ensure accurate tracking, utilization, and maintenance of IT devices within the organization. Addressing these issues will enhance operational efficiency, security, and compliance within GFPS's IT infrastructure.

Management shall update the network diagram to include more detailed information about network components, connections, and configurations. IP address ranges should be added to the network diagram to provide a comprehensive view of IP address allocations and assignments. A process should be established for regular review and updating of the network diagram to ensure its accuracy and relevance.

Management shall develop and implement a comprehensive IT security awareness training program for all employees. The training should cover topics such as phishing awareness, password security, data handling best practices, social engineering, and the importance of GDPR and KVKK compliance.

Corporate IT shall enhance communication with all local IT teams to provide training on the proper procedures for closing out incidents and updating statuses in the ticketing system. If needed, new, tailored training sessions will be developed to improve proficiency in utilizing ServiceNow, as well as in ticket extraction and monitoring processes.

Management must ensure that the status of resolved incidents is promptly updated in the Service Now ticketing system. This can be accomplished by establishing a process that mandates the local IT team to close out resolved incidents in the system immediately after resolving the issue. Utilizing training materials provided by Corporate IT will help ensure that team members are well-versed in the correct procedures for closing out incidents and updating statuses in the ticketing system. If necessary, specific training on the utilization of Service Now should be requested to further support team proficiency.

Management shall store the backup server in a distinct location, positioned at a sufficient distance from the primary servers. By doing so, this practice guarantees that in the event of a disaster affecting the primary server room, the backups will remain secure and easily accessible for recovery operations.
Corporate IT shall finalize the procurement of the necessary hardware to facilitate the relocation of the backup server to the secondary server room.

Management shall promptly take actions to enforce password changes, implement robust password practices in alignment with the GF possword policy, and proactively address the security vulnerabilities associated with default passwords across all evaluated devices.

Management shall develop a comprehensive maintenance schedule outlining specific tasks to be performed on a monthly, quarterly, and annual basis. These tasks should be  clearly defined, assigned to responsible individuals, and tracked systematically. If any maintenance tasks have been overlooked or delayed, deficiencies should be taken immediate addressed. This may involve rescheduling tasks, conducting overdue maintenance, or addressing any potential issues identified during inspections.

Management shall deactivate any unauthorized Wi-Fi networks and ensure that employees only use the networks provided by Corporate GF. Furthermore, it is essential to prioritize employee awareness and education regarding the dangers of connecting to non-standard Wi-Fi networks to encourage responsible network usage within the corporate setting. A business case involving Division IT and Corporate IT to move the ERP system into the corporate network should also be made

Management shall improve AD management practices and maintain data accuracy. It is advised to correct any incomplete or missing information in user accounts, such as department, job title, description, city, and office fields, to ensure accurate user profiles are maintained.

Management shall upgrade servers running outdated and unsupported operating systems such as Windows Server 2012, and Mware vCenter 6.2.0 to supported and secure versions or decommission them. Critical and important security updates for Windows Servers, Microsoft .Net, and SQL Server should be applied immediately to mitigate known vulnerabilities and reduce the risk of exploitation. It should be ensured  non-standard browser are unistalled from all servers. Standardization on the single antivirus solution approved by the corporate IT security team should be analysed in details to ensure consistent protection across the corporate network.

Management shall restrict access to sensitive folders and files to only authorized personnel who require such access to perform their job duties or delete the unrestricted folders. A role-based access controls (RBAC) should be enforced on the local infrastructure to ensure that users only have access to the information necessary for their roles.
Management shall implement a scheduled maintenance plan to ensure that CCTV cameras, recording devices, and other components are regularly inspected, cleaned, and secured.
Management shall address any relevant misconfigurations with the vendor, initiating the necessary corrective actions and ensuring confirmation upon the successful resolution.
The Head of Sales BU China should review the current version of BU DoA in comparison with GFCS division and reach an official alignment in terms of the conflicts between the two versions.

Book a provision for the ongoing litigation case. Going forward, a transparent communication channel should be established for all events that might require a provision to be made on the financial statement.
Re-emphasize the importance of maintaining effective first-aid kits throughout the company. Increase attention on first-aid kits during upcoming safety walks performed by the management team.
Consider accruing for the missing invoices on a lumpsum basis at each month end based on the balance of the GR/IR account. Ensure liabilities are fully captured on the balance sheet.
Consider developing second sourcing for new sand and bentonite or implement an alternative solution that mitigate the related risks.

Clarify and resolve the aged open POs. It is recommended that the Purchasing Manager conduct a monthly monitoring of all open POs and follow-up with the respective Buyer for aged items. This should mitigate the risk that some open POs are no longer being followed up due to personnel changes.

If possible, external maintenance should also be configured in the SAP PM module, so that the maintenance order generated by SAP can serve as a central and reliable reminder of due maintenance work. If the need for maintenance is based on hours of operation rather than on a regular basis, make sure there is a person responsible for ordering and recording outside maintenance for the equipment.

It is recommended that a repair report be completed for each repair job performed. This report should include the following details: the repaired machine, failure details, work performed, and the usage of spare parts. It should also be signed and dated by an independent personnel in addition to the R&M engineer who performed the repair.

Conduct a thorough review of the affected materials and their statuses in SAP and rectify any incorrect entries or configurations. It is important to ensure that all staff are fully aware of the requirement to timely release the spare parts into unrestricted status.

Ensure that all sales projects are submitted to the appropriate level of management for approval. It is also recommended that approval documentation be systematically filed with each sales project so that the lack of approval can be identified as soon as possible.
Review the controls in the ICS and update the description, applicability, responsible persons and evidence of testing, etc. and update the ICS details in the upcoming 2025 review process.

Improve the data quality in the Excel overview for reconciliation purposes and ensure a proper audit trail by maintaining all the supporting documents such as invoices on the local share drive for retrospective review and analysis.
Implement four-eyes principle controls in reporting the SIS figures to eliminate potential manual errors and improve traceability of the data sources.

For the monthly production variance analysis, all significant variances, regardless of negative or positive, should be highlighted and reviewed with actions defined. For cycle time review, if the review conclusion is that standard is not reasonable, the standard should be updated accordingly to reflect the real situation.
Anticipated EOP should not be used as the basis to exclude certain products from the impending loss provision calculation, especially when there is call-off quantity known to the company.

Approval for entertainment expenses should be obtained in advance of treatment activities. If the situation limits access to the application platform, documented pre-approval should be obtained, such as via email or Teams. Categorize the expense to the correct account.

For POs with past due delivery dates, export all types of expired POs and follow up. If a confirmation of delivery date postponement is agreed, change in SAP in time. Review the PO to avoid manual data entry errors. Review the KPI calculation formula to ensure that the KPI can reveal the actual situation of management.

Strengthen the on-site 5S and safety management controls on the shopfloor and warehouses to better ensure compliance and continuous improvement.
Provide continuous training to employees to enhance employees' safety awareness

For the two KPIs that are currently not calculated according to the defined evaluation method, the production team should review and decide which method should be used for future reference. For the production plan completion rate, the calculation method should continue to follow the defined method, i.e. actual production yield/planned yield.

For production machine, send the PM schedule to the production planner in time to arrange the maintenance period. Perform PM according to the scheduled plan. Review and change the PM frequency in SAP if it does not fit the current situation. For facility equipment, check the completion status of the PM plan and timely confirm the completion in SAP.

When reconciling with subcontractors, attention should be paid to the status of stocks, especially the non-conforming goods. To ensure timely return of these items to GF for further processing, a review of the balance should be conducted at the end of each month.
Set up a regular evaluation process to assess the lead time accuracy based on actual material delivery data and correct major gaps whenever necessary.

The process to regular review actual incurred shipping cost and compare it with quoted price should be resumed to include all new products. In case of actual costs exceeding the quoted price, especially when special transportation is involved, root cause should be analyzed and actions should be defined.
Nominate substitutes for every function and instruct and train them accordingly. Ensure that key knowledge is not kept isolated with one employee only.

Update and adapt the local DoA to current business responsibilities. Communicate the updated version to local employees. Ensure approvals for all business transactions are obtained accord-ing to the updated local DoA.

Ensure completeness and timely renewal of the intermediaries assessment (Annex 1) using the latest version of Annex 1 of CI 1061. Put a focus on the collection of a signed version of Compliance. If intermediaries refuse to sign the GF Compliance Agreement, assess their code of conduct and consult the Head of Corporate Compliance for a discussion of further proceedings. Obtain an approval from Corporate Compliance for the whole BU EMEA that the risk-based approach can be applied. Ensure that agents are considered as intermediaries as well since they pose an elevated risk.

Review and adjust the text of the General Purchase Conditions of the various languages and webpages and include a reference to the Code for Business Partners (where currently missing) to ensure adequate suppliers' acknowledgement of GFPS GPCs and GF’s Code for Business Partners.

Establish processes to systematically identify and collect data necessary to fulfill SIS reporting requirements. Develop a comprehensive Excel overview as well as a corresponding folder structure on the local share drive to consolidate reporting relevant data. Ensure that the audit trail from reported KPIs to supporting evidences (i.e. invoices) is in place.
Implement a documented review of manual journal entries that complies with the 4-eyes principle.
Consider claiming back the VAT from the tax authorities on the uncollectable trade receivables related to customer bankruptcy cases.

Consult Coporate Legal and Corporate IT and based on their input assess revoking or limiting system rights previously granted to the two external sales agents as well as retrieving GF’s hardware. Exclude the sales agents from the GFPS Germany homepage, as these are not GF employees. Compare the contracts established with these sales agents against the current Corporate Legal contract template. Investigate the HR related consequences that might result from the current set-up.
Implement a contract overview with summary of key specifics of each contract such as purpose, contract owner, end date, renewal / termination notice period etc.
Review current enrolment records of employees against the target group requirements published by Corporate Compliance for any missed enrolments. Enroll the employees for missed e-learnings.

Ensure the correct GL account is used going forward for booking scrap sales income. Reclassify current year transactions to the correct GL. 

Reclassify the prepaid expenses and deposits to the correct categories on KA8 based on the underlying nature of the transactions.

Ensure stock provision is adjusted each month according to the SAP calculation without exception. Ensure the provision for bonus and profit sharing is adjusted and booked according to the underlying calculation without discrepancy.

Collaborate and agree with Division and BU Controlling on the assumptions and the parameters to calculate the provision for employee service awards based on the local Award and Recognition Policy and the Corporate Accounting and Reporting Guidance on Employee Benefit. Book the calculated provision on the balance sheet accordingly.

Standard work will be implemented for payment and incoterms. A periodic review will be scheduled at a cadence appropriate to the level of spend. A consolidation of payment terms will be issued. All direct material suppliers will have defined incoterms. These topics will be completed in February 21 2025
Update the system parameters so that the payment proposal contains accurate payment dates rather than the payment run date. Use the scheduling function in ebanking.
Establish a narrower scope for the usage of P-Cards, ensuring it's usage is as exceptional as possible. Ensure the equivalent of 3-way match is performed for those purchases, including adequate SOD.
Ensure delivery dates in SAP are kept as accurate as possible at a PO line level.
Consider using return POs for credit note tracking. Ensure credit notes are obtained and posted against the corresponding line in the PO.

Update the current stock count procedure to ensure that adequate item count coverage is achieved. Ensure materials kept off-site are also counted by GF staff. Track the cumulative stock differences throughout the year.
Remind and re-emphasize with all employees the requirements of the GF Health & Safety standards. Employees must wear the required protective equipment.
For materials that have different combinations of components, consider adding additional versions of the BOM to reflect those differences.

Cooperate with the HR SSC team and the GF US lawyer on how to rectify missed tax treatments on gift cards awarded to employees in prior years. Consider switching to awards via payroll going forward instead of gift cards.

Management shall create a detailed document outlining all Technical and Organizational Measures (TOMs) tailored to the organization's specific operations and risks. All implemented TOMs should be documented properly, anda regular review process should be established  to assess their effectiveness and make necessary updates. An up-to-date inventory should be maintained of all personal data processed in thew local applications, including its storage locations and processing activities, to enhance transparency and accountability.

Management shall conduct a comprehensive assessment to identify and document all information assets, including production workstations and devices. This should include hardware, software, and any other relevant resources. All production workstations should be properly registered in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) within ServiceNow and other devices recorded in a dedicated database. This will enhance visibility and management of all assets. A schedule should be esablished for regular reviews and updates of the asset inventory to ensure it remains accurate and reflects any changes in the organization’s asset landscape or risk environment.

Management shall implement the following measures:
1. Enforce Personal Protective Equipment Compliance:
-implement mandatory training sessions on the importance and correct use of personal protective equipment (PPE).
-conduct regular inspections to ensure all employees are wearing required PPE, including safety helmets.
-establish a disciplinary action policy for non-compliance to reinforce the importance of safety protocols.
2. Secure High Voltage Electrical Panel:
-Install locking mechanisms on the high voltage electrical panel to prevent unauthorized access.
-Conduct a thorough inspection of electrical systems to ensure compliance with safety standards and address any additional hazards.
3. Enhance Surveillance and Security at Scornicesti Plan:
-Erect physical barriers or fencing to restrict access to abandoned and decaying buildings.
-Install security cameras in unmonitored areas, including the perimeter and storage areas, to monitor activities and ensure safety compliance.

Management shall restore the non-functioning video cameras in order to provide continuous surveillance and monitoring of security zones, allowing security personnel to observe and respond to any suspicious activity in real time. This enables a proactive approach to security, helping to prevent incidents before they occur. Security personnel should be trained to promptly report any faults.

Management shall enforce the usage of the GF ticketing system to log and track all IT requests received by the local IT coordinator. A standardized process should be created for provisioning and revoking user access rights across the quality sistem.
Training to employees should be provided on the importance to utilize tools or systems that enable online tracking and monitoring of incidents, requests and approval processes.
Awareness should be raised about the risks associated with offline approval workflows and the benefits of compliance with ISO/IEC 27001 Control A.9.2.

Management shall create a detailed DRP that outlines procedures for responding to IT disruptions, declaring disasters, and restoring IT services. Detailed recovery procedures should be documented with step-by-step instructions for restoring IT resources and recovering data. Regular testing and validation of recovery procedures should be conducted to ensure their effectiveness and reliability in real-world scenarios.

Management shall review and modify user permissions to ensure that operators do not have administrative access. Implement a system of role-based access control to limit privileges to only what is necessary for their job functions.
The wired network cards should be disabled on all workstations that do not require direct network access. The corporate Wi-Fi network should not be not visible to unauthorized devices. This may include hiding the SSID and implementing stronger authentication measures. A thorough review of installed software should be conducted and uninstall any unnecessary applications, particularly those that pose security risks, such as gaming software and non-standard communication tools.

Management shall isolate the production networks from the corporate network. This can be achieved by implementing appropriate network segmentation, access controls, and security measures to safeguard the integrity and confidentiality of the production systems and data

Management shall install the important security updates for the Microsoft OLE DB Driver and SQL Server to close the identified vulnerabilities. A thorough scan  should be performed of all servers and systems to identify any additional vulnerabilities that may exist. Settings for OS components, applications, and macro settings should be reviewed to eliminate any insecure configurations.

Management shall immediately change all default passwords on devices and systems to strong, unique passwords. Ensure that passwords meet complexity requirements of GF password policy. Vendors activities should be coordinated to ensure that security best practices are followed in the deployment and configuration of their systems.

Management shall establish network access control measures to enforce security policies and verify the integrity and compliance of devices before permitting them access to the corporate network. Furthermore, implementing a device registration procedure is advised, requiring unknown devices to be registered and approved before connecting to the corporate network. This process may entail deploying a security certificate or utilizing device-specific credentials for authentication purposes.

Management shall review and revise access permissions to ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive database backups and application folder. Proper access controls should be enforced, implementing strong authentication mechanisms, regularly monitoring and access to backup and application files, and encrypting sensitive data to maintain its confidentiality and integrity.

Management shall ensure that the access control systems in the Pitesti server rooms and laboratories are fully operational and capable of generating log files for all access event. The access control systems should be configured to produce comprehensive logs that record user entries, exits, and any exceptions. These logs should be regularly reviewed to identify any anomalies or unauthorized access attempts. An effective access control should be implemented at the entrance to the Scornicesti laboratory to prevent unauthorized entry and enhance monitoring. All doors to sensitive areas should be maintained in a locked position when not in use. Only authorized personnel should have access to keys or access system rights.

Management shall update the application password settings to require a minimum of ten characters, using a mix of upper and lower case letters, numbers, and special characters. A second layer of authentication should be introducted, such as SMS codes, authentication apps, or hardware tokens, to enhance security during user login. The vendor should be notified about the application vulnerabilities and their resolution should be requested.

Collaborate with Chinaust Legal to draft one AoA amendment to better align with the new Company Law in China, and request to release the share certificates once the new AoA is finalized and registered with the State Administration for Industry and Commerce (AIC).
Implement a formal documentation process for bank reconciliations and establish an independent review mechanism.
Correct the HBI balances in the Cognos KBHB reports for both legal entities in the next reporting period to accurately reflect the actual HBI balances and rectify the overstatements.

Management shall prioritize the completion of the IT Governance Policy by incorporating feedback and necessary corrections. Alignment shuld be ensured with GF Corporate standards and regulatory requirements. Formal approval should be seeked from the relevant stakeholders and leadership to ensure the policy is recognized and endorsed by the organization. Once approved, the finalized policy should be distributed to all employees, contractors, consultants, and other personnel within BFS.

Management shall conduct a thorough review and debugging of the cookie banner to ensure it functions properly and prevents the storage of cookies until explicit consent is obtained. The banner should clearly informs users about all the cookies used, their purposes, and the implications of their consent.
An efficient mechanism that requires users to actively opt-in to non-essential cookies (e.g., marketing and statistical cookies) should be activated before any data collection occurs. A comprehensive analysis of uncategorized cookies used on the website should be performed to ensure proper categorization for all the cookies.

Management shall conduct a thorough review of all outdated guidelines to identify necessary updates and revisions. It should be ensured that they align with current best practices and regulatory requirements. The completion and publication of the draft guidelines on Secure Software Development and Data Backup should be prioritized. They should undergo proper review and approval processes. A regular review schedule for all policies and guidelines should be established to ensure they remain current and effective over time. This could include annual or biannual reviews. A communication plan should be developed to inform all employees about the updated policies and guidelines, emphasizing the importance of compliance and the potential risks of relying on outdated information.

Management shall establish a clear timeline and prioritize the integration of all office computers and workstations into the existing CMDB or into ServiceNow to ensure comprehensive asset tracking. A thorough inventory of all IT assets, including laptops, desktops, and production workstations, should be performed to ensure that all devices are accounted for and included in the CMDB. DXC activities should be monitored to ensure that the new computers are not only replaced but also properly registered in the CMDB upon deployment.

Management shall review and revise the existing DRP for the Oracle ERP to reflect current business processes, technologies, and recovery strategies. It should be ensured that it is kept up to date regularly. The DRP should be expanded to encompass all business-critical applications hosted in the Helsinki data center. Recovery plans should be developed and documented tailored to each application’s specific needs. The business application recovery requirements should be clearly articulated for all critical applications, including recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) to guide recovery efforts. Regular testing of the DRP should be scheduled and performed to validate recovery procedures and ensure that all team members are familiar with their roles during a disaster. Results should be documented and necessary adjustments made based on findings.

Corporate IT shall consider adopting integrated security solutions that provide a holistic view of the security environment. This can help streamline alert management and improve the overall efficiency of the SOC. Protocols should be developed for regular communication and collaboration between the BFS SOC and the corporate GF SOC. This can include joint training sessions, shared threat intelligence, and coordinated response strategies to create a unified security posture. A mechanism should be established to integrate BFS network intrusion detection alerts into the SOC monitoring workflows. This will ensure that all potential threats are monitored and assessed consistently.

Management shall finalize the process to create a full trust relationship between the BFS and GF Corporate AD domains. This will allow users in one domain to authenticate and access resources in the other without the need for separate credentials. SSO should be implemented across the trusted domains. This will facilitate seamless access to shared resources and improve user productivity by eliminating the need for repeated credential entry. A centralized process should be enabled for managing user accounts across GF domains. This could involve using a federated identity management solution or automated provisioning tools that synchronize user identities and permissions.

Management shall establish a standardized process for documenting all firewall rules, including detailed descriptions, reference numbers, and the rationale behind each rule. This should be enforced for all new rules and retroactively applied to existing ones. Existing rules should be evaluated for redundancy and conflicts, and consolidate them where possible. Reducing the number of rules can simplify management and improve performance.

Management shall deploy a comprehensive security solution, to monitor the OT network continuously. It should be ensured that the solution can detect anomalies, unauthorized access, and potential threats in real time. On-site sensors should be installed to monitor network traffic, detect unexpected activity, and provide insights into device behavior within the OT network. These sensors should be integrated with the security monitoring solution for enhanced visibility.

Management shall establish a formal documentation of the vulnerability management process, including procedures for identification, assessment, prioritization, and remediation of vulnerabilities. A systematic approach should be adopted to prioritize vulnerabilities based on their severity and potential impact, utilizing frameworks like CVSS to ensure that high-risk vulnerabilities are addressed promptly. Scan results from Nessus and Microsoft Defender should be periodically exported and analyzed to identify trends, track remediation progress, and inform decision-making.

Management shall immediately apply critical and important security updates for Windows Server, Oracle Linux, Microsoft SQL Server, and other affected software to eliminate known vulnerabilities. Obsolete and unsupported software, such as Microsoft Access, Microsoft Office, Silverlight, and Oracle Java JRE, should be replaced with supported versions to ensure ongoing security updates and support. Insecure configurations across all servers should be reviewed and rectified, ensuring compliance with security best practices and vendor recommendations.

Management shall ensure that Microsoft Defender is activated on all devices, particularly the 35 where it is currently disabled, to provide essential real-time protection against malware. A thorough analysis of all devices should performed, particularly of the five computers with active malware. The identified threats on the infected computers should be removed or the devices deactivated.

Management shall ensure that the training program is easily accessible to all employees, taking into account different learning styles and preferences. Regular monitoring of training completion rates should be implemented by department and employee category to allow for early identification of groups with lower compliance rates and targeted intervention. The significance of data protection and compliance with GDPR should be communicated regularly to all employees. This can help foster a culture of security and awareness within the organization.

It should be re-emphasized to all employees that participant company and names must be specified on all expense claims. The approving line managers should not approve the claim until such details are furnished.

The local management team should further raise awareness and highlight the compliance risks when dealing with intermediaries across the company, especially amongst the sales teams. Agents should be treated with extra care as they carry even higher compliance risks than other types of intermediaries. The requirements of CI 1061 should be carefully studied and diligently followed.
Ensure recipient names and/or company names are always entered in the ERP, in addition to the address of the ship-to party in order to ensure an effective trade compliance screening process.
Consult Corporate Legal on how best to roll out the GF Code for Business Partners. This could mean updating the existing PO template with the suitable reference.

Review all reported figures in 2024 for potential typing errors and contact Corporate Sustainability to make the corrections wherever possible. Going forward, it is recommended to directly attach supporting documents (summary spreadsheets and underlying invoices) against each reported figure in SIS instead of keeping various tracking files outside SIS. This should enhance traceability of the figures reported. Going forward, in order to avoid typing errors, assign a second employee to review the figures entered following the four-eyes principle.
For accident reporting, a process should be established to verify and record the duration of hospitalization related to each case to properly classify the severity of the accident according to the definition published in SIS.

Re-enforce safety compliance with regards to hazardous materials and waste storages by in-creasing safety self-audits frequency at the respective areas.
Waste recycling companies should be contacted timely for waste collection in order to avoid accumulation of excess wastes on the plant premises.

Review the raw material list and remove both inactive suppliers and discontinued raw materials. Ensure that for active raw materials, a narrower portfolio of suppliers is maintained. Work with R&D, material planning and quality to ensure the specifics of the materials that remain are reflected in BOMs, routings, quality plans and material planning assumptions.
Review the ABC calculation so as to adequately capture consumption value. Update make-to-stock and make-to-order status for reclassified items. Adjust minimum stock levels.
Create new BOM versions that accurately reflect the different raw materials available for use. Update the routings with settings matching those materials.

Isolate the expired stock and finished goods made from expired batches. Review what can be done with the product together with R&D. Ensure that materials with expiration date are tracked and monitored accordingly, so that they are used before expiration.

Reverse / release the unnecessary slow-moving provision. Develop and maintain a stock provision reconciliation file that consolidates all stock adjustment-related entries to ensure transparency and accuracy.
Clarify responsibility for recording stock value adjustments related to TEANV+ and v-price by defining accountable legal entities. Adjust DI 3440 in case an exception is needed.
Monthly reconcile postings in SAP to the totals in calculations prepared for margin push-back.
Reinforce the importance of prompt reconciliation and resolution of open items. Establish a clear timeframe for clearing outstanding positions.
Align approval limits in xFlow in accordance with the DoA.

In collaboration with Head of BU EMEA, consider implementing a structured strategic planning process to assess and prioritize potential new international markets. Provide clear guidelines for market prioritization and resource allocation.
Ensure traceable approval in line with the local instruction and Delegation of Authorities and investigate an SAP based approval process for free deliveries.

Recommendation
Correct the formula in the Excel calculation template to ensure the 10% deduction is applied to the gross HB II receivables balance. Ensure the adjustment is reflected in the next reporting period.

Revise the calculation of the stock value adjustments to align it with the methodology outlined in DI 3440. Consider integrating the Israeli branch’s inventory into GFPS International’s SAP system to standardize calculations and reduce manual error risk.
Enroll the employees of the KSA’s representation office into relevant compliance trainings.

Finalize CI 1061 requirements using the risk-based approach defined for BU EMEA. Ask intermediaries to sign Annex 2 or obtain and assess the intermediary’s code of conduct and approval from the Managing Director. Sign a distributorship agreement in line with GF’s template. In case the intermediary refuses to sign the agreement, contact GF's Head of Corporate Compliance to assess the possibility to use general terms and conditions instead.

Establish a structured onboarding and integration process to ensure that newly integrated enti-ties, such as GFBFS Sweden, are formally introduced to GF’s Governance Framework, including all relevant Corporate Instructions (CIs). Conduct an assessment between the GF’s governance framework and the current framework of GFBFS. Identify and address any discrepancies or po-tential contradictions.
Ensure internal controls are clearly assigned to local employees and provide targeted training to enhance awareness, accountability, and effective execution.

Define responsibility of one person to control the figures currently reported by various stakeholders. Analyze the figures in comparison to the prior reporting period and understand the variations. Define a process for correcting SIS numbers that were erroneously reported.

As part of the mid-year and year-end financial reporting, define a process of identifying ship-ments to customers with DAP or DDU incoterms. Based on the confirmation from the freight for-warders, assess sales transactions for which revenue recognition should be deferred.

Ensure that the slow-moving provision is recorded in accordance with the "UPO Slowmower Analysis" (BI report). Exclude only those inventory items explicitly permitted by the Uponor Fi-nance Manual (e.g. new items) . Establish an approval workflow for items excluded from the slow-moving provision. Clarify the definitions of "write-off" and "write-down" in the Uponor Fi-nance Manual to enhance consistency in slow-moving inventory provisions. Incorporate input purchase price fluctuations and finished goods market price adjustments into the "UPO Slowmower Analysis" (Bi report) calculation methodology to align with IAS 2 and Swiss GAAP FER requirements. Update the Uponor Finance Manual to reflect these necessary adjustments and ensure alignment with IFRS and Swiss GAAR FER principles.

Use the template for long-service award published on Corporate Controlling’s page (We@GF) to calculate the provision for employee service awards annual as of 30 September each year. Rec-ord the calculated provision value in the financial statements accordingly.

Establish and implement a formalized periodic asset count procedure to ensure regular verification of fixed assets. Conduct a full physical asset count before the 2025 year-end closing. Ensure all fixed assets are properly tagged and reconciled with the records in Oracle. Analyze discrepancies between the physical count and the fixed asset register, investigate the causes, and take corrective actions.

Establish a streamlined process to ensure that all new lease agreements are promptly communicated to the Controlling department and the FSC upon initiation. Record all lease contracts in the "Fatman" system during the same month they commence to comply with IFRS 16 and prevent retrospective data entry issues. Use actual signed contracts as references in the "Fatman" system, rather than preliminary offers, to ensure transparency and traceability. Identify and reconcile all contracts for the currently recorded leases in "Fatman" and verify that the conditions in the contracts correspond to the conditions in "Fatman".
